mergers.merger_description:
Estia Investments Pty. Ltd. (**Estia**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the business assets of three residential aged care homes from Norsan Pty Ltd (**Norsan**), being Chirnside Views Residential Aged Care, Park Lane Croydon Residential Aged Care and Willowbrae - Melton Residential Aged Care (the **Acquisition**).

Estia is a registered provider of residential care services under the *Aged Care Act 2024 (Cth)* (**ACA**). Estia and its wholly owned subsidiaries currently operate over 90 residential aged care homes across Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland, and South Australia.

Norsan is a small family-owned supplier of three residential aged care services operating in certain parts of metropolitan Melbourne and outer Melbourne suburbs. Norsan is also a registered provider of residential care services under the ACA.

Both Estia and Norsan are providers of residential aged care services in certain areas within, and in the outer suburbs of metropolitan Melbourne.

determination_table_content.details: Estia Investments Pty. Ltd. (Estia) proposes to acquire substantially all of the business assets of three residential aged care homes from Norsan Pty Ltd (Norsan), being Chirnside Views Residential Aged Care (Chirnside Views), Park Lane Croydon Residential Aged Care (Park Lane Croydon) and Willowbrae - Melton Residential Aged Care (Willowbrae – Melton) (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Estia is a registered provider of residential care services under the Aged Care Act 2024 (Cth) (ACA). Estia and its wholly owned subsidiaries currently operate over 90 residential aged care homes across Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland, and South Australia.

Norsan is a small family-owned supplier of three residential aged care services operating in certain parts of metropolitan Melbourne and outer Melbourne suburbs. Norsan is also a registered provider of residential care services under the ACA.
Estia and Norsan overlap in the supply of residential aged care facilities in Melbourne.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form and information from third partes and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• The aggregation arising from the Acquisition, and taking into account previous acquisitions by Estia within the same local area as the Norsan facilities in the last three years, is not significant at a local or regional level (nor at the national level).

• Estia will likely continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of residential aged care facilities in the local areas surrounding each of Chirnside Views, Park Lane Croydon and Willowbrae – Melton.

mergers.merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (**Henkel**) proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of ATP Adhesives Systems Group GmbH (together with subsidiaries, **ATP**) from ATP Adhesives Holdings LP, which pre-transaction is controlled by affiliates of Arsenal Capital Partners.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftsmen. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. In Australia, Henkel supplies tapes and labels (**T&L**) adhesives and non-water-based pressure-sensitive adhesive (**PSA**) tapes.

ATP is a private company headquartered in Wollerau, Switzerland. ATP manufactures PSA tapes for various end uses. In Australia, ATP supplies only water-based PSA tapes.

events.title: Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Questionnaire
events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-40005/Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Questionnaire.pdf

events.title: Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (Henkel) proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of ATP Adhesives Systems Group GmbH (together with subsidiaries, ATP) from ATP Adhesives Holdings LP, which pre-transaction is controlled by affiliates of Arsenal Capital Partners.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftspeople. In Australia, Henkel supplies tapes and labels (T&L) adhesives and non-water-based pressure-sensitive adhesive (PSA) tapes. PSA tapes are tapes that form an adhesive bond upon the application of pressure. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt.

ATP is a private company headquartered in Wollerau, Switzerland.

ATP manufactures PSA tapes for various end uses. In Australia, ATP supplies only water-based PSA tapes to industrial customers.

Both Henkel and ATP primarily supply specialty PSA tapes as opposed to more general-use PSA tapes such as masking tape or packing tape. Speciality PSA tapes generally have specific use-cases in certain industries such as automotive, medical, electronics and construction. An example of a use-case in the automotive industry is to attach interior components like carpet to a vehicle.

determination_table_content.item: Overlap and relationship between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
The parties overlap in the supply of PSA tapes globally, including in Australia. T&L adhesives are a key input into PSA tapes.

There are narrower segments within the supply of PSA tapes, including non-water-based PSA tapes and water-based PSA tapes.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the ACCC undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, publicly available information, and feedback from market participants.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• The aggregation in the supply of PSA tapes resulting from the Acquisition appears low, and the merged entity would likely continue to be constrained by alternative suppliers of PSA tapes in Australia post-acquisition.

• The merged entity would be unlikely to have the ability and incentive to foreclose rivals’ access to inputs (in particular T&L adhesives) given there are strong alternative suppliers of T&L adhesives and PSA tapes in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (**Intrepid Travel**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (**Wild Bush Luxury**), together with the transfer of relevant intellectual property rights and employees. Wild Bush Luxury is currently held by Experience Co Limited.

Intrepid Group Pty Ltd (**IG**) is a global, purpose-led experiential travel company, specialising in small group tours and travel experiences. IG has 31 offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group adventures in 118 countries. Intrepid Travel is a subsidiary of IG.

Wild Bush Luxury is a boutique Australian tourism operator that manages and operates the ‘The Maria Island Walk’ (in Tasmania), ‘Arkaba Walk’ (in South Australia), ‘Arkaba Homestead’ (in South Australia), and ‘Bamurru Plains’ (in the Northern Territory).

determination_table_content.details: Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (Intrepid Travel) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (Wild Bush Luxury) (together, the Parties) together with the transfer of relevant intellectual property rights and employees (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Intrepid Travel, is a subsidiary of Intrepid Group Pty Ltd (Intrepid Group). Intrepid Group is a global, purpose-led experiential travel company specialising in small group tours and travel experiences. Intrepid Group has 31 offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group adventures in 118 countries.

The target, Wild Bush Luxury, is a boutique Australian tourism operator that manages and operates the ‘The Maria Island Walk’ (in Tasmania), the ‘Arkaba Walk’ and ‘Arkaba Homestead’ (in South Australia), and ‘Bamurru Plains’ (in the Northern Territory). Wild Bush Luxury is currently held by Experience Co Limited.

Intrepid Travel and Wild Bush Luxury overlap in the supply of guided walking experiences and/or accommodation in:

• the Flinders Ranges in South Australia

• the Top End in the Northern Territory, and

• Maria Island in Tasmania.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, information from third parties, and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• The Parties are not particularly close competitors in the supply of guided walking experiences and/or accommodation in the areas of overlap and/or surrounding geographic areas. Intrepid Travel only offers a single-day guided walk in Maria Island as part of a longer multi-stop tour in Tasmania, whereas Wild Bush Luxury’s offering involves a multi-day guided Maria Island walk with accommodation on the island. Similarly, Intrepid Travel’s guided walks in the Flinders Ranges and Top End involve routes and accommodation types which are different to those offered by Wild Bush Luxury.

• The Parties would continue to face competition from multiple alternative suppliers of guided walking experiences and/or accommodation in each of the areas of overlap and/or surrounding geographic areas. The Parties would also be constrained by consumers opting for self-guided walking tours in these areas.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Gilead Sciences, Inc. (Gilead), through its wholly owned subsidiary Ravens Sub, Inc. (Ravens Sub) proposes to acquire all the outstanding shares of Arcellx, Inc. (Arcellx) via a public cash tender offer. Following consummation of the offer, Ravens Sub will be merged with Arcellx, and Arcellx will continue as the surviving corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of Gilead.

Gilead is a biopharmaceutical company incorporated in the United States with headquarters in Foster City, California. Globally, Gilead’s activities focus on advancing medicines to prevent and treat life-threatening diseases, including HIV, liver disease, coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), inflammation and cancer. Its primary subsidiary in Australia is Gilead Sciences Pty. Ltd., through which Gilead supplies Australian customers with a range of medicines, including, but not limited to cell therapies for oncology indications.

Arcellx is a clinical-stage biotechnology company incorporated in the United States, with headquarters in Redwood City, California. It is focused on developing immunotherapies for patients with cancer and other incurable diseases.

Arcellx’s most advanced development program is a D-Domain-based chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell (CAR-T) therapy that targets the B-cell maturation antigen (or BCMA) for treatment of multiple myeloma. This technology is now called anitocabtagene autoleucel or anito-cel.

Gilead’s wholly owned subsidiary, Kite, and Arcellx co-develop and co-commercialise anito-cell and potential future next-generation autologous and non-autologous CAR-T cell therapy products for the treatment of multiple myeloma. Outside the United States, Gilead has the exclusive right to apply for regulatory approval, manufacture and commercialise any anito-cel products developed as cancer treatments, including for indications resulting from other clinical trials. Five of the locations for a Phase 3 clinical trial are in Australia (Royal Prince Alfred Hospital and Royal North Shore Hospital in New South Wales, Royal Adelaide Hospital in South Australia, Epworth HealthCare in Victoria, and Sir Charles Gairdner Hospital in Western Australia).

Gilead’s blood cancer treatment products marketed in Australia are Yescarta (Anti-CD19 CAR-T), Tecartus (Anti-CD19 CAR-T), and Zydelig (P13K inhibitor). Gilead does not have any manufacturing or R&D facilities in Australia. It runs clinical trials in Australia and employs an operations team to support those trials.

Arcellx’s operations are limited to the United States, it has not generated revenues from the sale of products in Australia (or anywhere else) to date, and its only indirect nexus to Australia is limited to the clinical trials exclusively run by Gilead. Its clinical and pre-clinical programs are narrowly focused on specific types of cell therapies, mainly for the treatment of cancer, and primarily, blood cancers.

determination_table_content.details: Gilead Sciences, Inc. (Gilead), through its wholly owned subsidiary Ravens Sub, Inc. (Ravens Sub) proposes to acquire all the outstanding shares of Arcellx, Inc. (Arcellx) via a public cash tender offer. Following consummation of the offer, Ravens Sub will be merged with Arcellx, and Arcellx will continue as the surviving corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of Gilead.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Gilead, is a biopharmaceutical company incorporated in the United States with headquarters in Foster City, California.

Globally, Gilead’s activities focus on advancing medicines to prevent and treat life-threatening diseases, including HIV, liver disease, coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), inflammation and cancer. Its primary subsidiary in Australia is Gilead Sciences Pty. Ltd., through which Gilead supplies Australian customers with a range of medicines, including, but not limited to cell therapies for oncology indications. In addition, Gilead runs clinical trials in Australia and employs an operations team to support those trials.

Arcellx, the target, is a clinical-stage biotechnology company incorporated in the United States, with headquarters in Redwood City, California. It is focused on developing immunotherapies for patients with cancer and other incurable diseases.

Arcellx’s operations are limited to the United States. It has not generated revenues from the sale of products in Australia to date, and its nexus to Australia is limited to clinical trials exclusively run by Gilead. Its clinical and pre-clinical programs focus on products for the treatment of cancer indications, primarily multiple myeloma, which are co-developed and co-commercialised by Gilead and Arcellx through an existing agreement. Outside the United States, Gilead has the exclusive right to apply for regulatory approval, manufacture and commercialise any anito-cel products developed as cancer treatments, including for indications resulting from other clinical trials.

determination_table_content.item: Overlap and relationship between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
There is no existing horizontal overlap between Gilead and Arcellx in Australia.

As described above, there is an existing relationship between Gilead and Arcellx allowing Gilead to exclusively commercialise jointly developed products in Australia.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• There are no competitive overlaps between Gilead and Arcellx in the research, development or marketing of multiple myeloma cell therapies in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Eli Lilly and Company (**Eli Lilly**) and CSL Behring LLC (**CSL Behring**) propose to enter into a Licence Agreement under which Eli Lilly would be granted certain exclusive rights to develop and commercialise Clazakizumab, an anti-interleukin-6 (**IL-6**) monoclonal antibody, for certain new indications including atrial fibrillation, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and other cardiovascular-related indications (the **Acquisition**). CSL Behring would retain exclusive rights to continue the development and commercialisation of Clazakizumab for the prevention of cardiovascular events in patients with end-stage kidney disease.

Eli Lilly is a US-based pharmaceutical company which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is headquartered in Indianapolis, US and is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

CSL Behring is a subsidiary of CSL Limited, a global biopharmaceutical business listed on the Australian Stock Exchange (ASX: CSL) and headquartered in Australia. CSL Limited develops, manufactures and markets therapies derived from human plasma and recombinant technologies for rare and serious diseases. It provides treatments for immunology, hematology, cardiovascular and metabolic disorders.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Eli Lilly and Company (Eli Lilly) and CSL Behring LLC (CSL Behring) propose to enter into a licence agreement under which Eli Lilly would be granted certain exclusive rights to develop and commercialise Clazakizumab, an anti-interleukin-6 (IL-6) monoclonal antibody, for certain new indications including atrial fibrillation, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and other cardiovascular indications (the Acquisition). Under the licence agreement, CSL Behring would retain exclusive rights to continue the development and commercialisation of Clazakizumab for the prevention of cardiovascular events in patients with end stage kidney disease.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Eli Lilly, is a US-based pharmaceutical company which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

The target, CSL Behring, is a subsidiary of CSL Limited, a publicly listed global biopharmaceutical business, which is headquartered in Australia and listed on the Australian Stock Exchange (ASX:CSL).

CSL Limited develops, manufactures and markets therapies derived from human plasma and recombinant technologies for rare and serious diseases. It provides treatments for immunology, hematology, cardiovascular and metabolic disorders.

determination_table_content.item: Overlap and relationship between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
Both parties are active in the development, manufacturing and marketing of human pharmaceutical products in Australia.

Clazakizumab is currently not commercialised by CSL Limited, and Eli Lilly does not market any IL-6 inhibitors or have any such products in clinical development. Eli Lilly does not market any other treatments for atrial fibrillation or atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Eli Lilly is developing other therapies for the treatment of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, one of the indications for which it plans to develop Clazakizumab. However, there are significant differences between these other pipeline therapies and Clazakizumab, including that the other therapies are not anti-inflammatory and that they have different therapeutic approaches, mechanisms of action, modality and target patients.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form and information from third parties.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• It appears unlikely that Eli Lilly’s existing pipeline therapies under development for atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (if they can be commercialised) would compete with Clazakizumab (if it can also be commercialised) because of fundamental differences between the therapies.

• There is no competitive overlap between Eli Lilly’s and CSL Behring’s research, development or marketing activities relating to IL-6 inhibitors.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Swiss Re International SE (**Swiss Re**) proposes to acquire certain assets from QBE Insurance Holdings Pty Ltd (**QBE**) relating to its Credit & Surety business (**QBE’s TC&S Business**), including data, employees and renewal rights (the **Acquisition**).

Swiss Re provides insurance, reinsurance and risk transfer solutions to mid to large-sized corporate customers. In Australia, Swiss Re provides a range of property and casualty insurance products, including non-payment insurance for banks, surety bonds and claims management services.

QBE is an Australian-based provider of a broad range of insurance and reinsurance products to personal, business, corporate and institutional customers. QBE’s TC&S Business provides:

* non‑payment insurance (also known as trade finance insurance) which insures commercial banks and financial institutions against the risk of counterparties’ non‑payment of loans, guarantees or letters of credit.
* trade credit insurance which insures corporate customers against the risk of non-payment of trade receivables arising from credit sales.
* surety bonds which involve providing a financial guarantee that a party will fulfill a contractual, statutory, or financial obligation with the insurer committing to intervene if the insured party fails to do so.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings

• the aggregation post-Acquisition appears low, with Swiss Re’s market shares in each of trade finance insurance and surety bonds estimated to be below 10%.

Novartis Finance Corporation (Novartis Finance), via its wholly-owned subsidiary Elba Merger Sub, Inc., proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding capital stock of Excellergy, Inc. (Excellergy) (the Acquisition).

Novartis Finance is a subsidiary of Novartis AG (collectively, Novartis). Novartis is a global medicines company headquartered in Basel, Switzerland. Novartis is engaged in the research, development, manufacturing, distribution, commercialisation and sale of medicines, with a focus on the following therapeutic areas: cardiovascular, renal and metabolic; immunology; neuroscience; oncology; and established brands.

In Australia, Novartis supplies a range of medicines to customers, including treatments for chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) such as Xolair, and has a further CSU medicine under evaluation by the Therapeutic Goods Administration.

Excellergy is a private biotechnology company based on Palo Alto, California, United States. Excellergy is developing next-generation anti‑immunoglobulin E (anti‑IgE) therapies for the treatment of IgE-driven allergic diseases, where the body produces specific IgE antibodies in response to substances that are generally harmless (allergens), resulting in allergic symptoms and inflammation. This includes Excellergy's lead asset, Exl-111, a half-life extended, high-affinity anti-IgE antibody, which is currently in Phase 1 clinical evaluation.

Excellergy's operations are limited to the United States and.it has not generated any revenue from sales of product in Australia (or anywhere else) to date. Excellergy's connection with Australia arises from the registration of trademarks and the establishment of an Australian subsidiary for the purpose of conducting clinical trials.

mergers.merger_description:
eBay Inc. (**eBay**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Depop Limited (**Depop**) from Etsy, Inc (**Etsy**) (the **Acquisition**).

eBay is a multinational e-commerce corporation headquartered in San Jose, California and listed on the Nasdaq. eBay facilitates business to consumer (**B2C**) and consumer to consumer (**C2C**) sales through its e-commerce platforms, including its Australian online marketplace, eBay.com.au.

eBay is a generalist platform which facilitates e-commerce transactions across a wide range of sectors in Australia, including electronics, cars, spare parts and accessories, furniture, collectibles, and fashion. eBay facilitates sales of both new and used products.

Depop started in 2011 as a social shopping platform and was acquired by Etsy in 2021. Depop is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Etsy, a global e-commerce company listed on the New York Stock Exchange and headquartered in New York, United States.

Depop operates a mobile-first pre-owned fashion online marketplace, and is predominantly active in the US, the UK and Australia, but also has a small level of activity in other countries.

The Acquisition is a global transaction.

eBay, Depop and Etsy overlap in the supply of online marketplace services to users, including buyers and sellers in Australia. eBay and Depop overlap in relation to the provision of these services for pre-owned fashion.

events.title: eBay - Depop - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: eBay Inc. (eBay)’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Depop Limited (Depop) (together, the Parties) from Etsy, Inc. (Etsy) (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, eBay, is a multinational e‑commerce company headquartered in San Jose, California. eBay operates a general online retail marketplace, facilitating both business‑to‑consumer (B2C) and consumer‑to‑consumer (C2C) transactions across a wide range of new and used product categories, including fashion, electronics, automotive parts and collectibles.

The target, Depop, is a multinational e‑commerce company headquartered in London and a wholly owned subsidiary of Etsy.

Depop operates a mobile‑first online retail marketplace, focused on pre‑owned fashion, with an emphasis on social discovery and peer‑to‑peer transactions. Depop is predominantly active in the United States, Australia and the United Kingdom.
The reasons for the ACCC’s determination are set out in section 2.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.
For the reasons given below, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect.
Overlap between the parties

eBay and Depop both provide online marketplaces that enable sellers to create listings for pre-owned fashion items (for example, clothing, shoes, accessories, handbags and watches) and buyers to search for and purchase those items.

eBay operates a general online retail marketplace that includes pre-owned fashion as a category, as well as a broad range of other product categories, such as electronics, automotive parts and collectibles. It offers multiple selling formats, including fixed price, live selling, and auctions. eBay also provides integrated payments and delivery options, buyer protection policies and seller performance standards. eBay has operated in Australia since 1999.

Depop is a mobile-first online retail marketplace that focuses on pre-owned fashion. It has a range of social discovery features, such as algorithmically curated feeds and the ability for buyers to follow other users and ‘like’ items.

Depop is primarily oriented towards C2C transactions, although there is a cohort of business sellers that use Depop regularly. Sales typically take place through a series of in-app ‘offers’, and Depop provides integrated payments, with sellers in Australia arranging shipping themselves. Depop has operated in Australia since 2020.
Industry background

Online marketplaces that include categories for pre-owned fashion typically involve a digital platform that provides a website or application (‘app’), which allows sellers to list items for sale. Sellers upload information regarding the product for sale including size (or measurements), condition (e.g., Like-New, Excellent, Good), brand, photographs, and a price. Potential purchasers can generally search by keyword or browse by category, with different search filters available depending on product type (e.g., Size, Condition, etc.).

Online marketplaces may facilitate sales through taking payment, or buyers and sellers may arrange to meet in person and process the sale outside the platform. Items listed for sale on the digital platform may come primarily from business sellers or consumer sellers, or from a mix of both. Similarly, while there are two distinct user groups: buyers and sellers, many individuals who buy items will also sell items, and they may do so using the same platform.

Online marketplaces can be general (offering a broad range of product categories) or specialised in one category (e.g. fashion, cars, watches). eBay, Facebook Marketplace and Gumtree are examples of general online marketplaces, while Depop, Vestiaire Collective, Chrono24, StockX and GOAT are examples of platforms which focus on a particular product category (i.e., fashion).

Online marketplaces are also multi-sided, which means they create value from attracting both sellers (inventory) and buyers (audience). As multi-sided platforms, they exhibit network effects—more listings draw more buyers, which in turn attracts more sellers. As inventory and audience numbers increase on a platform, this improves the platform’s ability to attract more users to its site and increase the number of items available for sale.
At the same time, user preferences are heterogeneous, and platforms may differentiate successfully by focusing on distinct categories or customer needs.

Users of online marketplaces may multi-home, and this may occur on both the buyer side and seller side (for example, a buyer browsing multiple platforms for the best price or buying different types of clothes from different platforms, or a seller maintaining listings across more than one platform at a time). However, multi-homing is more frictionless for buyers than for sellers, because sellers typically must create listings, manage queries, update item availability and coordinate shipping and returns on each platform they use.

Competition between online marketplaces occurs across multiple dimensions, including in relation to fees, ease of listing, search and discovery functionality, trust and safety settings, and shipping and payment options.
Competition assessment

The ACCC has considered the effects of the Acquisition by comparing the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition proceeds against the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition does not proceed.
Relevant areas of competition

The ACCC has considered whether the Acquisition is likely to substantially lessen competition in the supply of online marketplace services for C2C pre-owned fashion to sellers and/or buyers in Australia.

The ACCC has had regard to information and documents provided by the Parties (including demographic data on eBay and Depop sellers) as part of its assessment. However, the ACCC did not find it necessary to reach a concluded view on whether online marketplace services for C2C pre-owned fashion to sellers and/or buyers constitutes a distinct market or a segment of a broader market.
Horizontal unilateral effects in the supply of online marketplace services for C2C pre-owned fashion in Australia

The ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in relation to the supply of online marketplace services for C2C pre-owned fashion in Australia. The ACCC finds that, while the Acquisition would remove competition between eBay and Depop, post-acquisition, eBay would continue to face competition from alternative suppliers
The ACCC’s view, based on the information and documents provided by the Parties and market participants, is that:

items: There are several online marketplaces which feature C2C pre-owned fashion, but eBay and Depop are currently the largest suppliers that offer buyers and sellers integrated services for listing, messaging and payments.

Since entering Australia in 2020, Depop has rapidly increased its market share through a differentiated business model compared to other online marketplaces, with a stronger focus on discovery and social media.
eBay has more recently focused on growing its C2C business, including through eBay’s acquisition of Tise in 2025, and subsequent expansion of Tise into Australia in 2026.

eBay and Depop monitor and respond to each other in Australia. However, there is limited overlap between eBay and Depop pre-owned fashion sellers: during the last 3 years, only a small proportion of sellers on eBay sold a pre-owned fashion item on Depop in the same year, and vice versa.

eBay and Depop are also somewhat differentiated in terms of their format and users—eBay is more search-driven with an older demographic, whereas Depop is more discovery-driven with a younger demographic.

While network effects may create barriers to entry and expansion, online marketplaces for pre-owned fashion are a growing and dynamic segment, with competition driven in part by each platform’s ability to attract and retain new users.
The likely, timely and sufficient entry of a new platform will ensure eBay continues to face competition from alternative suppliers.

mergers.merger_description:
MA Marina Fund Holdco Pty Ltd and MA Marina Fund TT Pty Ltd (together, the **Acquirers**) propose to acquire 100% of the real property and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of the Gold Coast City Marina (**Gold Coast City Marina**). The Acquirers are indirectly solely controlled by funds managed by MA Financial Group Limited (**MA Financial**).

  

The Acquirers are part of the MA Marina Fund, managed by entities held by MA Financial, which invests in and operates marinas. The MA Marina Fund currently operates a portfolio of 16 marinas located along Australia’s eastern seaboard under the ‘d’Albora’ brand (**MA Marina Portfolio**). The MA Marina Portfolio includes 2 marinas in south-east Queensland – the Pacific Point Marina in Steiglitz on the Gold Coast, and the East Coast Marina in Manly, Brisbane.

Each marina in the MA Marina Portfolio operates as an integrated marina services facility combining vessel storage, maintenance and associated commercial uses. Marinas in the MA Marina Portfolio typically provide wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing, and marine fuel.

The Gold Coast City Marina is a full-service marina and marina industrial facility located in Coomera, Queensland. It provides wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing and marine fuel.

determination_table_content.details:
MA Marina Fund Holdco Pty Ltd and MA Marina Fund TT Pty Ltd’s (together, the Acquirers)’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the real property and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of the Gold Coast City Marina (Gold Coast City Marina) (the Acquisition).

The Acquirers are indirectly solely controlled by funds managed by MA Financial Group Limited (MA Financial).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The Acquirers are part of the MA Marina Fund, managed by entities held by MA Financial, which invests in and operates marinas. The MA Marina Fund currently operates a portfolio of 16 marinas located along Australia’s eastern seaboard under the ‘d’Albora’ brand (MA Marina Portfolio). The MA Marina Portfolio includes 2 marinas in south-east Queensland – the Pacific Point Marina in Steiglitz on the Gold Coast, and the East Coast Marina in Manly, Brisbane.

Each marina in the MA Marina Portfolio operates as an integrated marina services facility combining vessel storage, maintenance and associated commercial uses. Marinas in the MA Marina Portfolio typically provide wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing, and marine fuel.

The target, Gold Coast City Marina, is a full-service marina and marina industrial facility located in Coomera, Queensland. It provides wet berthing and other ancillary marina services including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop and industrial leasing and marine fuel.

determination_table_content.item: Overlap and relationship between the parties
determination_table_content.details: The Acquirers and MA Financial (together, the Parties) overlap in the Gold Coast region in the supply of wet berthing and ancillary marina services, including dry storage, boatyard storage, marine workshop, marine fuel and industrial leasing (e.g. to boat builders, mechanics, brokers), for recreational and commercial vessels.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, information from third parties, and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings:

• The Parties’ market share aggregation in either the supply of wet berthing or ancillary marina services as a result of the Acquisition is estimated to be low on any market definition;

• The Parties would continue to face competition from multiple alternative suppliers of wet berthing and ancillary marina services in the Gold Coast region, including The Boat Works, Marina Mirage, Main Beach Superyacht Marina, Southport Yacht Club, Hope Island Marina, Sanctuary Cove, Runaway Bay Marina and Hope Harbour Marina.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
On 27 February 2026, Paramount Skydance Corporation (**Paramount**) and Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (**WBD**) signed an agreement (the**Merger Agreement**). Under the terms of the Merger Agreement, Paramount will pay USD 31.00 per share in cash for all outstanding shares of WBD (the **Acquisition**).

Paramount is a global media, streaming and entertainment company that supplies content and experiences, including films, television series, and animated content.

Paramount’s portfolio of brands and production assets includes Paramount Pictures, Paramount Television, Paramount+, CBS, CBS News, CBS Sports, Nickelodeon, MTV, BET, Comedy Central, Showtime, Pluto TV and Skydance Media LLC animation, interactive/games and sports divisions.

In Australia, Paramount operates the free-to-air (**FTA**) television network, Network 10. Network 10 is one of five Australian FTA TV networks, and operates four television channels (Channel 10, 10 Drama, 10 Comedy and Nickelodeon) and a free online streaming service (‘10’). Paramount also operates Paramount+, a paid digital subscription video on-demand and live streaming service available to Australian consumers. Paramount also licenses audiovisual content to TV channels and digital services in Australia and distributes films to cinemas in Australia through its local theatrical distribution company.

WBD is a global media and entertainment company that creates and distributes a portfolio of content and products across television, film, streaming and interactive gaming.

WBD’s brands and production assets include Discovery Channel, CNN, DC Studios, New Line Cinema, TNT Sports, Eurosport, HBO Max, HBO, Warner Bros. Motion Picture Group, Warner Bros. Television Group, and Warner Bros. Games.

WBD operates several Pay TV services in Australia (Discovery, Investigation Discovery, Animal Planet, TLC, Discovery Turbo, Travel Channel and HGTV) and the subscription video on demand service HBO Max. In Australia, WBD distributes films to exhibitors via a third party, Universal Pictures. WBD also has local subsidiaries active in film and television production, including Big Pond Productions Pty Ltd and Warner Bros. International Television Production Australia.

NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, NDC), through its wholly-owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to acquire from the Comfy Dental Trust 100% of the business assets comprising the Comfy Dental Clinic (Comfy Dental).

NDC is a dental services organisation that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services. NDC has dental clinics in all Australian states and territories except Tasmania. It has one existing dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA (DB Dental Mandurah). NDC is majority owned by funds managed by Crescent Capital Partners.

Comfy Dental is a dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA that provides a range of general dentistry services.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, NDC, is a dental services organisation that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services. NDC has dental clinics in all Australian states and territories except Tasmania. It has one existing dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA (DB Dental Mandurah). NDC is majority owned by funds managed by Crescent Capital Partners.

The target, Comfy Dental is a dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA that provides a range of general dentistry services.
The parties overlap in the supply of general dentistry services.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• The aggregation in the supply of general dentistry services in the local area arising from the Acquisition is not significant. This assessment is not impacted by consideration of previous acquisitions by NDC in the last three years.

• NDC will continue to face competition from a number of alternative general dental clinics in the local area surrounding the target.

mergers.merger_description:
WIN Television Network Pty Ltd (**WIN TV**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in NBN Enterprises Pty Limited (**NBN**) from Nine Network Australia Holdings Pty Ltd and 100% of the shares in Television Holdings Darwin Pty Limited (**NTD**) from Nine Network Australia Pty Limited (the **Acquisition**).  


NBN and NTD are wholly owned subsidiaries of the Nine Network Australia Holdings Pty Ltd and Nine Network Australia Pty Limited respectively, and are ultimately controlled by Nine Entertainment Co. Holdings Limited (the parent company of a group of companies known as the Nine Group). Both NBN and NTD, via their respective wholly owned subsidiaries, operate regional free-to-air television stations. NBN broadcasts across the Northern NSW (**NNSW**) and Gold Coast regions, while NTD broadcasts to Darwin, Palmerston and the surrounding areas in the Northern Territory.   


WIN TV is the parent company of a group of wholly owned subsidiaries which broadcast free-to-air television in other parts of regional Australia.   

  

There is no geographic overlap between the local broadcasting areas of WIN TV, NBN and NTD.

determination_table_content.details: WIN Television Network Pty Ltd (WIN TV) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in NBN Enterprises Pty Limited (NBN) from Nine Network Australia Holdings Pty Ltd and 100% of the shares in Television Holdings Darwin Pty Limited (NTD) from Nine Network Australia Pty Limited (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, WIN TV, is the parent company of a group of wholly owned subsidiaries with broadcast free-to-air television in different licence areas throughout regional Australia. WIN TV acquires the majority of its content for broadcast through an affiliate relationship with the Nine Group.

The targets, NBN and NTD, operate regional free-to-air television stations, with NBN broadcasting across the Northern New South Wales and Gold Coast region and NTD broadcasting to Darwin, Palmerston and the surrounding areas in the Northern Territory. Both NBN and NTD currently broadcast a combination of content produced by the Nine Group, together with locally produced content.
There is no geographic overlap between the local broadcasting areas of WIN TV, NBN and NTD.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, information from third parties, and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• There is no overlap between the Parties as they operate in different geographic areas.

• The Parties would likely continue to face competition from multiple alternative suppliers of broadcasting and advertising in those regional areas in Australia, including by Seven Network and Ten Network.

mergers.merger_description:
Heidelberg Materials Australia Holdings Pty Ltd (**HMAH**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital of Regional Group Australia Pty Limited, Macquarie Geotechnical Pty Limited and Machinery Sales Pty Limited, being wholly owned subsidiaries of Maas Group Holdings Limited (**MGH**), which together comprise MGH’s construction materials business (**MAAS**) (the **Acquisition**).

The MAAS business includes the following assets (for completeness, this list also includes assets which MAAS has recently acquired from Colas which HMA intends to be included in the Acquisition):

  

(a)    43 quarries (including hard rock and natural sand quarries) in Queensland, New South Wales (**NSW**) and Victoria,   

(b)    18 fixed and 4 mobile ready-mix concrete (**RMX**) plants in Queensland, NSW and Victoria,   

(c)    4 operating asphalt sites in Queensland, NSW and the Australian Capital Territory (**ACT**), and an asphalt site under construction in Victoria,   

(d)    1 heavy construction and demolition (**C&D**) waste disposal and recycling facility in Victoria,   

(e)    5 fixed and 8 mobile materials testing and geotechnical services labs in Queensland, NSW and Victoria, and   

(f)    3 machinery sales sites in Queensland, NSW and Victoria.   


HMAH, formerly known as Hanson Holdings Australia Pty Ltd, together with its related entities in Australia (collectively, **HMA**) supplies a range of construction materials, including RMX, quarry products (including coarse and fine aggregates), asphalt and sustainable / recycled construction materials. HMA also operates waste disposal and recycling facilities and has a 50% interest in the Cement Australia joint venture, which produces and sells cement.

  

MGH is a diversified industrials group, which operates a civil construction and hire business, a construction equipment manufacture and retailing business, and a property development business. MGH’s construction materials business, MAAS, operates RMX plants, hard rock and natural sand quarries, an asphalt business, a recycling centre, and also provides materials testing and geotechnical services, and machinery sales and earthmoving services.

HMA and MAAS (the **Parties**) overlap in the supply of the following products in the following regions:

* **RMX** in Greater Melbourne and Geelong, Central Queensland (Rockhampton and Blackwater), NSW (Tamworth, Dubbo, Illawarra and Shoalhaven),
* **coarse aggregates** in Greater Melbourne, Central Queensland (Biloela), South East Queensland (to the extent that MAAS is the landholder of the Yatala quarry, which is operated by Crushing Dynamics under a lease), NSW (Dubbo, Illawarra and Shoalhaven),
* **fine aggregates** in Greater Melbourne,
* **asphalt** in Greater Melbourne (in the future once MAAS’s plant in Pakenham becomes operational), South East Queensland, and
* **heavy C&D waste disposal and recycling services** in Greater Melbourne.

The Parties’ coarse aggregates and fine aggregates products are upstream inputs into RMX and asphalt, and can also be used for other end uses such as road base, ballast, fill material, drainage and landscaping.

mergers.merger_description:
Maas Group Holdings Limited (**Maas**) proposes to acquire, through its subsidiary Asphalt Operators Australia Pty Ltd (**AOA**), a 50% interest in Coltek Asphalt Pty Ltd (**Coltek**), which will own and operate the hot-mix asphalt business currently owned and operated by a subsidiary of COLAS Australia Group Pty Ltd (**COLAS**) at Caloundra on the Sunshine Coast, Queensland. The remaining 50% interest in Coltek will be retained by COLAS, and Coltek will operate the hot-mix asphalt business at Caloundra as an incorporated joint venture between Maas and COLAS.

Maas also proposes to acquire, through its subsidiary Austek Roads NSW Pty Ltd (**Austek Roads NSW**) (which forms part of the AOA business), the hot-mix asphalt business currently owned and operated by a subsidiary of COLAS at Tomago, NSW (Newcastle).

Maas (ASX: MGH) is a diversified industrial group with operations in construction materials, civil construction and hire, manufacturing and equipment sales, and residential and commercial real estate. Under the Austek brand the AOA business produces asphalt and supplies roadlaying services (such as asphalt paving, asphalt repairs and spray seal services).

COLAS supplies asphalt, road maintenance and preservation services, quarrying and binder management services, dust management and quality control services, and research and development services. COLAS is a subsidiary of France-based COLAS SA.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Maas Group Holdings Limited (Maas) proposed acquisition, through subsidiary Asphalt Operators Australia Pty Ltd (AOA), of 50% interest in Coltek Asphalt Pty Ltd (Coltek), which will own and operate the hot-mix asphalt business currently owned and operated by a subsidiary of COLAS Australia Group Pty Ltd (COLAS) at Caloundra on the Sunshine Coast, Queensland and, through its subsidiary Austek Roads NSW Pty Ltd (Austek Roads NSW), of 100% interest in COLAS’ hot-mix asphalt business at Tomago, NSW (Newcastle).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Maas, is a diversified industrial group with operations in construction materials, civil construction and hire, manufacturing and equipment sales, and residential and commercial real estate.

Under the Austek brand the AOA business produces asphalt and supplies roadlaying services (such as asphalt paving, asphalt repairs and spray seal services).

The targets are two hot-mix asphalt plants owned by COLAS, in Caloundra and Tomago. COLAS supplies asphalt, road maintenance and preservation services, quarrying and binder management services, dust management and quality control services, and research and development services. COLAS is a subsidiary of France-based COLAS SA.

determination_table_content.item: Overlap and relationship between the parties
determination_table_content.details: Maas and COLAS both supply hot-mix asphalt production and road laying services, however the parties do not currently compete in the same geographic markets.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form and information from third partes.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• For the supply of hot-mix asphalt and roadlaying services, there is no horizontal overlap between Maas and COLAS in in the Newcastle/Hunter region. In the Sunshine Coast region there may be some minimal overlap.

• The Parties would likely continue to be constrained in both regions by alternative suppliers of hot-mix asphalt and roadlaying services including Boral, Downer EDI and Fulton Hogan.

• The Parties are unlikely to have the ability and/or incentive post-acquisition to foreclose downstream rivals’ access to hot-mix asphalt as there are alternative ex-bin hot-mix asphalt suppliers including Boral, Downer EDI and Fulton Hogan.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Mastercard Incorporated International (**Mastercard**) through its subsidiary Ares UK Holdco 2 Limited proposes to acquire BVNK Holdings Limited and B Corporation S.A.M (together, **BVNK**) from its current shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

Mastercard is a technology company in the global payments industry. Mastercard’s main activities include ownership and operation of payment card schemes and provision of switching services for card transactions. Mastercard provides a payments network to facilitate the switching of payment transactions in fiat, and offers payment capabilities that include automated clearing house transactions as well as other payment-related services.

BVNK supplies stablecoin infrastructure services, by building and supplying infrastructure to enable customers to send, receive, convert, spend and earn stablecoins and 'fiat' currency across a variety of payment 'rails' and blockchains.

Stablecoin is a type of cryptocurrency designed to keep a stable value, usually by being pegged to a traditional 'fiat' currency.

anzsic_codes.name: Other Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction

mergers.merger_description:
Symal Group Limited (**Symal Group**) proposes to acquire 80% of the entity which will own the business, assets and related entities of J. Davison Nominees Pty Ltd, P&A Equipment Pty Ltd and Davison Contractors Pty Ltd (collectively referred to as **Davison Earthmovers**). The final transaction will be subject to the completion of a restructure of the Davison Earthmovers.
  

Symal Group will then acquire 80% of the shares in P&A Equipment, with the balance of the 20% (**Option Shares**) owned by one or more entities owned and controlled by Paul and Anna Davison (**Davison Minority Shareholders**). 
  

The Parties will agree to a put and call option arrangement in respect of Symal Group acquiring the Option Shares following completion of the initial acquisition. 
  

Symal Group is an Australian based self-performing contractor providing construction and engineering services, specialising in the roads, rail, defence, justice, ports and energy sectors. The services provided by Symal Group include various forms of construction, project management, plant and equipment hire, material sales and recycling and repurposing. 
  

Davison Earthmovers is a South Australian based civil contractor specialising in civil construction and infrastructure. Their capabilities span bulk earthworks, excavation and material movement, and roadwork and pavement construction, alongside project management and design-and-construct offerings. 
  

Symal Group provides its services across Australia, particularly Victoria and Queensland, while Davison Earthmovers’ supply of services is limited to South Australia.

determination_table_content.details:
Symal Group Limited (Symal Group) proposes to acquire 80% of the entity which will own the business, assets and related entities of J.

Davison Nominees Pty Ltd, P&A Equipment Pty Ltd and Davison Contractors Pty Ltd (collectively referred to as Davison Earthmovers) (together known as the Parties). The final transaction will be subject to the completion of a restructure of Davison Earthmovers, where shares in the restructured entity will then be transferred to P&A Equipment Pty Ltd (P&A Equipment).

Symal Group will then acquire 80% of the shares in P&A Equipment, with the balance of the remaining 20% (Option Shares) owned by one or more entities owned and controlled by Paul and Anna Davison.

The Parties will agree to a put and call option arrangement in respect of Symal Group acquiring the Option Shares following completion of the initial acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Symal Group, is an Australian based self-performing contractor providing construction and engineering services, specialising in the roads, rail, defence, justice, ports and energy sectors.

The target, Davison Earthmovers, is an Australian based civil contracting business that specialises in civil construction and infrastructure delivery, with a core focus on large-scale development projects.

There is no material overlap between the Parties. Davison Earthmovers supplies bulk earthworks and roadworks services in South Australia only, while Symal Group mostly supplies services relating to construction and engineering, and mostly supplies these in Victoria and Queensland with negligible supply in South Australia.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form as well as further information provided by the Parties following pre-notification engagement with the ACCC.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings.

• The Parties would likely continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of civil contracting, earthworks and roadwork services in South Australia, Victoria and Queensland, including several competitors which are of a similar or larger size to the Parties.

• The Parties are not close competitors, as Davison Earthmovers supplies bulk earthworks and roadworks in South Australia only.

Symal Group primarily operates in Victoria and Queensland, with a negligible presence in South Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review, please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Hg Pooled Management Limited (**Hg Capital**) through a special purpose vehicle, Onward AcquireCo, Inc., proposes to acquire 100% of share capital in Onestream, Inc and OneStream Software LLC (together **OneStream**).

Hg Capital is a private equity firm focussed on investment in Europe and the US. Hg Capital’s portfolio companies include GS Topco GP LLC (insightsoftware), Prophix Software Inc (Prophix), and LucaNet Group GmbH (LucaNet). These companies provide various enterprise performance management software solutions throughout Australia for financial consolidation, budgeting, analysis, and planning and management reporting.

  

OneStream is a software provider offering a platform that unifies an enterprise's core financial functions and broader operation data and processes to increase the strategic impact of the Office of the CFO. OneStream's platform – the Digital Finance Cloud – provides capabilities centred on financial close and consolidation, and financial and operational planning and reporting (i.e. enterprise performance management).

The acquirer, Hg Capital, is a private equity firm focussed on investment in Europe and the US. Hg Capital’s portfolio companies include GS Topco GP LLC (insightsoftware), Prophix Software Inc (Prophix), and LucaNet Group GmbH (LucaNet). These companies provide various enterprise performance management software solutions throughout Australia for financial consolidation, budgeting, analysis, and planning and management reporting.

The target, OneStream, is a software provider offering a platform that unifies an enterprise's core financial functions and broader operation data and processes to increase the strategic impact of the Office of the CFO. In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, information from third partes and publicly available information.

The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the following findings:

• The Parties do not compete closely as they primarily offer a different range and quality of products to different groups of customers.
