mergers.merger_description:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa Fund (**Energy Bay**) proposes to acquire from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the **CCG Members**):

* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,
* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and
* first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in respect of any further sites of the kind described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application.

Energy Bay is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South Wales (**NSW**), Australian Capital Territory (**ACT**), Western Australia (**WA**), Victoria (**VIC**) and Queensland (**QLD**). Energy Bay Group also owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd (**Diamond Energy**), an electricity retailer that operates in all National Electricity Market (**NEM**) jurisdictions.

The CCG Members are subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group, which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (**CNI**) and Centuria Funds Management Limited (**CFML**). CNI is an ASX-listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria Capital Fund. The CCG Members invest in and manage properties for investment funds. As part of that primary business activity, in each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia (**SA**), and WA, they also:

* operate embedded network infrastructure at some of their sites,
* engage in behind the meter generation or storage at their sites, and
* supply and sell electricity to their commercial tenants connected to their embedded networks (**embedded network services**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa Fund (Energy Bay), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

a. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,

b. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and

c. first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in respect of any further sites of the kind described in the transaction documents, from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the CCG Members), which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (CNI) and Centuria Funds Management Limited (CFML), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The acquirer, Energy Bay, is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South Wales (NSW), Australian Capital Territory (ACT), Western Australia (WA), Victoria (VIC) and Queensland (QLD). Energy Bay Group also owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd, an electricity retailer that operates in all National Electricity Market (NEM) jurisdictions.

The vendors, the CCG Members, are subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group, which is comprised of CNI and CFML. CNI is an ASX-listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria Capital Fund.

In each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia, and WA, the CCG Members:

a. operate embedded network infrastructure,

b. engage in behind the meter generation and storage at their embedded network sites, and

c. supply and sell electricity to commercial tenants connected to their embedded networks. (together, Embedded Network Services). Through the Acquisition, Energy Bay Group will increase its share of supply of Embedded Network Services in WA and the NEM.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:

a. there is meaningful horizontal overlap between Energy Bay Group and the CCG Members in the supply of Embedded Network Services in WA and the NEM, and Energy Bay appears to be one of the larger suppliers of Embedded Network Services in the NEM

b. the extent to which Energy Bay Group is likely to be constrained by alternative suppliers of Embedded Network Services post-acquisition is uncertain

c. there is a vertical relationship between the Embedded Network Services supplied by the parties and the electricity retailing operations of Energy Bay Group’s subsidiary, Diamond Energy, and the impact of the Acquisition on Energy Bay Group’s ability and/or incentive to foreclose rivals warrants further consideration.

The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant consideration through the notification process, including via consultation with third parties.

Given the above, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the Acquisition meeting the notification thresholds. However, the ACCC notes that based on the Australian revenue and transaction value figures provided, the Acquisition may meet the monetary notification thresholds in ss 2-1 and 2-3 of the Competition and Consumer (Notification of Acquisitions) Determination 2025.

For these reasons, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Swan Topco Pty Ltd proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Stanley International College Pty Ltd (**Stanley International College**) by an acquisition vehicle, Swan Bidco Pty Ltd, that is indirectly and solely controlled by funds managed by Quadrant Private Equity (the **Acquisition**).

Quadrant Private Equity is a private equity firm headquartered in Sydney, Australia, and supplying financial and investment management services across Australia and New Zealand. It typically invests in mid-market companies across consumer and retail, healthcare, business, professional and industrial services, as well as technology and tech-enabled service industries. Quadrant Private Equity’s portfolio companies do not, and have not previously, held any interests in any higher education, vocational education or training service providers.

Stanley International College is an independent institute of higher education and vocational education and training services. It employs over 200 staff and is a multi-campus institution with a presence in Perth and Adelaide. Stanley International College provides a range of vocational training, bachelor’s and post-graduate degrees and professional year qualifications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Swan Topco Pty Ltd (Swan Topco), through its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary Swan Bidco Pty Ltd (Swan Bidco), applied for a notification waiver in respect of Swan Bidco’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in Stanley International College Pty Ltd (Stanley International College) from Chintalia Pty Ltd and Kaishwer Pty Ltd (together, the Vendors), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Swan Bidco, is indirectly controlled by funds managed by Quadrant Private Equity (QPE). QPE is a private equity firm headquartered in Sydney, Australia, and supplying financial and investment management services across Australia and New Zealand. It typically invests in mid-market companies across consumer and retail, healthcare, business, professional and industrial services, as well as technology and tech-enabled service industries.

The target, Stanley International College, is an independent institute of higher education and vocational education and training services. It employs over 200 staff and is a multi-campus institution with a presence in Perth and Adelaide.

Stanley International College provides a range of vocational training, bachelor and post-graduate degrees and professional year qualifications.

Quadrant Private Equity’s portfolio companies do not, and have not previously, held any interests in any higher education, vocational education or training service providers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap between QPE’s portfolio companies and Stanley International College in the supply of higher education and/or vocational education training services.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Adyen N.V. (**Adyen**) is a financial technology company headquartered in Amsterdam, The Netherlands. Adyen is active in the supply of a global payments platform that enables businesses to accept and process payments across online, mobile, and instore channels, and offers related products and services.

Talon.One GmbH (**Talon.One)** is a software company headquartered in Berlin, Germany Talon.One is active in the supply of a promotion, loyalty and incentive management platform through a 'software as a service' (SaaS) model.

Adyen proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Talon.One from its existing shareholders. Upon completion, Adyen will acquire sole control over Talon.One and its three wholly-owned subsidiaries: Talon.One Inc. (United States), Talon.One UK Ltd. (United Kingdom) and Talon.One SG Pte Ltd. (Singapore).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Adyen N.V. (Ayden) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Talon.One GmbH (Talon. One), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Adyen is a financial technology company headquartered in Amsterdam, The Netherlands. Adyen is active in the supply of a global payments platform that enables businesses to accept and process payments across online, mobile, and instore channels, and offers related products and services.

The target, Talon.One is a software company headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Talon.One is active in the supply of a promotion, loyalty and incentive management platform through a 'software as a service' (SaaS) model.

Following completion, Adyen and Talon.One intend to develop functionality that would allow loyalty features to be integrated more closely into the payment processing flow.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Adyen and Talon.One in the supply of promotion, loyalty and/or incentive management platform services

b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products and services post-acquisition

c. there are alternative suppliers of payments platforms and promotion, loyalty and incentive management platforms in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
KKR Spire Aggregator L.P., a special purpose vehicle indirectly wholly owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. L.P. (together with its affiliates, **KKR**) alongside Carrick Capital and other members of the consortium propose acquiring control over Saviynt, Inc.  

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds.

Saviynt is a cyber security provider focused on identity solutions. Saviynt’s solutions allow companies (and other organisations) to manage log-in details for their employees when accessing company resources. Saviynt provides an Identity Governance & Administration software that centralizes lifecycle management, access requests, compliance, and audit across hybrid IT environments, including human and non-human identities, along with a number of adjacent identity products.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: KKR Spire Aggregator L.P. applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. L.P. (KKR) led consortium’s proposed acquisition of share capital in Saviynt, Inc. (Saviynt), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
27 January 2026

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions.

KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds KKR Spire Aggregator L.P. is a special purpose vehicle indirectly wholly owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by KKR.

Alongside KKR, vehicles controlled by asset management company Carrick Management Partners II (collectively, Carrick Capital), LLC, Verify Investments, LLC and Harjeet Kaur Shariff, LLC are proposing to acquire an interest in Saviynt. Carrick Capital is an investment firm focussed on enterprise software, software-as-a-service and technology-enabled services, with experience in founding, scaling and optimising businesses.

The target, Saviynt, is a cyber security provider focused on identity solutions. Saviynt’s solutions allow companies (and other organisations) to manage log-in details for their employees when accessing company resources. Saviynt provides interoperable Identity Governance & Administration software that centralises lifecycle management, access requests, compliance, and audit across hybrid IT environments, including human and non-human identities, along with a number of adjacent identity products.

The KKR led consortium does not supply goods or services that horizontally overlap with Saviynt. The KKR led consortium, with its investments in software-related services, may be considered to provide products that are vertical and/or complementary in nature to Saviynt’s cybersecurity products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant, including in the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap between the KKR led consortium’s portfolio companies’ and Saviynt in the supply of identify focussed cybersecurity software.

b. Whilst there is a potential vertical and/or conglomerate relationship between cybersecurity products supplied by the KKR led consortium and Saviynt, the parties are likely to continue to be competitively constrained by a wide range of alternative suppliers of cybersecurity and identity focussed cybersecurity software in Australia and globally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
IBWM Pty Ltd (**IBWM**), a subsidiary of Ironbark Investment Partners Pty Ltd (**Ironbark**), will acquire 40% of the shares on issue in Viola Group Holdings Pty Ltd (**Viola Group**). Viola Group is the parent company of Viola Finance Pty Ltd, Viola Private Holdings Pty Ltd, Viola Services Pty Ltd and Viola Private Wealth Pty Ltd (**Viola**).

Ironbark has a business division which provides financial advice to retail and wholesale clients. Viola Group focuses on private wealth and high net worth clients.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: IBWM Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Ironbark Investment Partners Pty Ltd (Ironbark), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 40% of the shares on issue in Viola Group Holdings Pty Ltd (Viola Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
9 April 2026

The acquirer, IBWM Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Ironbark. Ironbark has a business division which provides financial advice to retail and wholesale clients.

The target, Viola Group, is the parent company of Viola Finance Pty Ltd, Viola Private Holdings Pty Ltd, Viola Services Pty Ltd and Viola Private Wealth Pty Ltd (Viola). Viola Group focuses on private wealth and high net worth clients.

Both parties provide financial advice and wealth management services in Sydney. The services provided by Ironbark and Viola include financial planning advice, superannuation advice, retirement planning, cash flow planning, listed and unlisted securities advice, transaction execution and management, tax advice and life insurance advice.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Ironbark and Viola in the supply of financial advice and wealth management services in Sydney and across Australia

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial advice and wealth management services in Sydney and across Australia is estimated to be low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. there are alternative suppliers of financial advice and wealth management services in Sydney and across Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The acquisition will involve a deleveraging through a debt-to-equity swap resulting in Segula’s creditors, Eiffel Investment Group SAS (**Eiffel**) and Eurazeo SE (**Eurazeo**), acquiring joint control of Segula Holding SAS (**Segula**).

The acquisition is part of a financial restructuring aimed at saving Segula from judicial liquidation. It will be carried out under the supervision of the Nanterre Commercial Court (accelerated safeguard procedure) in France and result in Eiffel and Eurazeo each holding 50% of the voting rights in Segula.

Eiffel is an asset management company ultimately and exclusively controlled by Impala SAS (**Impala**). Impala is a French private diversified investment company, active in France and internationally in asset management and corporate finance, as well as in the energy and finance sectors.

Eurazeo is a French listed global investment group active in private equity, private debt and real assets.

Segula is a global engineering and technology consulting group providing technical services and innovation solutions primarily for the automotive, aerospace, energy, rail and marine sectors. Segula’s activities in Australia are currently limited to the provision of professional engineering services to primarily the automotive and rail sectors.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Segula Holdings SAS (Segula) has agreed with the primary holders of bond debt issued by Segula to convert their existing liabilities to equity. As part of the restructure, Eiffel Investment Group SAS (Eiffel) and Eurazeo SE (Eurazeo) will acquire joint control of Segula by way of a debt-to-equity swap (the Acquisition).

Eiffel and Eurazeo jointly applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
26 February 2026

Eiffel is an asset management company ultimately and exclusively controlled by Impala SAS (Impala). Impala is a French private diversified investment company, active in France and internationally in the asset management, finance, and energy sectors.

Eurazeo is a French listed global investment group active in private equity, private debt and real assets.

The target, Segula, is a global engineering and technology consulting group providing technical services and innovation solutions primarily for the automotive, aerospace, energy, rail and marine sectors.

Segula’s activities in Australia are currently limited to the provision of professional engineering services to primarily the automotive and rail sectors.

Eiffel and Eurazeo do not provide engineering and technology consulting services in Australia. Eiffel and Eurazeo’s connected entities may have commercial relationships with Segula as potential customers for engineering consulting services or as potential suppliers of solutions or equipment useful to Segula.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Eiffel, Eurazeo and Segula in the supply of engineering and technology consulting services in Australia, and there is a limited commercial relationship between Eiffel’s and Eurazeo’s connected entities and Sequla

b. Segula’s share of engineering and technology consulting services in Australia is low

c. there are alternative suppliers of engineering and technology consulting services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The transaction involves the indirect acquisition of joint control by funds advised or managed by HongShan Capital Advisors Limited (**HSG**) and by Rosa Investments Pte. Ltd. (**Rosa**), an investment holding company indirectly wholly owned by Temasek Holdings Private Limited (**Temasek**), over Golden Goose Group S.p.A. (**GGG**), a holding company which in turns owns the entire share capital of Golden Goose S.p.A. (**Golden Goose**).

Golden Goose is an Italian-headquartered company specialising in the design, manufacture and sale of premium apparel, with a strong focus on footwear, in addition to ready-to-wear clothing and accessories. Golden Goose distributes its products through its own retail stores, wholesale channels, and its online platform, operating across Europe, the United States and Asia.

HSG is a venture capital and private equity firm headquartered in Hong Kong. HSG holds controlling interests in Holzweiler Items AS (**Holzweiler**) and Ami Paris SAS (**Ami Paris**).

Incorporated in 1974, Temasek is an investment company headquartered in Singapore and has a global portfolio covering a range of industries.

The acquisition consists of BidCo (a company wholly owned by GG11 S.p.A. (**HoldCo**), an entity indirectly wholly owned by HSG), acquiring:

a. 100% of the shares in Astrum S.a.p.A. di Astrum 4 S.r.l. & C., a company which, in turn, owns 88.05% of the share capital of GGG; and

b. the shares representing the remaining 11.95% of the share capital of GGG from PSLG Limited, pursuant to a sale and purchase agreement entered into by and among Permira VII Investment Platform Limited, Astrum 4 S.r.l., Astrum 5 S.r.l., Astrum 6 S.r.l., CEP IV Participations S.à r.l., SICAR and Carlyle Growth Investments III, on one side, and BidCo, as purchaser, on the other side.

Immediately prior to or at closing of the acquisition, Rosa and True Light Investments P Pte. Ltd., certain managers of GGG, funds advised or managed by Permira Holdings Limited (**Permira**) and funds advised or managed by Carlyle will invest alongside HSG in HoldCo, and indirectly in BidCo and GGG.

Following the acquisition, GGG will be jointly controlled by HSG and Temasek.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rosa Investments Pte. Ltd. (Rosa) and GG12 S.p.A. (BidCo) applied for a notification waiver in respect of the following proposed indirect acquisition, by funds advised or managed by HongShan Capital Advisors Limited (HSG) and by Rosa, an investment holding company indirectly wholly owned by Temasek Holdings Private Limited (Temasek), of joint control over Golden Goose Group S.p.A. (GGG), a holding company which in turns owns the entire share capital of Golden Goose S.p.A. (Golden Goose), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition consists of BidCo (a company wholly owned by GG11 S.p.A. (HoldCo), an entity indirectly wholly owned by HSG), acquiring:

a. 100% of the shares in Astrum S.a.p.A. di Astrum 4 S.r.l. & C., a company which, in turn, owns 88.05% of the share capital of GGG; and

b. the shares representing the remaining 11.95% of the share capital of GGG from PSLG Limited, pursuant to a sale and purchase agreement entered into by and among Permira VII Investment Platform Limited, Astrum 4 S.r.l., Astrum 5 S.r.l., Astrum 6 S.r.l., CEP IV Participations S.à r.l., SICAR and Carlyle Growth Investments III, on one side, and BidCo, as purchaser, on the other side.

Immediately prior to or at closing of the Acquisition, Rosa and True Light Investments P Pte. Ltd., certain managers of GGG, funds advised or managed by Permira Holdings Limited (Permira) and funds advised or managed by Carlyle will invest alongside HSG in HoldCo, and indirectly in BidCo and GGG.

Following the Acquisition, GGG will be jointly controlled by HSG and Temasek.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
6 March 2026

HSG is a venture capital and private equity firm headquartered in Hong Kong. HSG holds controlling interests in Holzweiler Items AS (Holzweiler) and Ami Paris SAS (Ami Paris). Holzweiler is a Norwegian outdoor-inspired apparel and lifestyle brand that serves international consumers via online sales and digital channels, including those in Australia. Ami Paris is a high-end French fashion house founded in 2011 that offers clothing and accessories for men and women.

Rosa is an investment holding company indirectly wholly owned by Temasek. Temasek is an investment company headquartered in Singapore and has a global portfolio covering a range of industries.

The target, Golden Goose, is an Italian-headquartered company specialising in the design, manufacture and sale of premium apparel, with a strong focus on footwear, in addition to ready-to-wear clothing and accessories. Golden Goose distributes its products through its own retail stores, wholesale channels, and its online platform, operating across Europe, the United States and Asia. Golden Goose is currently controlled by funds and affiliates managed or advised by Permira.

HSG’s portfolio companies (Holzweiler and Ami Paris) and Golden Goose both supply luxury goods (including clothing) to customers in Australia. Temasek does not control any portfolio companies that are active in the luxury fashion sector in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between HSG’s portfolio companies and Golden Goose, and no horizontal overlap between Temasek (and its controlled portfolio companies) and Golden Goose, in the supply of luxury goods

b. there are several alternative suppliers of luxury goods, including luxury fashion, footwear or accessories, to customers in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Coles Group Limited (**Coles**) is an Australian public company (ASX:COL) that primarily operates supermarkets and liquor stores. Coles also has a property development arm, Coles Group Property Developments Ltd (**CGPD**), that buys land and constructs shopping centres, supermarkets and liquor outlets to then sell to third party buyers. Powercor Australia Ltd (**Powercor**) is the electricity distribution company responsible for electricity distribution in the Yarrawonga area.

CGPD is finalising fit-out works for a new Coles supermarket and Liquorland at Kaiela Business Park, 14 Frank Drive, Yarrawonga VIC 3730 (**Coles Yarrawonga**), scheduled to open on 27 May 2026. Powercor proposes to sell CGPD a small parcel of land (approximately 52 square metres) located adjacent to Coles Yarrawonga, at (Res 2) Frank Drive, Yarrawonga, which is used as an electricity substation.

Powercor will retain ownership of all substation infrastructure. Upon settlement, CGPD will grant Powercor a long-term lease to operate the substation (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition is being undertaken at Powercor’s request to facilitate the provision of permanent power to Coles Yarrawonga.

determination_table_content.details: Coles Group Property Developments Ltd (CGPD) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a parcel of land at (Res 2) Frank Drive, Yarrawonga VIC (target land) from Powercor Australia Ltd (Powercor), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, CGPD, is a property development arm of Coles Group Limited (Coles). Coles is an Australian public company (ASX:COL) that primarily operates supermarkets and liquor stores. CGPD buys land and constructs shopping centres, supermarkets and liquor outlets to then sell to third party buyers.

The vendor, Powercor, is the electricity distribution company responsible for electricity distribution in the Yarrawonga area.

The target land is located in Yarrawonga, VIC and is currently used as an electricity substation. The target land is adjacent to a new Coles supermarket and Liquorland store being opened at Kaiela Business Park, 14 Frank Drive, Yarrawonga VIC 3730 (Coles Yarrawonga).

The target land would be used to provide Coles Yarrawonga with a permanent electricity supply.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the target land is not currently used for grocery retailing and will be used to supply permanent electricity to Coles Yarrawonga

b. Coles does not have an existing supermarket or liquor store in Yarrawonga

c. there are alternative grocery retailers, including Woolworths, Aldi and IGA, and retail suppliers of packaged liquor for offsite consumption in the local area.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) being met. The ACCC does however observe that the targeted notification requirements for supermarkets determined under section 51ABQ(1) are likely to apply.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
TMX Markets Australia Pty Ltd (**TMX**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of TMX Group Limited (**TMX Group**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Middlebury Holdings Pty Limited (**Middlebury Holdings**) (including its wholly-owned subsidiaries) through a Share Purchase Agreement (the **Acquisition**).

TMX is a special purpose vehicle incorporated for the purpose of the Acquisition, and does not carry out any business operations in Australia. TMX is a subsidiary of TMX Group, a publicly traded company that operates globally and builds digital communities and analytic solutions that facilitate the funding, growth and success of businesses, traders and investors. TMX has no connected entities in Australia.

TMX Group’s key operations include the Toronto Stock Exchange (**TSX**), TSX Venture Exchange, TSX Alpha Exchange, the Canadian Depository for Securities, Montréal Exchange, Canadian Derivatives Clearing Corporation, TSX Trust, TMX Trayport, TMX Datalinx, TMX VettaFi and TMX Newsfile, which together provide listing markets, trading markets, clearing facilities, depository services, technology solutions, data products and other services to the global financial community.

TMX Group is headquartered in Toronto and operates offices across North America (Montréal, Calgary, Vancouver and New York), as well as in key international markets including London, Singapore, and Vienna.

Cboe Australia Pty Ltd (**Cboe Australia**) is a wholly owned subsidiary of Middlebury Holdings, and is an ASIC-regulated securities market operator that provides investors with access to local and global investment opportunities. It operates as an alternative exchange in Australia, and facilitates trading in a range of financial products, including ASX-listed equities, exchange-traded funds and structured products, supported by the proprietary trading technology derived from Cboe Global Markets’ international platforms.

In October 2025, ASIC approved a listing market application filed by Cboe Australia. Cboe Australia is accordingly approved to list new companies through initial public offerings and launch exchange-traded funds and structured products.

Cboe Australia Services Pty Ltd provides various technical services, including software development and network management, and corporate services to Cboe Australia.

determination_table_content.details: TMX Markets Australia Pty Ltd (TMX), a wholly-owned subsidiary of TMX Group Limited (TMX Group), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in Middlebury Holdings Pty Limited (Middlebury Holdings) (including its wholly-owned subsidiaries), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, TMX, is a special purpose vehicle incorporated for the purpose of the Acquisition, and does not carry out any business operations in Australia. TMX is a subsidiary of TMX Group, a publicly traded company that operates globally and builds digital communities and analytic solutions that facilitate the funding, growth and success of businesses, traders and investors.

TMX Group’s key operations include Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), TSX Venture Exchange, TSX Alpha Exchange, the Canadian Depository for Securities, Montréal Exchange, Canadian Derivatives Clearing Corporation, TSX Trust, TMX Trayport, TMX Datalinx, TMX VettaFi and TMX Newsfile, which together provide listing markets, trading markets, clearing facilities, depository services, technology solutions, data products and other services to the global financial community. TMX is headquartered in Toronto and operates offices across North America (Montréal, Calgary, Vancouver and New York), as well as in key international markets including London, Singapore, and Vienna.

The target, Middlebury Holdings, is the holding company for Cboe Australia Pty Ltd (Cboe Australia) and Cboe Australian Services Pty Ltd (Cboe Australian Services).

Cboe Australia is an ASIC-regulated securities market operator that provides investors with access to local and global investment opportunities. It operates as an alternative exchange in Australia, and facilitates trading in a range of financial products, including ASX-listed equities, exchange-traded funds and structured products, supported by the proprietary trading technology derived from Cboe Global Markets’ international platforms.

Cboe Australia Services provides various technical services, including software development and network management, and corporate services to Cboe Australia.

TMX and Cboe Australia supply exchange services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the Acquisition does not result in the removal of a competitor as TMX does not operate an exchange in Australia

b. there is limited overlap between TMX and Cboe Australia in the supply of exchange services in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Midea Imaging Technology (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (the **Acquirer**) proposes to indirectly acquire 77.5% of the issued share capital of Esaote S.p.A. (**Esaote**) (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition will result in Midea Group Co., Ltd. (**Midea**) owning 87.73% of the issued share capital of Esaote.

Midea is a global technology group organised into several divisions, including Smart Home, Industrial Technology, Intelligent Building Technology, Robotics & Automation, Energy Solutions, Healthcare and Smart Logistics, among others. The Acquisition concerns Midea’s Healthcare division, which Midea operates mainly through the Acquirer, and its listed affiliate Beijing Wandong Medical Technology Co., Ltd. (**Wandong**). Wandong supplies medical imaging products and services. It supplies magnetic resonance imaging (**MRI**), computed tomography, digital subtraction angiography, and digital radiography equipment, primarily in mainland China. The Acquirer also owns the business of Carestream Healthcare International Company Limited (**Carestream**). Carestream is a worldwide provider of medical imaging systems; X-ray imaging systems for non-destructive testing; and printing and film products.

Esaote is an international medical technology group that operates in the field of medical diagnostic equipment, primarily ultrasound systems, open and dedicated MRI systems designed for musculoskeletal and extremity imaging, and healthcare information technology.

determination_table_content.details:
Midea Imaging Technology (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (the Acquirer) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed indirect acquisition of 77.5% of the share capital in Esaote S.p.A (Esaote), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition will result in Midea Group Co., Ltd. (Midea) owning 87.73% of the issued share capital of Esaote.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Midea is a global technology group organised into several divisions, including Smart Home, Industrial Technology, Intelligent Building Technology, Robotics & Automation, Energy Solutions, Healthcare and Smart Logistics, among others. The Acquisition concerns Midea’s Healthcare division, which Midea operates mainly through the Acquirer, and its listed affiliate Beijing Wandong Medical Technology Co., Ltd. (Wandong).

Wandong supplies medical imaging products and services. It supplies magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), computed tomography, digital subtraction angiography, and digital radiography equipment, primarily in mainland China.

The Acquirer also owns the business of Carestream Healthcare International Company Limited (Carestream). Carestream is a worldwide provider of medical imaging systems; X-ray imaging systems for non-destructive testing; and printing and film products.

Esaote is an international medical technology group that operates in the field of medical diagnostic equipment, primarily ultrasound systems, open and dedicated MRI systems designed for musculoskeletal and extremity imaging, and healthcare information technology.

Midea and Esaote overlap in the supply of medical imaging systems and equipment in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap in the supply of medical imaging systems and equipment, and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

b. there is no horizontal overlap between the parties in the various categories of medical imaging systems and equipment products that each supply

c. there are several alternative suppliers of medical imaging systems and equipment to customers in Australia

d. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of different medical imaging systems and equipment products post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
As part of its activities, Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (**Stockland**) operates a land lease communities (**LLC**) business, which currently comprises a portfolio of 39 LLC projects nationally, including both established communities and communities under development/in planning.

Stockland proposes, as a restructure, to transfer the land and development assets from two Stockland LLC projects, Halcyon Jardin and Halcyon Evergreen (in Clyde and Clyde North in Victoria), from Stockland controlled trusts/entities to newly established special purpose trusts (the **Acquisition**):

* The Jardin and Evergreen land will be transferred to SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd as trustee for the SMLLP Jardin Trust (**Jardin Land Buyer**) and SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd as trustee for the SMLLP Evergreen Trust (**Evergreen Land Buyer**) respectively.
* The Jardin and Evergreen development rights will be granted and development 'work in progress' will be transferred to SMLLP Sub Pty Ltd as trustee of the SMLLP Development Sub Trust (**Development Buyer**).

At the time of the Acquisition, the Jardin Land Buyer, Evergreen Land Buyer and Development Buyer will be (indirectly) controlled by Stockland. As newly established trusts, the Jardin and Evergreen Land Buyers and Development Buyer have no existing activities. Stockland will, post transfers, continue to develop the Jardin and Evergreen projects.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (together Stockland) applied for a notification waiver in respect of SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd’s and SMLLP Sub Pty Ltd (Stockland Buyers) proposed acquisition of the land and development rights of the Halcyon Jardin and Halcyon Evergreen projects, currently held by Stockland LLC No.2 Pty Ltd and Stockland Land Lease Management Pty Limited (Stockland Sellers), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
13 February 2026

The acquirer, Stockland, operates a land lease communities (LLC) business, which currently comprises a portfolio of 39 LLC projects nationally, including both established communities and communities under development/in planning.

The Stockland Buyers are newly established trusts which will be (indirectly) controlled by Stockland.

The Stockland Sellers are entities indirectly, wholly owned by Stockland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. The Acquisition involves the transfer of assets between Stockland-controlled entities.

b. There is no horizontal consolidation or removal of a competitor as a result of the Acquisition.

Given the above considerations that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

The ACCC notes that it also considered the potential application of section 51ABD (internal restructures and reorganisations) of the Act.

The application states that, on the facts of this matter, including the particular entities and structures involved, it was not clear that the Acquisition fell within that provision. Given this uncertainty, the ACCC considered it appropriate to assess the notification waiver application on the basis that the acquisition provisions in the Act applied to the Acquisition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (**Premier Fresh**) proposes to purchase 100% of the shares in the following entities that are owned by Carmelo De Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**):

(a)        North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd, and

(b)        Double D Produce Pty Ltd

(collectively, the **Domenico Group**),

as well as the business, land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group.

The Domenico Group and the Vendor are involved in the growing, packing and value add processing for capsicums located in Ayr, Queensland.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum production under a marketing agreement.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities known as the Premier Fresh Group, is a horticulture marketer and supply chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable supply lines.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (Premier Fresh) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd and Double D Produce Pty Ltd (collectively, the Domenico Group), as well as the business, land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
12 March 2026

The acquirer, Premier Fresh, is a horticulture marketer and supply chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable supply lines, including capsicums. Premier Fresh, through its related entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum production under a marketing agreement.

The target, the Domenico Group, and the vendor, Carmelo De Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd, are involved in the growing, packing and value add processing for capsicums located in Queensland.

Premier Fresh and the Domenico Group overlap in the supply of capsicums in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided in the application. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including further information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:

a. the extent of horizontal competitive overlap and the nature of the vertical relationship between Premier Fresh and the Domenico Group in the production and supply of capsicums in Australia is unclear based on the application

b. the potential for foreclosure, noting that Premier Fresh is one of the largest horticultural wholesalers.

c. the geographic dimensions of the markets

d. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by alternative producers and suppliers of capsicums in Australia.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Duncan Holdco III LLC, a holding company that is ultimately controlled by SoftBank Group Corp. (Japan) (**SoftBank**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis) of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (**DigitalBridge**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition includes an indirect acquisition of all of DigitalBridge’s interests in each of the relevant general partner entities which manage DigitalBridge investment funds, through Duncan Holdco III LLC.

DigitalBridge is a dedicated, global-scale digital infrastructure investment firm that invests, directly and through its managed funds and their portfolio companies, in five key areas: (i) data centres; (ii) cell towers; (iii) fibre networks; (iv) small cells; and (v) edge infrastructure. DigitalBridge is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, United States of America with key offices in New York, Los Angeles, London, Luxembourg and Singapore. DigitalBridge is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

SoftBank is a publicly traded global investor headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, with a varied portfolio of investments and subsidiaries focused on the technology sector, many of which are minority, non-controlled investments. Companies and investments controlled by SoftBank conduct business activities globally across a wide range of sectors, including online learning, mobile and fixed-line communications services, microelectronics, transportation, software-as-a-service, internet advertising and e-commerce, banking, payment and investment, IT-platforms, artificial intelligence and mobile robots.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Duncan Holdco III LLC (Duncan Holdco 3) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis) of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (DigitalBridge) through 2 coordinated reverse subsidiary mergers, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). Duncan Holdco 3 is ultimately a subsidiary of SoftBank Group Corp. (Japan) (SoftBank).

The Acquisition involves Duncan Sub I Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Duncan Holdco 3, merging with and into DigitalBridge, with DigitalBridge surviving as a wholly owned direct subsidiary of Duncan Holdco 3. Immediately following, Duncan Sub II LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Duncan Sub I Inc., will merge with and into DigitalBridge Operating Company, LLC, with DigitalBridge Operating Company, LLC surviving as a direct subsidiary of DigitalBridge.

As a result of the Acquisition, each of DigitalBridge and DigitalBridge Operating Company, LLC will become solely controlled, indirect subsidiaries of SoftBank.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 March 2026

SoftBank is a publicly traded global investor headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, with a varied portfolio of investments and subsidiaries focused on the technology sector. Companies and investments controlled by SoftBank conduct business activities globally across a wide range of sectors, including online learning, mobile and fixed-line communications services, microelectronics, transportation, software-as-a-service, internet advertising and e-commerce, banking, payment and investment, IT platforms, artificial intelligence and mobile robots.

The acquirer, Duncan Holdco 3, is a holding company created for the purposes of the Acquisition and is ultimately a subsidiary controlled by SoftBank.

The target, DigitalBridge, is a dedicated, global-scale digital infrastructure investment firm that invests, directly and through its managed funds and their portfolio companies, in data centres, cell towers, fibre networks, small cells, and edge infrastructure.

DigitalBridge is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, United States of America with key offices in New York, Los Angeles, London, Luxembourg and Singapore. DigitalBridge is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

Both SoftBank and DigitalBridge have connected entities that supply internet connectivity services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the connected entities of SoftBank and DigitalBridge in the supply of internet connectivity services in Australia

b. SoftBank and DigitalBridge have a small presence in the supply of internet connectivity services in Australia

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of internet connectivity services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Enverus, Inc. (**Enverus**), a Texas corporation, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of SBS Optionco, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and SBS Blocker, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (collectively, with each of their direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Spatial Business Systems**).

Enverus is a global Software-as-a-Service and data provider to the energy industry. Founded in 1999, and headquartered in Austin, Texas, Enverus offers a platform that integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable energy customers. Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc.

Spatial Business Systems is a global business headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas utilities, engineering and construction firms and broadband infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and design workflows, integrate spatial and design data and improve design effectiveness.

In Australia, Spatial Business Systems provides distribution design and substation services to customers.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Enverus, Inc. (Enverus) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of SBS Optionco, LLC and SBS Blocker, LLC (collectively, with each of their direct and indirect subsidiaries, Spatial Business Systems), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Enverus, is a global Software-as-a-Service and data provider to the energy industry. Enverus was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas. Enverus offers a platform that integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable energy customers.

Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its affiliates or affiliated investment funds, Blackstone). Blackstone, Inc. is a US-based corporation, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.

The target, Spatial Business Systems, is a global business headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas utilities, engineering and construction firms, and broadband infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and design workflows, integrate spatial and design data, and improve design effectiveness. In Australia, Spatial Business Systems provides distribution design and substation design services to customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap, and limited potential vertical relationships, between Enverus or Blackstone’s controlled portfolio companies and Spatial Business Systems in the supply of software for designing electric and gas distribution networks and substations to customers in Australia

b. Spatial Business Systems has a small presence in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

mergers.merger_description:
Wentworth Capital Investment Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and subsidiaries, **Wentworth**) is an Australian asset manager based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate private credit.

Funds managed by Wentworth will acquire 100% of the hotel businesses and related assets of Novotel Darling Harbour and Ibis Darling Harbour (**Hotel Businesses**) from Pearl Hotels D 1993 Pty Limited and the leasehold interests in the land on which the Hotel Businesses operate (**Property**) from The Trust Company (Australia) Limited in its capacity as trustee of the Pearl Hotels ZC 2013 Trust.

The Hotel Businesses currently offer hotel accommodation services (and ancillary services related to the operation of a hotel) under brands associated with the Accor Group, which will continue to manage the hotels post-acquisition.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Wentworth Inn Prop TC Pty Ltd, Wentworth Inn OpCo I Pty Ltd and Wentworth Inn OpCo II Pty Ltd (the Acquirers) applied for a notification waiver in respect of their proposed acquisition of:

• 100% of the hotel businesses and related assets of Novotel Darling Harbour and Ibis Darling Harbour (Hotel Businesses) from Pearl Hotels D 1993 Pty Limited

• the leasehold interests in the land on which the Hotel Businesses operate (the Property) from The Trust Company (Australia)

Limited in its capacity as trustee of the Pearl Hotels ZC 2013 Trust as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The Acquirers are funds managed by Wentworth Capital Investment Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and subsidiaries, Wentworth). Wentworth is an Australian asset manager based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate private credit.

The Hotel Businesses offer hotel accommodation services and ancillary services related to the operation of a hotel under brands associated with the Accor Group (which will continue to manage the hotels post-Acquisition).

Wentworth states that it does not hold any interests in hotel or other short-term accommodation businesses in Australia. However, one non-controlling co-investor in the Acquirers, Mulpha Australia Limited (which is ultimately owned by Mulpha International Bhd (together, Mulpha)), has interests in the InterContinental Sydney at Circular Quay and a proposed venue in Sussex Street, Sydney.

Mulpha is an international real estate and hospitality investor headquartered in Malaysia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• there appears to be no, or a very limited, horizontal overlap between Wentworth and the Hotel Businesses

• there are alternative suppliers of short-term accommodation and ancillary services in Sydney, including in Darling Harbour

• the Accor Group will continue to operate and manage the Hotel Businesses post-Acquisition.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
