mergers.merger_description:
Gainsdale Pty Ltd, Hootie Blowfish Pty Ltd, and Counting Crows Pty Ltd, which are entities associated with Mr Matthew Turner and his family (together, the **Turner Family Entities**), propose to increase their existing shareholdings in Pedal Group Pty Ltd (**Pedal Group**) by collectively acquiring from Flight Centre Travel Group Limited (**Flight Centre**) shares representing (in aggregate) 20.32% of the shares in Pedal Group. In connection with this acquisition, Pedal Group will buy back the remaining shares held by Flight Centre in Pedal Group, representing (in aggregate) 26.68% of the shares in Pedal Group. The share buy-back and proposed acquisition of shares in Pedal Group by the Turner Family Entities (together, the **Acquisition**) are interrelated and conditional upon each other.

Upon completion of the Acquisition, the Turner Family Entities’ shareholding in Pedal Group will increase from 36.30% to 77.23%. The balance of the Pedal Group share register will continue to be held by existing and former Pedal Group employees.

Pedal Group is a retailer and/or wholesaler of bicycles, e-bikes, scooters, associated accessories and a provider of bicycle / e-bike maintenance services. In Australia, Pedal Group operates 71 “99 Bikes”-branded retail stores and the “Advance Traders” wholesale business. Pedal Group also supplies bicycle rental services in Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1)

determination_table_content.details:
Gainsdale Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of:

• the proposed acquisition by Gainsdale Pty Ltd, Hootie Blowfish Pty Ltd, and Counting Crows Pty Ltd (together, the Turner Family Entities) of an additional 20.32% of the shares (in aggregate) in Pedal Group Pty Ltd (Pedal Group) from Flight Centre Travel Group Limited (Flight Centre)

• Pedal Group’s buy-back of Flight Centre’s remaining shares in Pedal Group, representing (in aggregate) 26.68% of the shares in Pedal Group, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

Upon completion of the Acquisition, the Turner Family Entities’ shareholding in Pedal Group will increase from 36.30% to 77.23%.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
30 April 2026

The acquirers, the Turner Family Entities, are entities associated with Mr Matthew Turner and his family.

The target, Pedal Group, is a retailer and wholesaler of bicycles, e-bikes, scooters, associated accessories and a provider of bicycle / e-bike maintenance services. In Australia, Pedal Group operates 71 ‘99 Bikes’ branded retail stores and the ‘Advance Traders’ wholesale business. Pedal Group also supplies bicycle rental services in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. aside from their existing interests in Pedal Group, there are no horizontal overlaps or vertical relationships between the Turner Family Entities (including their connected entities) and Pedal Group (including its connected entities) in the retail and wholesale supply of bicycles, e-bikes, scooters, associated accessories in Australia or in the supply of bike maintenance and rental services

b. there are alternative retail and wholesale suppliers of bicycles, e-bikes, scooters and associated accessories in Australia

c. there are alternative suppliers of maintenance services and rental services in areas where Pedal Group supplies such services.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Coforge Limited (**Coforge**) proposes to acquire Encora US Holdco, Inc. and Encora Holdings Limited (together, **Encora**). As consideration, Encora Holdco Limited and AI Altius Parent (**Cayman**) Limited (together, the Sellers) propose to acquire a non-controlling, minority interest in Coforge.

Coforge is a global technology services and solutions provider based in India. Coforge offers a broad range of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers. Coforge operates across 25 countries, including Australia.

Encora is a global digital engineering services company specialising in supplying a narrow subset of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers (namely, software product development). Encora is based in the United States and operates across a number of countries globally, with a limited presence in Australia.

The Sellers are indirectly owned by private equity funds managed and/or advised by Advent International, L.P. and/or its affiliates (**Advent**).

Advent is a private equity investment firm based in the United States. Advent is focused on equity stakes and the management of investment funds in various sectors, including healthcare, industrial, technology, retail, consumer, and business and financial services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Encora Holdco Limited and AI Altius Parent (Cayman) Limited (together, the Sellers) applied for a notification waiver in respect of the proposal by:

• Coforge Limited (Coforge) to acquire 100% of the share capital in Encora US Holdco, Inc. and Encora Holdings Limited (together, Encora); and

• the Sellers to acquire a non-controlling, minority interest in Coforge as consideration, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
13 February 2026

Coforge is a global technology services and solutions provider based in India. Coforge offers a broad range of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers. Coforge operates across 25 countries, including Australia.

Encora is a global digital engineering services company specialising in supplying a narrow subset of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers (namely, software product development). Encora is based in the United States and operates across a number of countries globally, including Australia.

Coforge and Encora supply digital engineering solutions. This encompasses professional services to design, build and customise software. These services generally involve bespoke solutions that are developed by a provider for a specific enterprise customer.

The Sellers are indirectly owned by private equity funds managed and/or advised by Advent International, L.P. and/or its affiliates (Advent). Advent is a private equity investment firm based in the United States. Advent is focused on equity stakes and the management of investment funds in various sectors, including healthcare, industrial, technology, retail, consumer, and business and financial services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. While there is horizontal overlap between Coforge and Encora in the supply of digital engineering services in Australia, the merger parties’ combined share is low.

b. There are alternative suppliers of digital engineering services in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee of the Australian Retirement Trust (**ART**) is an Australian superannuation fund. ART manages retirement savings for >2.4 million members and >207,000 participating employers across Australia as at 30 June 2025. ART has limited interests in a small number of shopping centres in Sydney and New South Wales.

Scentre Group Limited (ASX: SCG) (**Scentre Group**) is an ASX-listed Australian real estate investment trust established in 2014. Scentre Group owns, develops and manages a portfolio of 42 Westfield shopping centres across Australia and New Zealand, including Westfield Sydney. Its business encompasses property investment, development, design, construction, leasing and asset management.

ART (through a wholly owned trust) has entered into an agreement with various wholly owned Scentre Group entities to acquire either:

* a 19.9% interest in certain digital advertising assets located at Westfield Sydney, and the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income; or
* only the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income
  
  (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is related to ART’s proposed acquisition of a 19.9% interest in the property trusts that own the various parcels of land that comprise Westfield Sydney, announced 23 December 2025.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
QIC TT Company Pty Ltd (QIC), an indirectly wholly owned entity of the Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee of the Australian Retirement Trust (ART), applied for a notification waiver. The application was made in respect of ART’s proposed acquisition (through a wholly owned entity) from Scentre Group Limited (Scentre Group) of:

• a 19.9% interest in certain digital advertising assets located at Westfield Sydney, and the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income; or

• only the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
20 February 2026

ART is one of Australia’s largest superannuation funds. ART manages retirement savings for more than 2.4 million members and over 207,000 participating employers across Australia as at 30 June 2025.

The vendor, Scentre Group, is an Australian real estate investment trust, and a top 30 ASX-listed stapled property group. Scentre Group owns, develops, and manages a portfolio of 42 Westfield shopping centres (including Westfield Sydney) across Australia and New Zealand. Scentre Group’s business encompasses property investment, development, design, construction, leasing, and asset management.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition (with or without the digital advertising assets) is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the Acquisition gives rise to a limited horizontal overlap in the supply of out-of-home advertising, gift cards, and other ancillary or incidental services provided at shopping centres

b. any increment to ART’s share of revenue in the above services arising from the Acquisition is low

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of advertising opportunities, gift cards, and shopping centre ancillary services.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Lecta Limited and Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. have agreed with certain creditors of the Lecta Group to restructure the group’s financial indebtedness. As part of that restructure, funds managed by subsidiaries of Apollo Global Management (together with its indirect subsidiaries managing the investment funds, **Apollo**) will indirectly acquire sole control over Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. (together with its subsidiaries, **Lecta**) by way of a debt-to-equity swap (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will be effected by a new holding company, Reed Bidco Limited (**BidCo**), acquiring all of the common equity in Lecta and issuing new equity to the funds managed by Apollo, among other creditors of Lecta.

Apollo is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City, New York. Apollo offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors.

Lecta is a manufacturer and supplier of specialty papers for labels, flexible packaging and other high value-added print segments, as well as coated and uncoated paper for publishing. Lecta has its main operating offices in Barcelona, Spain and operates several production facilities in Europe, including manufacturing facilities producing pulp, base paper, specialty papers, self-adhesive papers and coated woodfree paper, with seven mills across Spain, France and Italy and a self-adhesive converting centre in Germany.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Lecta Limited and Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. have agreed with certain creditors of the Lecta Group to restructure the group’s financial indebtedness. As part of that restructure, funds managed by subsidiaries of Apollo Global Management (together with its indirect subsidiaries managing the investment funds, Apollo) will indirectly acquire sole control over Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. (together with its subsidiaries, Lecta) by way of a debt-to-equity swap (the Acquisition)

The Acquisition will be effected by a new holding company, Reed Bidco Limited (BidCo), acquiring all of the common equity in Lecta and issuing new equity to the funds managed by Apollo, among other creditors of Lecta.

Apollo, Apollo Capital Management L.P. and BidCo jointly applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
19 February 2026

The acquirer, Apollo, is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City, New York. Apollo offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors.

The target, Lecta, is a manufacturer and supplier of specialty papers for labels, flexible packaging and other high value-added print segments, as well as coated and uncoated paper for publishing.

Lecta has its main operating offices in Barcelona, Spain and operates several production facilities in Europe, including manufacturing facilities producing pulp, base paper, specialty papers, self-adhesive papers and coated woodfree paper, with seven mills across Spain, France and Italy and a self-adhesive converting centre in Germany.

Within Australia, Lecta's activities include distributing two types of paper products via third-party distributors: self-adhesive labelstock and metallised paper.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap between Apollo’s portfolio of controlled companies and Lecta in the supply of paper products in Australia.

b. There is no vertical relationship in Australia between Apollo’s portfolio of controlled companies and Lecta.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquirer, Global Garella, S.L., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Colliers International Group Inc., (**CIGI**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Ayesa Engineering, S.A.U. (**Ayesa**) from Ayesa Inversiones, S.L.U.

CIGI is a global, diversified professional services and investment management company that operates through three divisions (Real Estate Services, Engineering and Investment Management) in over 70 countries. In Australia, its engineering consultancy services include engineering design services and site supervision and project management services, in particular, civil-based engineering consultancy services for projects in the property and buildings and water sectors.

Ayesa provides engineering consultancy services, including engineering design and site supervision and project management services, in various countries worldwide. In Australia, Ayesa supplies only engineering design services, in particular, mechanical, electrical and plumbing design services for projects in the buildings and cities, industry, and transportation sectors.

In Australia, CIGI and Ayesa overlap in the supply of engineering consultancy services. CIGI supplies both engineering design services and site supervision and project management services, in particular, civil-based engineering consultancy services for projects in the buildings and cities and water segments. While Ayesa supplies site supervision and project management services in other countries, in Australia, its engineering consultancy services are limited to engineering design services, in particular, mechanical, electrical and plumbing design services in the buildings and cities, industry, and transportation sectors.

The Sellers are part of MA Financial’s Brunswick Fund, a single asset investment fund.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of products and services available at hotels and hospitality venues, and retail suppliers of packaged liquor for offsite consumption.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

The ACCC notes that it also considered the potential application of section 51ABD (internal restructures and reorganisations) of the Act.

The application noted the potential application of this provision and the ACCC considered that the Acquisition might fall within it.

However, based on facts of this matter and having regard to the entities and structures involved, the ACCC considered it was appropriate to make a determination on the basis that the acquisition provisions in the Act applied to the Acquisition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (**AZ Next Generation Advisory**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in BMM Partners Pty Ltd, trading as Intend Financial (**Intend Financial**).

AZ Next Generation Advisory is an Australian professional services advisory company established in 2014 that owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting businesses with operations and customers across Australia, including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.

Intend Financial is a financial planning business established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland (Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.

determination_table_content.details: AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (AZ Next Generation Advisory) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in BMM Partners Pty Ltd (Intend Financial), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, AZ Next Generation Advisory, is an Australian professional services advisory company established in 2014 that owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting businesses with operations and customers across Australia, including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.

AZ Next Generation Advisory is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty Ltd whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management LP (Oaktree) and Azimut Holding S.p.a. (Azimut). Oaktree is a global investment management firm and Azimut is a global asset and wealth management business.

The target, Intend Financial, is a financial planning business established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland (Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.

AZ Next Generation Advisory and Intend Financial both supply financial planning services, which include wealth management, retirement planning, insurance, investment, estate and succession, debt management, business planning and superannuation advisory services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between AZ Next Generation Advisory and Intend Financial in the supply of financial planning services in Northern Tasmania, Gold Coast, Greater Melbourne and across Australia

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial planning services in the relevant geographic areas is estimated to be low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of financial planning services in Northern Tasmania, Gold Coast, Greater Melbourne and across Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of the Telegraph Road Clinic (**TRC**) in Bracken Ridge, Queensland, through a business and assets acquisition agreement (the **Acquisition**).

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 68 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria, but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care clinics within 10km of TRC in Bracken Ridge, Queensland. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital (**Genesis**), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s investments also include Holdsworth House Medical Group (**Holdsworth House**), which operates a general medical practice in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane and another general medical practice in Sydney which is co-located with a medical research centre, specialist medical clinic and dentistry clinic.

The target, TRC, is a family medical practice providing comprehensive general practice medical care from its clinic located in the Bracken Ridge Plaza, Shop 26, 250 Telegraph Road, Bracken Ridge, Queensland, and home visits to nearby patients (within a 3km radius of the clinic). TRC is owned and operated by Telegraph Road Medical Services Pty Ltd as trustee for Telegraph Road Medical Services Unit Trust.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Ochre Health Pty Limited (Ochre Health) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the business and all of the assets of Telegraph Road Clinic (TRC) in Bracken Ridge, Queensland, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 68 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital (Genesis), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s investments also include Holdsworth House Medical Group (Holdsworth House), which operates a general medical practice in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane and another general medical practice in Sydney which is co-located with a medical research centre, specialist medical clinic and dentistry clinic.

The target, TRC, is a family medical practice providing comprehensive general practice medical care from its clinic located in the Bracken Ridge Plaza, Shop 26, 250 Telegraph Road, Bracken Ridge, Queensland, and home visits to nearby patients (within a 3km radius of the clinic). TRC is owned and operated by Telegraph Road Medical Services Pty Ltd as trustee for Telegraph Road Medical Services Unit Trust.

Ochre Health, Genesis and TRC supply general practice medical services in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no overlap in the supply of general practice medical services in the local Bracken Ridge area in Queensland and a small horizontal overlap between Ochre Health, Genesis and TRC in the broader Brisbane area

b. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical services near Bracken Ridge, Queensland.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Wipro Networks Pte Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Wipro Limited (**Wipro**), proposes to acquire all the shares in Mindsprint Pte Limited (**Mindsprint**).

Wipro will not acquire Mindsprint's current 51% interest in Mindsprint Pty Ltd (**Mindsprint Australia**), a joint venture in Australia.

Wipro is a global technology, consulting and outsourcing company, with over 230,000 employees serving more than 1,400 clients across 175 cities. Wipro maintains long-standing relationships across key sectors including financial services, healthcare, government, retail, consumer goods, technology, manufacturing, energy and utilities. Its business is structured around four global business lines: Technology Services, Business Process Services, Consulting Services and Engineering.

Mindsprint offers information technology and related services for the food & agritech, manufacturing, retail and consumer packaged goods industries. It offers solutions in consulting, enterprise applications, digital engineering, data analytics, cloud and infrastructure services, cybersecurity, intelligent enterprise operations and business process outsourcing.

The services offered by Wipro and Mindsprint overlap in relation to technology services in particular, cloud, cybersecurity, business process services and consulting services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Wipro Networks Pte Ltd, a subsidiary of Wipro Limited (Wipro), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Mindsprint Pte Limited (Mindsprint), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition does not include Wipro acquiring Mindsprint's current 51% interest in Mindsprint Pty Ltd (Mindsprint Australia), a joint venture with XL Tech in Australia.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 April 2026

Wipro is an Indian global technology, consulting and outsourcing company, with over 230,000 employees serving more than 1,400 clients across 175 cities. Wipro maintains relationships across key sectors including financial services, healthcare, government, retail, consumer goods, technology, manufacturing, energy and utilities. Its business is structured around four global business lines: Technology Services, Business Process Services, Consulting Services and Engineering.

Mindsprint is a Singapore-based technology consultancy that offers information technology and related services for the food & agritech, manufacturing, retail and consumer packaged goods. It offers solutions in consulting, enterprise applications, digital engineering, data analytics, cloud and infrastructure services, cybersecurity, intelligent enterprise operations and business process outsourcing.

Both Wipro and Mindsprint offer information technology solutions (including cloud and cybersecurity), business and consulting services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• a. Wipro is not acquiring Mindsprint’s interest in Mindsprint Australia and, beyond the joint venture, Mindsprint has a small presence in Australia

b. there are alternative providers of information technology, business and consulting services in Australia, and the combined share in the supply of these services is estimated to be low.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Carlyle Group Inc. (**Carlyle**) is a global investment firm headquartered in Washington DC, USA, with assets under management across three business segments: (i) Global Private Equity (including corporate private equity and real estate funds); (ii) Global Credit (including liquid credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and (iii) Carlyle AlpInvest (including primary, secondary, and co-investments, commingled funds, and separately managed accounts).

Peloton Computer Enterprises Ltd. (**Peloton**) is a global supplier of software predominantly used by exploration and production (**E&P**) operators in the upstream oil and gas industry. E&P is an early phase in the oil and gas industry concerned with locating and extracting raw materials.

Carlyle, via its controlled investment vehicle (CG Panther Parentco, L.P., **Panther**), proposes to acquire non-voting and non-convertible preferred equity in Peloton, as well as warrants that (if exercised) would provide Carlyle with up to c. 14% of the fully-diluted equity in Peloton (pro forma for the transaction). Carlyle will ultimately (via Panther) hold only (i) preferred equity and (ii) warrants amounting up to c. 14% holding of common equity interests in Peloton on a lookthrough basis.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CG Panther Parentco, L.P. (Panther), which is a controlled investment vehicle of The Carlyle Group Inc. (Carlyle), applied for a notification waiver in respect of Carlyle’s proposed acquisition of:

a. non-voting and non-convertible preferred equity in Peloton Computer Enterprises Ltd. (Peloton), and

b. warrants that (if exercised) would provide Carlyle with up to c. 14% of the fully-diluted equity in Peloton (pro forma for the transaction), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
30 January 2026

The acquirer is Carlyle, a global investment firm headquartered in Washington DC, USA. Carlyle has assets under management across three business segments: Global Private Equity (including corporate private equity and real estate funds); Global Credit (including liquid credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and Carlyle AlpInvest (including primary, secondary, and co-investments, commingled funds, and separately managed accounts).

The target, Peloton, is a global supplier of software predominantly used by exploration and production operators in the upstream oil and gas industry.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant, including in the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. The Acquisition does not appear likely to result in any horizontal overlaps between Carlyle (including its connected entities) and Peloton in Australia.

b. There appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.

c. Peloton appears to have a relatively small presence in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Pty Ltd (**H-E Mining**) intends to enter into an agreement for lease with ATB Morton Pty Ltd (**ATB Morton**) in respect of land located at 88 Michelmore Street, Paget, Mackay, Queensland (the **Property**) and improvements to be constructed by ATB Morton.

ATB Morton will acquire and construct a purpose-built industrial warehouse and workshop facility on the Property. ATB Morton will then lease the Property and improvements constructed by ATB Morton to H-E Mining.

The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling Division Pty Ltd (**H-E Cooling**).

HEP Australia Holdings Pty Ltd is the parent entity of H-E Mining and H-E Cooling. These companies are providers of parts, engineered components, and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and related industries, supporting both surface and underground operations as well as crushing and materials processing applications.

ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Pty Ltd (H-E Mining) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed agreement for lease with ATB Morton Pty Ltd (ATB Morton) in respect of land located at 88 Michelmore Street, Paget, Mackay, Queensland (the Property) and improvements to be constructed by ATB Morton, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
12 February 2026

The acquirer, H-E Mining, supplies parts, engineered components, and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and related industries, supporting both surface and underground operations as well as crushing and materials processing applications. H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling Division Pty Ltd (H-E Cooling) provides cooling and heat-transfer solutions for mining and heavy industrial equipment. HEP Australia Holdings Pty Ltd is the parent entity of H-E Mining and H-E Cooling.

ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer, and will be the landlord of the Property. ATB Morton will construct a purpose-built industrial warehouse and workshop facility on the Property. ATB Morton will then lease the Property and improvements constructed by ATB Morton to H-E Mining.

The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Cooling, and would be an input to their operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application and publicly available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal competitive overlap.

b. There appear to be alternative sites available for H-E Mining’s and H-E Cooling’s competitors.

c. The Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or prevent competition from rivals.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Telstra Limited (**Telstra**) is a leading Australian telecommunications and technology company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network, cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and managed/professional services.

Infosys Technologies Limited (**Infosys**) is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider. It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology, engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint, with operations across 59 countries.

Under an agreement, Telstra will outsource certain business functions to Infosys and Infosys will transform Telstra’s enterprise IT stack in respect of underlying carriage services supplied to certain Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers. As part of the agreement, Telstra and Infosys will acquire rights in relation to certain intellectual property.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Telstra Limited (Telstra) applied for a notification waiver in respect of a proposed agreement with Infosys Technologies Limited (Infosys), to the extent it involves an acquisition of assets and/or legal or equitable rights that are not property (the Acquisition). The agreement provides for Telstra to outsource certain business functions to Infosys and for Infosys to transform its Enterprise IT stack in respect of underlying carriage services provided to certain Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application. The Acquisition includes Telstra and Infosys acquiring rights in relation to certain intellectual property.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
19 March 2026

Telstra is an Australian telecommunications and technology company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network, cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and managed/professional services.

Infosys is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider.

It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology, engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint, with operations across 59 countries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the Acquisition is not likely to result in a material increase in concentration in the supply of the services offered by Telstra and Infosys in Australia

b. there is a low risk of foreclosure resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply. While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

The ACCC notes that it also considered whether the Acquisition fell within the scope of sections 51ABU and 51ABV of the Act. Based on the information provided by the parties and the facts of this matter, in particular the assets and/or legal or equitable rights involved, including intellectual property rights, the ACCC considered it appropriate to make a determination in relation to the notification waiver application.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (**Hall & McLean**) proposes to acquire a 100% freehold interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp, QLD 4350 (the **Property**), from Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**) (the **Acquisition**).

Hall & McLean is a 50:50 joint venture between Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (**Ellerslie**) and the McLean family (through Alpair Pty Ltd).

Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based on the Darling Downs in Queensland

Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken egg production sector.

Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40% held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.

Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen, an egg distributer.

The Property is located ~13km outside of Toowoomba in Queensland and currently used by Ellerslie as an egg grading and cold storage site.

Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its egg production business. Prior to Ellerslie entering into that lease, the Property was vacant for a period of approximately 5 years.

Following completion of the Acquisition, Ellerslie will continue to use the Property for egg grading and cold storage, albeit as owner rather than lessee.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (Hall & McLean) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 100% freehold interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp QLD 4350 (the Property), from Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the Vendor), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
23 March 2026

The acquirer, Hall & McLean, is a 50:50 joint venture between Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (Ellerslie) and the McLean family (through Alpair Pty Ltd).

Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based in the Darling Downs region in Queensland.

Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken egg production sector. Ellerslie layer farms produce caged, cage free, free range, pasture raised and organic shell eggs.

Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40% held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.

Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen, an egg distributor.

Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its egg production business.

The Vendor does not operate any egg farming or production business in Australia.

The Property would be an input into the operations of Ellerslie.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the Acquisition is an acquisition of land by an egg producer from a party who is not an egg producer

b. Ellerslie has been using the land as a lessee for 5 years and intends to continue using the land for the same purposes, but as an owner

c. there appear to be alternative sites available for Ellerslie’s and the McLean family’s competitors In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: PT Asian Bulk Logistics – Engage Marine

other_parties.name: AUSTRALIAN GLOBAL MARINE AND LOGISTICS PTY LTD

mergers.merger_description:
PT Asian Bulk Logistics, through its Australian subsidiary, Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine TopCo Pty Ltd (**Engage Marine**).

Engage Marine provides terminal and harbour towage including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and port services).

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.

Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting transhipment and logistics operations.

PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates two additional subsidiary companies in Australia – One Rail Australia and SCF Containers.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of PT Asian Bulk Logistics, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine TopCo Pty Ltd (Engage Marine), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
19 March 2026

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.

Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting transhipment and logistics operations. Transhipment Services Australia primarily services Australian mining companies in northern Queensland.

PT Asian Bulk Logistics also operates two additional subsidiary companies in Australia, One Rail Australia and SCF Containers. One Rail Australia is an Australian rail-based freight and logistics company specializing in coal, bulk, and intermodal transport, primarily operating in New South Wales and Queensland.

The target, Engage Marine, provides terminal and harbour towage including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and port services). Engage Marine services mining companies, port authorities, terminal operators, and vessels directly, at major ports around Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, including the parties’ revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:

a. the extent of any potential horizontal overlap and/or complementary relationship between PT Asian Bulk Logistics (including its subsidiaries) and Engage Marine in the supply of marine services, transhipment services, port logistics, and freight and logistics, in Australia

b. the relevant geographic dimension of the supply of PT Asian Bulk Logistics’ and Engage Marine’s services in Australia

c. the nature and extent of PT Asian Bulk Logistics’ and Engage Marine’s supply of services to mining companies in Australia

d. the extent to which the parties are likely to face competition from alternative suppliers of marine services, transhipment services, and port logistics in Australia.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Herron Todd White – CJA Lee Property Valuers
mergers.merger_description:
Herron Todd White (Bidco) Pty Ltd (**HTW Bidco**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in CJA Lee Property Pty Ltd (**CJA Lee Property**) from C McMahon Enterprises Pty Ltd as trustee for the C&N McMahon Family Trust (**C McMahon Enterprises**) (50%) and T.J. Shaw Pty Ltd as trustee for the T.J. Shaw Family Trust (**T.J. Shaw**) (50%) (the **Acquisition**). As part of the Acquisition, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw will be issued shares in the ultimate parent company of HTW Bidco – Herron Todd White (Holdings) Pty Ltd (**HTW Holdings**). Post-completion, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw will own 0.15% of the shares in HTW Holdings.

HTW Holdings and its fully owned subsidiaries (**HTW Group**) provide property valuation and property advisory services in Australia. HTW Group primarily provides residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

HTW Group is majority-owned by funds managed by QPE No. 7 Management Pty Ltd, an entity within the Quadrant Private Equity group (**QPE**). Apart from HTW Group, QPE is not involved in the supply of property valuation and property advisory services in Australia.

CJA Lee Property is an independent advisory firm that provides property valuations, property advisory, and energy advisory services in the Gippsland / south-east Victoria area. Historically, CJA Lee Property has predominantly supplied residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw are holding companies of CJA Lee Property and, apart from CJA Lee Property, do not operate any other businesses.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Herron Todd White (Bidco) Pty Ltd (HTW Bidco) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in CJA Lee Property Pty Ltd (CJA Lee Property) from C McMahon Enterprises Pty Ltd as trustee for the C&N McMahon Family Trust (C McMahon Enterprises) and T.J. Shaw Pty Ltd as trustee for the T.J. Shaw Family Trust (T.J. Shaw), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

As part of the Acquisition, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw will be issued shares in Herron Todd White (Holdings) Pty Ltd. Post-completion, they will own 0.15% of the shares in that entity.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The acquirer, HTW Bidco, is a subsidiary of Herron Todd White (Holdings) Pty Ltd (together with its fully owned subsidiaries, HTW Group). The HTW Group provides property valuation and property advisory services in Australia. HTW Group primarily provides residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

HTW Group is majority-owned by funds managed by QPE No. 7 Management Pty Ltd, an entity within the Quadrant Private Equity group (QPE). Apart from HTW Group, QPE is not involved in the supply of property valuation and property advisory services in Australia.

CJA Lee Property is an independent advisory firm that provides property valuations, property advisory, and energy advisory services in the Gippsland/south-east Victoria area. Historically, CJA Lee Property has predominantly supplied residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw are holding companies of CJA Lee Property and, apart from CJA Lee Property, do not operate any other businesses.

HTW Group and CJA Lee Property both supply physical and electronic property valuation and advisory services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, including HTW Group’s revenue in Australia and the cumulative Australian revenue of CJA Lee Property and from acquisitions in the past three years, the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met.

Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:

a. there is a material horizontal competitive overlap between HTW Group and CJA Lee Property in the supply of physical property valuation and advisory services in the Gippsland region of Victoria

b. the merger parties’ combined share in the supply of physical property valuation and advisory services to customers in the Gippsland region appears to be substantial

c. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by alternative suppliers of physical property valuation and advisory services in the Gippsland region is uncertain.

The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant consideration through the notification process, including via consultation with third parties.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Goldcup 39483 AB (under name change to Prometheus BidCo AB), a company ultimately controlled by IK Partners (**IK Partners**), intends to acquire all shares in SELATEK Holding AB and its subsidiaries (together, **Selatek**) through a share sale.

IK Partners is a European private equity firm focused on investing primarily across Northern Continental Europe. It has eleven current funds under management, and its portfolio companies operate across a range of different business sectors.

Selatek is a Swedish company that operates across three core business areas: Electrical, Automation and Security. Selatek’s subsidiaries are active primarily as consultants, installers and suppliers within the electrical engineering and building services sector, as well as in the construction, servicing and installation of automation and security solutions. Selatek derives Australian revenue through one subsidiary, Deltatec AB (**Deltatec**), which is a company incorporated in Sweden. In Australia, Deltatec supplies limited programming services of control systems involving water and sanitation engineering services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Goldcup 39483 AB (under name change to Prometheus BidCo AB), a company controlled by IK Partners (IK Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the shares in SELATEK Holding AB and its subsidiaries (Selatek), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, IK Partners, is a European private equity firm which invests primarily across Northern Continental Europe. Its portfolio companies operate across a range of different business sectors. The Wendel Group, a French investment firm, has a majority shareholding in IK Partners.

The target, Selatek, is a Swedish company that operates across three core business areas: Electrical, Automation and Security. Selatek’s subsidiaries are active primarily as consultants, installers and suppliers within the electrical engineering and building services sector, as well as in the construction, servicing and installation of automation and security solutions. Selatek’s only subsidiary with sales to Australia is Deltatec AB (Deltatec). Deltatec is a Swedish company which supplies limited programming services of control systems involving water and sanitation engineering services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no or limited horizontal overlap and no vertical relationship between IK Partners and Selatek (including their connected entities) in Australia

b. Selatek has a small presence in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Prime Property Fund Asia Limited Partnership (**PRIME Asia**) proposes to acquire units in the Stockland Sienna Wood Retail No. 2 Trust and the Stockland Providence Retail Trust (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is taking place as part of arrangements pursuant to which PRIME Asia as an institutional investor will provide capital for the development, operation and management of retail town centre assets (including relevantly those located in Sienna Wood in Western Australia and Providence in Queensland) by Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (**Stockland**).

PRIME Asia is a fund advised by Morgan Stanley Real Estate Investing. It is an open-ended commingled real estate investment fund with an investment mandate to acquire institutional-quality real estate investments across the Asia region, including in Australia.

As part of its activities, Stockland owns and operates a portfolio of retail town centres.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Prime Property Fund Asia Limited Partnership (PRIME Asia) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 49.9% of the units in the Stockland Sienna Wood Retail No. 2 Trust (Sienna Wood Trust) and 70.1% of the units in the Stockland Providence Retail Trust (Providence Trust), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition is taking place as part of arrangements pursuant to which PRIME Asia as an institutional investor will provide capital for the development, operation and management of retail town centre assets (including relevantly those located in Sienna Wood in Western Australia and Providence in Queensland) by Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (Stockland).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, PRIME Asia, is a fund advised by Morgan Stanley Real Estate Investing. It is an open-ended commingled real estate investment fund with an investment mandate to acquire institutional-quality real estate investments across the Asia region, including in Australia.

The Sienna Wood and Providence sites are retail town centres within master planned communities located in South East Perth, Western Australia, and South West Brisbane, Queensland, respectively, that are currently indirectly wholly owned by Stockland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between any existing interests of PRIME Asia (and its connected entities) in the local regions and the Sienna Wood and Providence retail town centres.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
