mergers.merger_description:
KK Group Invest A/S (**KK Group Invest**) plans to acquire 100% of the share capital of PowerCon Holding ApS and its wholly-owned subsidiaries PowerCon A/S and PowerCon USA Inc (collectively, **PowerCon**), by way of share transfer (the **Acquisition**).

PowerCon is a group of companies headquartered in Denmark that supply infrastructure solutions for shore power supply for use by ships at berth in ports globally, including in Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, Spain, Australia and the USA. In Australia, PowerCon supplies a shore power supply system for the Port Authority of New South Wales.

KK Group Invest is a Danish-registered global technology company whose core business is the development, production, sale and servicing of power electronics system solutions and technology for onshore and offshore wind turbines. In Australia, KK Group Invest supplies mostly monitoring and service products to customers based in Australia.

KK Group Invest is part of the A.P. Moller Group. A.P. Møller Holding A/S is the parent company of this group and is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Danish commercial foundation A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine Mc-Kinney Møllers Fond til almene Formaal.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: KK Group Invest A/S (KK Group Invest) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital of PowerCon Holding ApS, and its subsidiaries PowerCon A/S and PowerCon USA Inc (together, PowerCon), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, KK Group Invest, is a Danish-registered global technology company whose core business is the development, production, sale and servicing of power electronics system solutions and technology for onshore and offshore wind turbines. In Australia, KK Group Invest supplies mostly monitoring and service products to customers based in Australia.

KK Group Invest is part of the A.P. Moller Group. Other entities within the A.P Moller Group include Svitzer, a provider of global towage services, and A.P. Moller-Maersk A/S (APMM), a container liner shipping, container terminal, port operations, and port services provider.

The target, PowerCon, is a group of companies headquartered in Denmark that supply infrastructure solutions for shore power supply for use by ships at berth in ports globally, including in Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, Spain, Australia and the USA. In Australia, PowerCon supplies a shore power supply system for the Port Authority of New South Wales for use in the White Bay Cruise Terminal in Sydney.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between KK Group Invest (or Svitzer and APMM) and PowerCon in the supply of shore power supply systems in Australia

b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of maritime services post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Albany Motors Pty Ltd (**Albany Motors**), a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd (**Regent Motors Group**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (**Esperance Motor Group**) from Buverie Pty Ltd as trustee for the Schwarzback Family Trust.

Esperance Motor Group operates a Toyota and Ford dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and aftersales services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Luke Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Albany Motors Pty Ltd (Albany Motors) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital of Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (Esperance Motor Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Albany Motors, is a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd (Regent Motors Group). Regent Motors Group owns and operates franchised motor vehicle dealerships across Western Australia and Northern Territory, and supplies new and used motor vehicles (including Toyota, Ford, Hyundai, Volkswagen, BMW, MG, Mini, Isuzu, Renault, GMSV, Suzuki and Subaru vehicles), plus after sale services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet sales/service, and finance and insurance facilitation.

The target, Esperance Motor Group, operates a Toyota and Ford dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and after sale services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant, including in the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap between Regent Motors Group and Esperance Motor Group in the supply of new and used motor vehicles and after sale services in Esperance and the surrounding areas.

b. There is a potential horizontal overlap between Regent Motors Group and Esperance Motor Group in the broader area of southern WA. If Perth is excluded from this area, there are independent alternatives in Albany, Kalgoorlie, Katanning and Narrogin. If Perth is included in this area, the merged parties would likely face competition from numerous additional alternative dealerships in Perth.

c. Any increment to Regent Motors Group share of the supply of Toyota and Ford vehicles in Western Australia is small.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Delinea Inc. (**Delinea**), a company ultimately controlled by funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (**TPG**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in StrongDM, Inc (**StrongDM**).

TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.

Delinea is a software business specialising in solutions for identity security through its identity platform that enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond to threats in real-time.

StrongDM is a provider of just-in-time based Privileged Access Management (**PAM**) software primarily deployed and operated in cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific identify for a specific purpose.

Delinea and StrongDM supply software focused on validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (**IAM**) software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical access.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Delinea Inc. (Delinea) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in StrongDM, Inc. (StrongDM), through a merger with, Spartan Merger Sub, Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Delinea, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
3 February 2026

The acquirer, Delinea, is a software business specialising in solutions for identity security through its identity platform that enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond to threats in real-time. Delinea is a company ultimately controlled by funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (TPG). TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.

The target, StrongDM, delivers just-in-time based privileged access management (PAM) software primarily deployed and operated in cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific identify for a specific purpose.

Delinea and StrongDM overlap in the supply of software focused on validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (IAM) software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical access.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, public information and other industry information held by the ACCC, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information outlined above, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. StrongDM has a very small presence in Australia.

b. There are numerous alternative suppliers of IAM or PAM software in Australia and globally.

c. While there is horizontal overlap between the software supplied by Delinea and StrongDM, the increase in share of supply in Australia arising from the Acquisition would be very small.

d. Apart from Delinea, no horizontal overlap has been identified between any of TPG’s other connected entities and StrongDM.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Limited (**AVC**) proposes to acquire a leasehold (via lease assignment), the benefit of a liquor licence and certain plant and equipment (with certain exclusions) of Quay Restaurant (**Quay**) from Q.R.A. Pty Limited (the **Acquisition**). The Vendor will not transfer the existing business of Quay to AVC, only the specific assets mentioned above.   

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.   

Quay is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy 6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay Restaurant offers fine dining with views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited (Australian Venue Co), via AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a leasehold, benefit of a liquor licence and certain assets of Quay Restaurant (Quay), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
9 February 2026

The acquirer, Australian Venue Co, operates licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. Their venues are individually branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The target, Quay, is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy 6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay Restaurant is a contemporary restaurant that offers fine dining with views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge and plans to cease trading in mid-February 2026.

Australian Venue Co, through its venue The Squire’s Landing, and Quay both supply retail hospitality restaurants in the same building in the Overseas Passenger Terminal.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• While there is horizontal overlap between Quay and Australian Venue Co, the combined share of the parties in the supply of retail hospitality restaurants in the Circular Quay area of Sydney is low.

• The presence of alternative suppliers of retail hospitality restaurants in proximity to the Circular Quay area of Sydney.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met. Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Five V Capital Pty Ltd (**Five V Capital**) is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. Five V Capital manages a number of Five V funds, including Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund. Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund are investment funds that pursue a strategy of investing in private mid-market businesses.

Five V Capital and an incorporated company that is wholly owned by Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, Overwatch BidCo Pty Ltd, propose to acquire the majority of the issued shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the **FTP Group**) from the existing shareholders of FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

FTP Group is based in Australia, Canada and New Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’, ‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’.

FTP Solutions is a provider of integrated network and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry. Its flagship product, ‘IMS’, is a hardware agnostic software tool designed to monitor and visualise the network and operations of mine sites in real-time. IMS allows its users to quickly identify and resolve network issues that could result in operational downtime.

Faarm and Yarn Mesh operate in the agricultural sector. Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data (e.g. water usage, fertiliser, soil quality, weather and livestock metrics) into a single interface. Yarn Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, Five V Capital Pty Ltd and Five V Horizons Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of the majority of the issued shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the FTP Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, is indirectly wholly owned by Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, investment funds managed by Five V Capital Pty Ltd (Five V Capital). Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund pursue strategies of investing in private mid-market businesses. Five V Capital is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. It manages a number of Five V funds which have investments in a wide range of businesses and industries.

The target, FTP Group, is based in Australia, Canada and New Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’, ‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’. FTP Solutions provides integrated network and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry.

Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data into a single interface to enable farmers to monitor farms. Yarn Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.

A connected entity of Five V Capital, Beacon TopCo Pty Ltd trading as Digital Matter (Digital Matter), supplies IoT hardware solutions and device management software that enable long-term asset tracking, sensor monitoring and telematics applications. Digital Matter’s solutions can be used in several settings, including in the agricultural setting, for example for remote sensor monitoring, environmental monitoring, and water monitoring.

Broadly, Digital Matter and FTP Group’s Faarm business (Faarm) both supply solutions that facilitate remote monitoring for use in agricultural settings. Digital Matter supplies hardware devices (with some associated software such as device management software), whereas Faarm offers a platform that integrates with third party hardware.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between FTP Group and Five V Capital and its connected entities.

b. There are several other suppliers of agricultural monitoring solutions and of IoT hardware solutions and device management software in Australia.

c. Faarm has a relatively small presence in Australia.

d. While there is a potential complementary relationship between Digital Matter and Faarm in the supply of monitoring solutions for use in agricultural settings, there is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct and/or bundling or tying of products post-Acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (**Brisbane Airport Corporation**) operates, manages, and develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and facilitates commercial/retail/industrial activities through the leasing of the airport land.

Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (**GOZ**) is an ASX-listed real estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial properties which it leases to commercial tenants. Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (**Growthpoint**) holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as lessor).

Brisbane Airport Corporation has entered into a Contract for the Sale and Purchase of Commercial Real Estate to acquire from Growthpoint its interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (Brisbane Airport Corporation) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition from Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (Growthpoint) of Growthpoint’s interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
8 April 2026

The acquirer, Brisbane Airport Corporation, operates, manages, and develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and facilitates commercial, retail and industrial activity through the leasing of the airport land.

Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (GOZ) is an ASX-listed real estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial properties which it leases to commercial tenants.

Growthpoint holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport (the Property) under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as lessor).

The Property would be an input into the operations of Brisbane Airport Corporation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Brisbane Airport Corporation is the head lessor in respect of the Property and will be acquiring interests in existing leases

b. there are alternative sites available to develop or lease at Brisbane Airport, and in the wider Brisbane area, that are held by third parties.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The INNIO Group operates in the industrial equipment and energy infrastructure sector, primarily as a global manufacturer of gas power units under the Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. Its products are supplied to industries such as power generation, oil and gas (particularly for gas compression), utilities and large-scale industrial users. INNIO’s power units are used for distributed power generation, combined heat and power applications, and gas compression, serving a broad range of commercial, industrial and utility customers.

Enerflex Australia operates in the energy infrastructure services sector with a strong focus on supporting the gas industry as well as industrial energy users. The company provides aftermarket services such as maintenance, repair, spare parts, field services, retrofits, and operations and maintenance for a wide variety of gas compression and engine-power equipment.

Two wholly owned subsidiaries of INNIO Holding (INNIO Jenbacher International and INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands) propose to acquire from Enerflex Ltd (together with all its subsidiaries, **Enerflex Group**) the issued share capital in certain Enerflex Group operating companies in Australia, Indonesia and Thailand (**Target Companies**), which will transfer Enerflex Group’s aftermarket service businesses in those countries to the INNIO Group (**APAC Transaction**).

The Australian element of the APAC Transaction (the **Acquisition**) comprises the acquisition by INNIO Jenbacher International of all of the issued share capital in Enerflex Service Pty Limited (**Enerflex Australia**) from Enerflex Australasia Holdings Pty Ltd., an indirect, wholly-owned subsidiary of Enerflex Ltd. On completion of the Acquisition, Enerflex Australia will become an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of INNIO Holding and part of the INNIO Group’s global Jenbacher/Waukesha gas compression power units and services business.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Two wholly owned subsidiaries of INNIO Holding GmbH (INNIO Jenbacher International B.V. and INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands B.V.), propose to acquire from Enerflex Ltd (together with all its subsidiaries, Enerflex Group) the issued share capital in certain Enerflex Group operating companies in Australia, Indonesia and Thailand, which will transfer Enerflex Group’s aftermarket service businesses in those countries to the INNIO Group (APAC Transaction).

The Australian element of the APAC Transaction comprises the acquisition by INNIO Jenbacher International B.V. of all of the issued share capital in Enerflex Service Pty Limited (Enerflex Australia) from Enerflex Australasia Holdings Pty Ltd., an indirect, wholly-owned subsidiary of Enerflex Ltd, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

On completion of the Acquisition, Enerflex Australia will become an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of INNIO Holding GmbH and part of the INNIO Group’s global Jenbacher/Waukesha gas compression power units and services business.

INNIO Jenbacher International B.V., INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands B.V., INNIO Holding GmbH and INNIO Holding subsidiaries (together, the INNIO Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
16 April 2026

The INNIO Group operates in the industrial equipment and energy infrastructure sector, primarily as a global manufacturer of gas power units under the Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. Its products are supplied to industries such as power generation, oil and gas, utilities and large-scale industrial users. INNIO’s power units are used for distributed power generation, combined heat and power applications, and gas compression, serving a broad range of commercial, industrial and utility customers.

INNIO Holding GmbH, and thereby the INNIO Group, is indirectly held, managed and/or advised by Advent International, L.P. (Advent) and/or its affiliates. Advent is a globally active private equity investor based in Boston, USA.

The target, Enerflex Australia, operates in the energy infrastructure services sector with a focus on supporting the gas industry as well as industrial energy users. The company provides aftermarket services such as maintenance, repair, spare parts, field services, retrofits, and operations and maintenance for a wide variety of gas compression and engine-power equipment.

The INNIO Group is an upstream supplier of new compression and power generation power units. Enerflex Australia is a downstream multi-brand aftermarket services provider for energy infrastructure operators. The INNIO Group and Enerflex Australia also both supply certain spare parts for gas compression power units to customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, including the parties’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:

a. the vertical relationship between the products and services supplied by the INNIO Group and Enerflex Australia, their relative market position, and the likely impact of the Acquisition on the parties’ ability and/or incentive to foreclose rivals

b. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by alternative suppliers of gas compression power units and aftermarket services (including the provision of spare parts) to customers in Australia.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
BGIS Pty Ltd (**BGIS**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in the Ascot Air Facilities Pty Ltd (**Ascot Air**) from its shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

BGIS is a national provider of integrated facilities management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air conditioning (**HVAC**) services largely as part of broader facilities management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.

Ascot Air is a provider of commercial HVAC and mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air conditioning systems, building management systems and controls, drafting and design support and commissioning services.

Ascot Air provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane, Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, BGIS, is a national provider of integrated facilities management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) services largely as part of broader facilities management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.

The target, Ascot Air, is a provider of commercial HVAC and mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air conditioning systems, building management systems and controls, drafting and design support and commissioning services. Ascot Air provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane, Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.

Both parties supply commercial HVAC and related mechanical services within South East Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between BGIS and Ascot Air in the supply of commercial HVAC and related mechanical services to areas within South East Queensland

b. while there is a potential vertical supply relationship between the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition

c. there are alternative suppliers of commercial HVAC and related mechanical services in areas within South East Queensland.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Rosewood AG, LP, a Guernsey limited partnership (indirectly owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund) proposes to acquire voting rights in Collagen Holdings, LLC (trading as **Regenity**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Eighth Cinven Fund is indirectly managed and controlled by Cinven Limited. Cinven Limited has exclusive authority to make investment and management decisions for Eighth Cinven Fund.

Eighth Cinven Fund and Cinven Limited are part of Cinven. Cinven is a private equity firm engaged in the provision of investment management and investment advisory services to a number of investment funds. The controlled companies in Cinven’s portfolio of investments are active in a variety of sectors including business services, consumer, financial services, healthcare, industrials and technology, media and telecommunications.

The Acquisition will result in Cinven Limited and the existing investor, Linden Capital Partners, having joint control of Regenity.

Linden Capital Partners is a Chicago-based private equity firm focused exclusively on the healthcare industry.

Regenity is a global developer and manufacturer of bioresorbable technologies. The company is headquartered in Paramus, New Jersey.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Rosewood AG, LP (Rosewood) applied for a notification waiver in relation to its proposed acquisition of joint control over the voting rights in Collagen Holdings LLC (trading as Regenity), alongside the existing investor, Linden Capital Partners, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
1 April 2026

The acquirer, Rosewood, is a Guernsey limited partnership indirectly owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund, which is indirectly managed and controlled by Cinven Limited. Cinven Limited has exclusive authority to make investment and management decisions for Eighth Cinven Fund. Eighth Cinven Fund and Cinven Limited are part of Cinven.

Cinven is a private equity firm engaged in the provision of investment management and investment advisory services to a number of investment funds. The controlled companies in Cinven’s portfolio of investments are active in a variety of sectors including business services, consumer, financial services, healthcare, industrials and technology, media and telecommunications.

The target, Regenity, is a global developer and manufacturer of bioresorbable technologies. The company is headquartered in Paramus, New Jersey.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no competitive overlap or complementary relationship between Cinven and its connected entities and Regenity in the supply of bioresorbable technologies in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc. (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to acquire the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition consists of:

• CVC Capital Partners to indirectly acquire, through Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., an 80% shareholding and sole control over the SpecialtyCo Business and EssentialCo Business (which together comprise the animal nutrition and health division), and

• the DSM-Firmenich Group to (indirectly through to be incorporated entities) reinvest in each of the SpecialityCo Business and the EssentialCo Business to retain a ~20% non-controlling, minority stake in each.

Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, CVC Capital Partners, is a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and secondaries. CVC Capital Partners hold interests in companies active in a variety of industries around the world, including in financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retailing and distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-Pacific region.

The target, the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group, is a global provider of science-based animal nutrition and health solutions. Its core business is the manufacture and supply of animal nutrition ingredients, particularly vitamins and carotenoids, for livestock and aquaculture feed. It is also involved in the premix industry, which serve as a link from feed additives to final feed composition in the animal nutrition value chain.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap between the products and services supplied by the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group and CVC Capital Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia, or any vertical relationships.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Host-Plus Pty. Limited (**Hostplus**) proposes to acquire 50% of CH Investment Holding Trust (**CH Holding Trust**), a Charter Hall managed vehicle (the **Acquisition**).

Through the Acquisition, Hostplus will gain indirect economic exposure to 50% of the commercial property assets indirectly owned by CH Holding Trust, including an office building in the Sydney CBD.

Hostplus is the trustee for both the Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust and the Hostplus Superannuation Fund. The Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust is the unit trust vehicle that the Hostplus Superannuation Fund utilises to access investments for its members.

Charter Hall is an Australian property investment and funds management group, acting as a fiduciary for wholesale, institutional, and retail investors.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Host-Plus Pty. Limited as trustee for the Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust (Hostplus) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 50% of CH Investment Holding Trust (CH Holding Trust), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Hostplus, is the trustee for each of the Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust and the Hostplus Superannuation Fund. The Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust is the unit trust vehicle that the Hostplus Superannuation Fund utilises to access investments for its members. The Hostplus Superannuation Fund has 1.87 million members (as at 30 June 2025), with current funds under management of $134 billion (as at 30 June 2025). Hostplus invests across a diversified portfolio of asset classes, including equities, fixed income, infrastructure, property, private equity and alternative investments, for the benefit of its members. Hostplus has passive minority investments in funds that have exposure to commercial office space in the Sydney CBD.

The target, CH Holding Trust, indirectly holds commercial property assets in the Sydney CBD. CH Holding Trust is managed by Charter Hall which is an Australian property investment and funds management group, that acts as a fiduciary for wholesale, institutional, and retail investors.

Through the Acquisition, Hostplus will gain a 50% indirect interest in the commercial property assets indirectly owned by CH Holding Trust in the Sydney CBD.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the increment to Hostplus’ interests in commercial office property in Sydney CBD will be small

b. there are alternative commercial office offerings in the Sydney CBD.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Ryowa II GPS Pty Ltd (**Ryowa**) proposes to acquire:

* all or substantially all of the assets of the Phosphate Hill fertiliser mine and manufacturing plant (**Phosphate Hill**), and
* 100% of the issued share capital in PH Queensland Gas Pty Ltd (**PHQG**),

from Dyno Nobel Limited (**DNL**) and Southern Cross Operations Pty Ltd (**SCO**) (collectively, the **Sellers**) through an Asset Purchase Agreement.

Ryowa's ultimate parent company, Mayfair Australia Corporation Pty Ltd (**Mayfair Corporation**), is an Australian energy and resources company.  Ryowa seeks to enter the agriculture industry through the acquisition of Phosphate Hill. Ryowa currently owns a minority interest in the Gladstone Power Station.

Phosphate Hill is a fertiliser manufacturing plant located in Queensland. PHQG owns a minority interest in an undeveloped gas tenement in Queensland’s Surat Basin.

DNL is an international explosive manufacturing business. In Australia, DNL manufactures and supplies commercial explosives and provides commercial blasting services. SCO is a wholly owned subsidiary of DNL which operates in the fertiliser manufacturing business.

The Sellers seek to exit from Phosphate Hill to complete DNL's separation from its fertiliser endeavours.

determination_table_content.details:
Ryowa II GPS Pty Ltd (Ryowa), whose ultimate parent company is Mayfair Australia Corporation Pty Ltd (Mayfair Corporation), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

a. all or substantially all of the assets of the Phosphate Hill fertiliser mine and manufacturing plant (Phosphate Hill), and

b. 100% of the issued share capital in PH Queensland Gas Pty Ltd (PHQG) from Dyno Nobel Limited (DNL) and Southern Cross Operations Pty Ltd (SCO), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Ryowa, currently owns a minority interest in the Gladstone Power Station. Ryowa’s ultimate parent company, Mayfair Corporation, is an Australia energy and resources company.

The target, Phosphate Hill, is currently owned by DNL, an international explosive manufacturing business. In Australia, DNL manufactures and supplies commercial explosives and provides commercial blasting services. SCO is a wholly owned subsidiary of DNL, which currently operates in the fertiliser manufacturing business through Phosphate Hill.

PHQG owns a minority interest in the Range Gas Project, a development project in Queensland’s Surat Basin.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between the merger parties in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Zijin Gold (Group) Co., Ltd., (**Zijin**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining Group Co., Ltd. (**Zijin Mining**), proposes to acquire an interest in Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co., Ltd. (**Chifeng**), so that post-acquisition Zijin Mining as a whole will hold approximately 25.85% of the total number of shares in Chifeng, and acquire controlling power in Chifeng.

Zijin Mining is a global mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. It is primarily engaged in exploration, mining and processing of mineral resources. Its principal products include gold, copper, zinc, lithium, silver, molybdenum and other metals such as iron ore concentrate, cobalt and sulfuric acid.

Zijin, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining invests and manages the exploration and mining of mineral resources.

Norton Gold Fields Pty Ltd (**Norton**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Gold International Company Limited (a company under control of Zijin Mining), is a gold producer with mining and processing operations in Western Australia. Norton has a number of owned and controlled Australian entities, including Paddington Gold Pty Limited. The Norton Group is one of Australia's largest domestic gold producers with mining and processing complexes located in Western Australia's Kalgoorlie gold region.

Chifeng is a gold mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. Chifeng owns 7 operating mines and a resources recycling company in China, Southeast Asia and West Africa. Chifeng’s main product is gold, and it also produces copper, lead, zinc, molybdenum and rare earths.

Chifeng exports gold doré to customers in Australia but does not have any operating mines, assets or subsidiaries in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Zijin Gold (Group) Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining Group Co., Ltd. (Zijin Mining), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of an interest Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co., Ltd. (Chifeng), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

Post-Acquisition, Zijin Mining will hold approximately 25.85% of the total number of shares in Chifeng, and acquire controlling power in Chifeng.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Zijin Mining is a global mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. It is primarily engaged in exploration, mining and processing of mineral resources.

Its principal products include gold, copper, zinc, lithium, silver, molybdenum and other metals such as iron ore concentrate, cobalt and sulfuric acid.

Norton Gold Fields Pty Ltd (Norton), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Gold International Company Limited (a company under control of Zijin Mining), is a gold producer with mining and processing operations in Western Australia. The Norton Group is one of Australia's largest domestic gold producers with mining and processing complexes located in Western Australia's Kalgoorlie gold region.

Chifeng is a gold mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. Chifeng owns 7 operating mines and a resources recycling company in China, Southeast Asia and West Africa. Chifeng’s main product is gold, and it also produces copper, lead, zinc, molybdenum and rare earths.

Chifeng does not have any operating mines, assets or subsidiaries in Australia.

Zijin Mining and Chifeng both supply gold doré to customers in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of gold doré to customers in Australia is low

b. there are several alternative suppliers of gold doré to customers in Australia

c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of gold concentrate, gold doré and gold bullion post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The proposed acquisition involves the acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued share capital of Schroders plc (**Schroders**) by Pantheon, LLC (**Pantheon**), a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of Nuveen, LLC (**Nuveen**) (the **Acquisition**). Following completion, Nuveen, wholly owned by the Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association of America (**TIAA**), will hold 100% of the issued share capital of Schroders and therefore will have sole control of Schroders.

On 12 February 2026, the Boards of Nuveen and Schroders announced that they had agreed the terms of the Acquisition which is governed by the UK Takeover Code and is intended to be implemented by way of a court‑sanctioned scheme of arrangement.

Nuveen is the asset manager for TIAA. It is US-headquartered and offers a comprehensive range of investment solutions designed to secure the long-term financial goals of institutional and individual investors. Nuveen has ~AUD 2.17 trillion (~USD 1.4 trillion) in assets under management (**AUM**) as of 31 December 2025 – with approximately 93.5% in the Americas, 4% in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (**EMEA**) and 2.5% in Asia-Pacific (**APAC**).

TIAA is a New York stock life insurance company that provides secure retirements and outcome-focused investment solutions primarily to US-based individuals and institutions. TIAA has ~AUD 2.33 trillion (~USD 1.5 trillion) in AUM, and administration and total statutory capital of ~AUD 74.4 billion (~USD 48 billion) as of 31 December 2025.

Pantheon is a direct, wholly owned subsidiary of Nuveen that has been newly incorporated for the purpose of facilitating the Acquisition.

Schroders is a UK-based global investment manager which provides active asset management, wealth management and investment solutions. Schroders had ~AUD 1.68 trillion (~GBP 824 billion) of AUM as of 31 December 2025, with 12% in the Americas, 64% in EMEA and 25% in APAC. Schroders operates three core business divisions: Public Markets, Wealth Management, and Schroders Capital, which focuses on private markets, including private equity and real estate.

determination_table_content.details:
Pantheon LLC (Pantheon), a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of Nuveen, LLC (Nuveen), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued share capital of Schroders plc (Schroders), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

Following completion, Nuveen will hold 100% of the issued share capital of Schroders and will have sole control of Schroders.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Pantheon, is a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of Nuveen.

Nuveen is the asset manager for, and wholly owned by, the Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association of America (TIAA). Nuveen is US-headquartered and offers a comprehensive range of investment solutions designed to secure the long-term financial goals of institutional and individual investors. Nuveen has ~AUD 2.17 trillion (~USD 1.4 trillion) in AUM as of 31 December 2025 – with approximately 93.5% in the Americas, 4% in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) and 2.5% in Asia-Pacific (APAC).

TIAA is a New York stock life insurance company that provides secure retirements and outcome-focused investment solutions primarily to US-based individuals and institutions. TIAA has ~AUD 2.33 trillion (~USD 1.5 trillion) in assets under management (AUM), and administration and total statutory capital of ~AUD 74.4 billion (~USD 48 billion) as of 31 December 2025.

The target, Schroders is a UK-based global investment manager which provides active asset management, wealth management and investment solutions. Schroders had ~AUD 1.68 trillion (~GBP 824 billion) of AUM as of 31 December 2025, with 12% in the Americas, 64% in EMEA and 25% in APAC. Schroders operates three core business divisions: Public Markets, Wealth Management, and Schroders Capital, which focuses on private markets, including private equity and real estate.

Both Nuveen and Schroders supply asset management services to customers in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of asset management services to customers in Australia is low

b. there are alternative suppliers of asset management services to customers in Australia

c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of asset management and wealth management services post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition involves the merger of Indovida India Private Limited (**Indovida India**) with and into EPL Limited (**EPL**), by way of absorption of Indovida India as a going concern. In consideration of the merger, EPL (as the post-merger resultant entity) will issue and allot shares on a proportionate basis to the shareholders of Indovida India. As a result of the Acquisition, Indorama Netherland B.V. (**INBV**) (an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Indorama Ventures Public Company Limited (**IVL**)) and Blackstone Inc. (**Blackstone**, which exercises sole control over Epsilon Bidco Pte. Ltd. (**Epsilon**), the existing controlling shareholder of EPL) will acquire indirect joint control over EPL. As a consequence of the Acquisition, INBV's direct interest in EPL will increase from 24.51% to approximately 52%.

EPL is a publicly listed Indian company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of packaging products, including laminated and extruded plastic tubes, caps and closures, and laminates. Its products are supplied to customers in the fast-moving consumer goods and pharmaceutical industries.

Prior to completion of the Acquisition, various subsidiaries of Indovida Netherlands B.V. will be consolidated under Indovida India. Globally, the subsidiaries which will form part of the Indovida Group are engaged in the manufacture and sale of pre-forms, bottles, and high-density polyethylene (**HDPE**) and polypropylene (**PP**) closures for use in the food and beverage and consumer products sectors. The Indovida Group's activities in Australia are limited to the supply of imported packaging products, specifically pre-forms made from polyethylene terephthalate (**PET**), and closures made from HDPE and PP.

The Indovida Group forms part of the wider IVL Group, a global conglomerate headed by IVL. It has diversified business interests, including in plastic packaging.

Epsilon is a Singaporean-registered investment holding entity owned by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, a global alternative asset manager.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: EPL Limited (EPL) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of Indovida India Private Limited (Indovida India) and its proposed subsidiaries, through a share-for-share exchange, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, EPL, is a publicly listed Indian company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of packaging products, including laminated and extruded plastic tubes, caps and closures, and laminates. Its products are supplied to customers in fast-moving consumer goods and pharmaceutical industries, including in Australia.

The target, Indovida India, and its proposed subsidiaries (described below) (together, the Indovida Group) form part of the wider IVL Group, a global conglomerate headed by Indorama Ventures Public Company Limited. It has diversified business interests, including in plastic packaging.

Prior to completion of the Acquisition, various subsidiaries of Indovida Netherlands B.V. will be consolidated under Indovida India.

Subsequently, the Indovida Group will comprise Indovida India and its proposed subsidiaries, which are engaged in the manufacture and sale of pre-forms, bottles, and high-density polyethylene (HDPE) and polypropylene (PP) closures for use in the food and beverage and consumer products sectors globally. The Indovida Group’s activities in Australia are limited to the supply of imported packaging products, specifically pre-forms made from polyethylene terephthalate, and closures made from HDPE and PP.

The parties are therefore both active in the supply of plastic packaging products in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is limited overlap between the parties in the supply of plastic packaging products in Australia, as their respective offerings serve different use cases

b. the parties each have a small Australian presence

c. there are alternative suppliers of the types of plastic packaging products offered by the parties in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
McCarroll’s of Moss Vale Pty Ltd (**MMV**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets of 2 separate and independently owned motor vehicle dealership businesses, Country Motor Company Pty Ltd (**Country Motor Company**) and Kinghorn Motors Pty Ltd (**Kinghorn Motors**), via a special purpose vehicle, MAG South Coast Pty Ltd (**MAG South Coast**). As part of the transaction, MMV, via McCarroll’s Property Holdings Pty Ltd, also proposes to acquire the land on which Country Motor Company and Kinghorn Motors respectively operate.

MMV is a new and used vehicle retail business operating 15 franchised dealerships across locations in the Sydney, North Shore, Hornsby, and Newcastle areas of New South Wales (**NSW**). It is the authorised dealer of 15 original equipment manufacturer brands, including Alfa Romeo, BYD, GWM, Ferrari, Maserati, and Volkswagen, and also supplies parts, servicing, and finance.

Country Motor Company operates three franchised dealerships in the Nowra region on the NSW South Coast, representing Hyundai, Isuzu, and GWM. Kinghorn Motors operates two franchised dealerships in the Nowra region, representing Ford and Volkswagen. Both businesses also supply parts, servicing, and finance.

The parties all supply new and used cars, parts, servicing, and financing solutions. However, MMV does not presently operate any franchised dealerships in the Nowra area and is not an authorised dealer for Ford, Hyundai, or Isuzu.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
McCarroll’s of Moss Vale Pty Ltd (MMV) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

• 2 separate and independently owned motor vehicle dealership businesses, Country Motor Company Pty Ltd (Country Motor Company) and Kinghorn Motors Pty Ltd (Kinghorn Motors) (together, the Targets), via a special purpose vehicle, MAG South Coast Pty Ltd (MAG South Coast), and

• the land on which the Country Motor Company and Kinghorn Motors businesses are operated, via McCarroll Property Holdings Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The acquirer, MMV, is a new and used vehicle retail business operating 15 franchised dealerships across locations in the Sydney, North Shore, Hornsby, and Newcastle areas of New South Wales (NSW). It is the authorised dealer of 15 original equipment manufacturer (OEM) brands. It also supplies ancillary services including parts, servicing, and finance.

The Targets, Country Motor Company and Kinghorn Motors, are independently owned and operated, franchised dealerships located in the Nowra region on the South Coast of NSW which supply new and used cars, aftermarket parts, car servicing, and finance. Country Motor Company operates three dealerships (representing Hyundai, Isuzu, and GWM), and Kinghorn Motors operates two dealerships (representing Ford and Volkswagen).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, including the parties’ revenues in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition, which involves multiple independently operated dealerships, would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. In particular:

a. the Acquisition would combine the separately owned second- and third- largest independent dealerships and reduce the number dealerships of new and used cars in the Nowra region of NSW

b. the merged entity’s combined share of supply of new and used cars in the Nowra region of NSW may be significant

c. there is uncertainty regarding the extent to which the merged entity would be likely to face intra-brand and inter-brand competition from alternative dealerships in the Nowra region, the surrounding areas, or in NSW more broadly.

The ACCC considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via consultation with third parties.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
