mergers.merger_name: AXDI - minority interest in La Trobe Financial
mergers.merger_description:
AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (**AXDI**) proposes to acquire a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd (the **Target**) from BCP AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP LLC) (**Brookfield**), by way of a share sale agreement.

AXDI is a newly incorporated holding entity managed by Axight Capital Limited (**Axight**), that was established for the sole purpose of acquiring an interest in the issued share capital of the Target pursuant to the acquisition.

The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets under management, specialising in finance and credit investment solutions. La Trobe Financial operates across the following aspects of the wealth value chain:

* investment management and product manufacturing (managers investing client capital and manufacturers creating products to be acquired),
* platforms (technology platforms that allow advisers to manage clients' portfolios), and
* distribution (professional financial advisers who provide tailored advice to individuals and execute investment decisions).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (AXDI) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd (the holding company of La Trobe Financial) (the Target) from BCP AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP LLC) (Brookfield), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, AXDI, is a newly incorporated holding entity managed by Axight Capital Limited (Axight), established for the sole purpose of acquiring a minority interest in the issued share capital of the Target. Axight is an Abu Dhabi-based private equity-focused investment manager with over US$5 billion in assets under management and a focus on investing in developed and developing markets across Asia Pacific.

The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets under management, specialising in finance and credit investment solutions. La Trobe Financial’s core offering is Australian Real Estate Private Credit, being the provision of residential and commercial loans originated through independent mortgage broker channels in Australia. It also offers:

a. an ASX-listed Private Credit Fund, providing investors with monthly income and daily liquidity from diversified Australian real estate private credit and US mid-market corporate credit, and

b. a US Private Credit Fund, providing both wholesale and retail Australian investors with access to a curated and unique set of investment products.

The vendor, Brookfield, operates in the private equity industry, focusing on acquiring high-quality, cash-generative businesses in various sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between the target or La Trobe Financial and AXDI (or their portfolio companies) in the supply of residential and commercial loan or alternative asset management services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

determination_table_content.details: MedHealth Pty Limited (MedHealth) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the business and assets of Connect Allied Health Pty Ltd (Connect), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, MedHealth is an Australian subsidiary of the ExamWorks Group (ExamWorks). ExamWorks is a United States-based independent provider of services including independent medical examinations, peer reviews, bill reviews, Medicare compliance, record retrieval, document management and related services.

ExamWorks operates in Australia through MedHealth, which provides a range of services to a variety of businesses and consumers across the private and public sectors.

MedHealth’s Allied Health Services Division provides allied health services to a range of patients including NDIS participants, private health insurance clients and customers from other funding sources (for example, Department of Veterans’ Affairs, Medicare, My Aged Care and workers compensation) across Australia through a number of brands. Its brands include Ability Action Australia and Living to the Max which operate in South Australia.

In South Australia, MedHealth provides occupational therapy, speech pathology, exercise physiology, and physiotherapy allied health services.

The target, Connect, was founded in 2016 and is a multi-disciplinary AHS provider operating in South Australia. Connect provides clinic and community-based speech therapy, occupational therapy, physiotherapy, positive behavioural support, psychology and podiatry services across Adelaide and the Riverland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of allied health services in South Australia is low and the increment arising from the Acquisition would be small

b. there are numerous alternative suppliers of various allied health services to customers in Adelaide and regions in South Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services

mergers.merger_description:
Alta Genetics Australia Pty Ltd (**Alta Genetics**) is an Australian subsidiary of the URUS Group. The URUS Group is a global company active in the agricultural sector, specifically providing products and services in bovine genetics and reproductive technology.

Valley Agricultural Software, Inc. (**VAS**) is a subsidiary of the URUS Group. VAS supplies software and information solutions targeted specifically to dairy herd management for intensive dairy operations. The URUS Group, and by extension its subsidiaries, are indirectly controlled by funds advised or managed by affiliates (the **CVC Funds**) of CVC Capital Partners plc (**CVC Capital Partners**), a public limited company incorporated in Jersey and listed in Amsterdam. The CVC Network is a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and secondaries, and includes CVC Capital Partners, its subsidiaries, and the CVC Funds.

AgriWebb Pty Ltd (**AgriWebb**) is an Australian provider of livestock and farming management software. AgriWebb's software management solutions are designed to assist farmers in managing their day-to-day operational business with farm record keeping, farm mapping, livestock management, pasture and grazing management, task and workflow management, and farm auditing and compliance.

The parties are both active in the supply of livestock management software. VAS's software is designed to support dairy herd management for intensive dairy operations, whereas AgriWebb's software is directed towards grazing livestock (i.e. beef cattle and sheep) and farming management.

The Acquisition concerns the acquisition of 100% of the shares in AgriWebb by the URUS Group (via Alta Genetics).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Alta Genetics Australia Pty Ltd (Alta Genetics), an Australian subsidiary of the URUS Group which is indirectly controlled by funds advised or managed by affiliates of CVC Capital Partners plc (CVC Capital Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in AgriWebb Pty Ltd (AgriWebb), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Alta Genetics, is an Australian subsidiary of the URUS Group. The URUS Group is a global company active in the agricultural sector, specifically providing products and services in bovine genetics and reproductive technology. Another subsidiary of the URUS Group, Valley Agricultural Software, Inc. (VAS), supplies software and information solutions targeted to dairy herd management for intensive dairy operations.

The URUS Group, and by extension its subsidiaries, are indirectly controlled by funds advised or managed by affiliates of CVC Capital Partners (together, the CVC Funds), a public limited company incorporated in Jersey and listed in Amsterdam. The CVC Network is a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure, and secondaries, and includes CVC Capital Partners, each of its subsidiaries, and the CVC Funds.

The target, AgriWebb, is an Australian provider of livestock and farming management software. AgriWebb’s software management solutions are designed to assist farmers in managing their day-to-day operational business with farm record keeping, farm mapping, livestock management, pasture and grazing management, task and workflow management, and farm auditing and compliance.

The parties are both active in the supply of livestock management software in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

• Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is limited overlap between the product offerings of the parties, as their respective offerings primarily serve different use cases

b. there are alternative providers of the types of livestock management software offered by the parties, both globally and in Australia

c. VAS has a small Australian presence, and any increment in share of supply arising from the Acquisition is estimated to be small.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Tinicum and Blackstone led consortium – Senior
mergers.merger_description:
BMA IX LLC is a wholly owned Delaware subsidiary of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, **Blackstone**). Zeus UK Bidco Limited (Bidco),a newly formed company which will be indirectly jointly controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum Incorporated (**Tinicum**) and by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone (together, the **Consortium**), propose to acquire 100% of the shares in Senior plc (together with its subsidiaries, **Senior**), by way of a Court approved scheme of arrangement (Scheme) under Part 26 of the UK Companies Act 2006 (the **Acquisition**). Following the Acquisition, Tinicum and Blackstone will have a controlling interest in Senior.

Tinicum is a US-based private investment firm, focused on investing in both private and public companies across various sectors, including engineered products, industrial distribution and services, industrial software and technology, and specialty infrastructure. Funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum control two portfolio companies active in the aerospace fluid conveyance sector, PennAero and AeroFlow.

Blackstone is a US-headquartered global alternative asset manager. Blackstone has a broad portfolio of investments across multiple sectors. As part of the structure of the Acquisition, Blackstone will acquire joint control of Tinicum subsidiary, AeroFlow.

Senior is a UK publicly listed company that designs and manufactures high-technology components and systems for principal original equipment manufacturers in the worldwide aerospace and defence, land vehicle, power and energy, and industrials markets.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BMA IX LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, Blackstone). Zeus UK Bidco Limited (Zeus) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Senior plc (Senior), by way of a Court approved scheme of arrangement under Part 26 of the UK Companies Act 2006, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

Zeus is indirectly jointly controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum Incorporated (Tinicum) and by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone.

Following the Acquisition, Tinicum and Blackstone will have a controlling interest in Senior. The Acquisition will also provide Blackstone joint control together with Tinicum over AeroFlow Technologies, L.L.C. (AeroFlow).

The application for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition was jointly made by:

• Zeus

• Tinicum

• BMA IX LLC.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
1 June 2026

Tinicum is a US-based private investment firm, focused on investing in both private and public companies across sectors including engineered products, industrial distribution and services, industrial software and technology, and specialty infrastructure. Tinicum has an existing ~2.36% interest in Senior. Funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum also control PennAero and AeroFlow, which are active in the aerospace fluid conveyance sector. As a preparatory step to the Acquisition, AeroFlow will be transferred to Zeus as a capital contribution for the Acquisition through an intra-group reorganisation.

Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm.

The Acquisition also includes a non-controlling minority co-investment from Cliffwater. Cliffwater is an independent alternative investment advisor and fund manager. Its investment in Zeus is a passive investment with no control rights.

The target, Senior, is a UK publicly listed company that designs and manufactures high-technology components and systems for principal original equipment manufacturers in the worldwide aerospace and defence, land vehicle, power and energy, and industrials industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap and limited vertical relationships between the parties in Australia

b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of aerospace components post-acquisition

c. AeroFlow and Senior have a small presence in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

mergers.merger_description:
WSP Global Inc. (**WSP**) is a global engineering consulting services firm, which is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. In Australia, WSP operates through its wholly owned subsidiary, WSP Australia Pty Limited, and provides engineering services to customers across various industries (except where otherwise indicated, WSP and WSP Australia Pty Ltd are collectively referred to as WSP).

TRC Companies, Inc. (**TRC**) (referred to as the Target, together with its related subsidiaries forming the TRC group) is a US-based privately held engineering consulting and construction services firm which predominantly services clients in the US who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets. TRC has no subsidiary, employees or other physical presence in Australia and has immaterial supplies to Australian customers from its offshore operations.

WSP has entered into an agreement to indirectly acquire 100% of the shares of TRC.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Chair Cass-Gottlieb pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: WSP Global Inc. (WSP) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in TRC Companies, Inc. (TRC), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
12 January 2026

The acquirer, WSP, is a global engineering consulting services firm which provides services related to planning, engineering, quality assurance and implementation of large engineering projects in the construction, energy, transport, infrastructure, building, and natural resource sectors in Australia.

The target, TRC, is an engineering consulting and construction management firm that predominantly works with clients in the US who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets, including energy providers, utility providers, transportation agencies, government bodies, and industrial and commercial enterprises. TRC provides limited services to customers outside of the US.

The parties both supply engineering consulting services, including for the energy, transport and infrastructure sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act), being: (i) the object of the Act, (ii) the interests of consumers, (iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply, and (iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in any market.

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is very limited horizontal overlap between WSP and TRC in the supply of goods or services in Australia. The information provided by WSP in its application indicates that TRC currently has no presence in Australia and generates negligible revenue from offshore sales to customers in Australia.

b. The parties’ combined market share in the broader supply of engineering consulting services in Australia is very low and any aggregation in more specialised service offerings would be minimal.

c. The parties are likely to continue to be constrained by alternative global or national suppliers of engineering consulting services in Australia, including design and project management services in the energy, environment, transport and infrastructure sectors.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-35001/WSP-TRC (WA-35001) - Notification waiver determination - Acquisition not required to be notified - 12 January 2026.pdf

comments.commentary:
WSP's global acquisition of TRC Companies was the one of the first two acquisitions to be granted a notification waiver by the ACCC. Both parties supply engineering consulting services, including for energy, transport and infrastructure sectors. The ACCC determined the acquisition did not require notification because:

- there is very limited horizontal overlap in Australia, as TRC has no Australian presence and generates negligible revenue from Australian customers;
- the parties' combined market share in engineering consulting services in Australia is very low; and
- alternative global and national suppliers remain.

While notification thresholds were likely met, the ACCC waived the requirement given its view that there were minimal competition concerns.

mergers.merger_description:
IFS North America Inc. (**IFS North America**), via its wholly owned subsidiary incorporated for the purposes of the transaction, Fjord Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Softeon, Inc. (**Softeon**) through a reverse triangular merger where:

(a) Merger Sub will merge with and into Softeon; and

(b) Merger Sub will then cease, and Softeon will be the surviving company.

As a result, Softeon will become a wholly owned subsidiary of IFS North America.

IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together, IFS). IFS develops and commercialises business software for customers that manufacture and distribute goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations. Its technology enables manufacturing businesses to maintain complex assets and manage service-focused operations to enhance productivity, efficiency and sustainability.

Softeon is headquartered in Reston, VA. and supplies a supply chain fulfilment software platform for warehouses. Softeon operates in the warehouse management systems segment and provides a suite of supply chain software solutions on a single, integrated services-based technology platform that offers the following cloud-based software capabilities: warehouse management system, warehouse execution system and distributed order management solutions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: IFS North America Inc. (IFS North America) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, via its subsidiary Fjord Merger Sub Inc (Merger Sub), of 100% of the share capital in Softeon Inc. (Softeon), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
27 January 2026

The acquirer, IFS North America, develops and commercialises business software for customers that manufacture and distribute goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations.

The software includes functionalities that use artificial intelligence to support productivity, efficiency, and sustainable objectives.

IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together, IFS). IFS is jointly controlled by EQT IX, an investment fund managed by EQT Fund Management S.à r.l. (a global investment organisation), and by Hg Pooled Management Limited, an investor in software and services businesses, with a focus on Europe and the United States.

The target, Softeon, is a global software provider of warehouse management systems and innovative supply chain solutions. Softeon is headquartered in Reston, Virginia, USA and has a very limited presence in Australia.

Both parties supply enterprise application software, however IFS’s Australian business mainly supplies enterprise resource planning services, whereas Softeon specialises in warehouse management and supply chain management solutions. These services are largely complementary.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Softeon currently has a very small presence in Australia.

b. There is a small horizontal overlap between IFS and Softeon in the supply of enterprise application software in Australia, and no overlap in the supply of enterprise resource planning and supply chain management services.

c. The parties’ combined shares in the supply of enterprise application software in Australia are small, and they are likely to continue to be constrained by several competitors.

d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from bundling or tying of products post-merger.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

mergers.merger_description:
Salesforce, Inc (**Salesforce**) is a global supplier of customer relationship management (**CRM**) software and other software-as-a-service solutions that enable companies to manage and improve their relationships with customers. Salesforce is a publicly listed company on the New York Stock Exchange and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Qualified.com, Inc (**Qualified**), founded in 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco, California, is a provider of an agentic marketing product. Qualified offers its products worldwide, including in Australia.

Both Salesforce and Qualified supply conversation automation solutions for business-to-business (**B2B**) uses, which integrate with Salesforce’s CRM software. Conversation automation solutions for B2B uses includes tools, such as virtual assistants, artificial intelligence (**AI**) agents, and chatbots, that support functions such as outbound and inbound sales prospecting for lead generation, customer qualification, customer research, meeting scheduling, and personalised product recommendations.

Salesforce proposes to acquire by merger all of the outstanding voting securities of Qualified.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Salesforce, Inc (Salesforce) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the outstanding voting securities of Qualified.com, Inc (Qualified), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
20 January 2026

The acquirer, Salesforce, is a global supplier of customer relationship management (CRM) software and other software-as-a-service solutions that enable companies to manage and improve their relationship with customers. Salesforce is a publicly listed company on the New York Stock Exchange and is headquartered in San Francisco, California. Salesforce offers a suite of artificial intelligence (AI) agents, which are integrated with its core CRM offerings and automate tasks related to sales, service, marketing and commerce, such as personalised engagement with potential and existing customers and scheduling meetings.

The target, Qualified, is a supplier of an AI-based sales development representative (SDR) agent, known as Piper, which integrates with Salesforce’s CRM platform. Piper is an agentic marketing product that is focused on engaging customers through a company’s website and converting them into buyers. Qualified is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and offers its products worldwide, including in Australia.

Salesforce and Qualified overlap in Australia in the supply of conversation automation solutions for business-to-business (B2B) uses, such as virtual assistants, AI agents or chatbots, to support sales and marketing teams to engage with customers.

In addition to the horizontal overlap, there is a vertical relationship between Qualified’s product Piper and Salesforce’s CRM platform.

Piper uses Salesforce’s CRM data to inform its agent’s interactions and records information about customer engagement in Salesforce’s CRM platform.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, public information and other industry information in the possession of the ACCC from its consideration of prior matters, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act), being: (i) the object of the Act, (ii) the interests of consumers, (iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply, and (iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in any market.

Based on the information provided in the application, including Salesforce’s revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC also considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:

a. Salesforce is the leading provider of CRM software to businesses in Australia and is significantly larger than the next largest supplier.

b. The parties operate in complex and dynamic markets, including the provision of agentic AI.

c. There is a vertical relationship between the products supplied by Salesforce and Qualified. The extent of the parties’ ability and/or incentive to foreclose rivals to Qualified or Salesforce warrants further consideration to be able to form a conclusive view on the competition impacts of the Acquisition, despite the relatively small presence of Qualified in Australia at present.

The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant consideration through the notification process, including via consultation with third parties.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
L Catterton Management Limited, together with its controlled funds and affiliates (LCAT), proposes to acquire ~45% of the issued share capital of Ex Nihilo Group (Ex Nihilo), through LCAT’s controlled entity, Saphir Holdings SAS (Saphir).

LCAT is a consumer-focused private equity firm based in the United States with assets under management globally.

Ex Nihilo is a luxury fragrance house based in France specialising in the creation, production, and wholesale and retail distribution of high-end perfumes and related products. Ex Nihilo was launched in 2013 and has retailers operating worldwide.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: L Catterton Management Limited (LCAT) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, through its controlled entity Saphir Holdings SAS (Saphir) and together with its controlled funds and affiliates, of ~45% of the issued share capital in Ex Nihilo Group (Ex Nihilo), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
4 February 2026

LCAT is a consumer-focused private equity firm based in the United States with assets under management globally. LCAT has 12 controlled investments that are active in the wholesale and retail supply of perfumes. Three of these investments (Vyrao, A.P.C. and Merit) have activities in Australia. The acquirer, Saphir, is a controlled entity of LCAT.

The target, Ex Nihilo, is a luxury fragrance house based in France specialising in the creation, production, and wholesale and retail distribution of high-end perfumes and related products. Ex Nihilo was launched in 2013 and has retailers operating worldwide.

LCAT’s controlled investments and Ex Nihilo overlap in the wholesale and retail supply of perfumes in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Ex Nihilo has a small presence in Australia.

b. The horizontal overlap between entities controlled by LCAT and Ex Nihilo in the wholesale and retail supply of perfumes in Australia appears to be small.

c. The parties’ combined share of supply in the wholesale and retail supply of perfumes is low.

d. There are numerous alternative wholesale and retail suppliers of perfumes in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Superloop Limited (ASX: SLC) (**Superloop**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Lynham Networks Pty. Ltd. (**Lynham Networks**).

Superloop’s core business is retail broadband services, operating as a retail service provider (**RSP**) supplying broadband services to end users, primarily using the NBN network as well as a private fibre-to-the-premises (**FTTP**) network. Superloop also has a number of wholesale FTTP networks under its subsidiaries, VostroNet and Frontier, which are statutory infrastructure providers (**SIP**) that build and operate wholesale access FTTP broadband networks for declared service areas. Superloop provides retail fixed broadband services on its FTTP network as well as wholesaling FTTP services to other RSPs on a non-discriminatory basis.

Lynham Network is a FTTP network operator and SIP supplying wholesale, open access network services that enable RSPs to sell fixed broadband services to end customers. Its core business involves building and operating fibre networks primarily for new residential and mixed-use developments. Lynham Network also has a retail arm, Lightning Broadband, which sells broadband services to residential and business customers but only over the Lynham FTTP network. Lynham does not offer broadband services over the NBN network.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Superloop Limited (Superloop) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Lynham Networks Pty. Ltd. (Lynham Networks), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 February 2026

The acquirer, Superloop, is primarily a retail broadband services business, operating as a retail service provider supplying broadband services to end users, primarily using the NBN network as well as a private fibre-to-the-premises (FTTP) network. Superloop also has a number of wholesale FTTP networks under its subsidiaries, VostroNet and Frontier, which are statutory infrastructure providers (SIP) that build and operate wholesale access FTTP broadband networks for declared service areas. Superloop provides retail fixed broadband services on its FTTP network as well as wholesaling FTTP services to other retail service providers on a non-discriminatory basis.

The target, Lynham Networks, is primarily a FTTP operator that builds and operates fibre networks primarily for new residential and mixed-use developments. Lynham Networks is a SIP supplying wholesale, open access network services that enable retail service providers to sell fixed broadband services to end customers. Lynham Networks also has a retail arm, Lightning Broadband, which sells broadband services to residential and business customers but only over the Lynham Networks FTTP network. Lynham Networks does not offer broadband services over the NBN network.

Both Superloop and Lynham Networks operate in the telecommunications and internet industry providing fixed retail and wholesale broadband services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Superloop and Lynham Networks in the supply of retail and wholesale fixed broadband services in Australia

b. the presence of alternative providers of retail and wholesale fixed broadband services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

mergers.merger_description:
Victorian Funds Management Corporation (**VFMC**), as investment manager of several investment vehicles (**VFMC Clients**), proposes for the VFMC Clients to subscribe to units in two stapled trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (**Land Trust**) and RF CorVal MHE Operator Trust (**Operator Trust**) (together, **MHE Fund**).

MHE Fund is a stapled structure pursuant to which the Land Trust holds properties and leases them to the Operator Trust. MHE Fund’s downstream co-venture, also comprised of a stapled land trust and operator trust, develops and manages residential property.

The VFMC Clients involved in the acquisition are the Emergency Services Superannuation Board (as trustee for Emergency Services Superannuation Scheme), the Transport Accident Commission, the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority, the Victorian WorkCover Authority, and the VFMC Investment Fund (of which VFMC is the trustee).

Following completion of the transaction, State Street Australia Ltd, acting as custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, will hold minority interests in the relevant MHE Fund entities.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Victorian Funds Management Corporation (VFMC), as investment manager of the following investment vehicles:

• the Emergency Services Superannuation Board (as trustee for Emergency Services Superannuation Scheme)

• the Transport Accident Commission

• the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority

• the Victorian WorkCover Authority, and

• the VFMC Investment Fund (of which VFMC is the trustee) (together, the VFMC Clients), proposes that the VFMC Clients subscribe to units in two stapled trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (Land Trust) and RF CorVal MHE Operator Trust (Operator Trust) (together, the MHE Fund), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

As part of the Acquisition, State Street Australia Ltd (State Street), acting as custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, will hold minority interests in the relevant MHE Fund entities.

A notification waiver application in respect of the Acquisition was made by VFMC, the Emergency Service Superannuation Board, the Transport Accident Commission, the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority and the Victorian WorkCover Authority.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
26 March 2026

VFMC is a statutory authority established under the Victorian Funds Management Corporation Act 1994 (Vic) to provide investment and funds management services to public sector insurance and superannuation agencies.

VFMC acts as investment manager for a number of clients and invests across a range of asset classes, including Australian and international equities, infrastructure and property, inflation linked bonds, diversified fixed interest, non-traditional strategies (hedge funds, private credit, insurance and others) and cash.

State Street, the entity that will hold the units in the relevant MHE Fund entities as a custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, provides financial solutions to institutional investors through investment servicing and management, and market and data intelligence.

The target, the MHE Fund, is active in the property and management industry. The MHE Fund is a stapled structure pursuant to which the Land Trust holds properties and leases them to the Operator Trust.

MHE Fund’s downstream co-venture, also comprised of a stapled land trust and operator trust, develops and manages residential property. CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee of the MHE Fund.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between the current investments of VFMC and/or the VFMC Clients and the activities of the MHE Fund

b. there are alternative developers and managers of residential property and manufactured housing estates in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
On 27 February 2026, Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (an affiliate of various funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc. (**Blackstone**)) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to acquire all the issued shares in Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (**ACT**).

ACT is a US-based engineering and manufacturing company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling solutions for a range of industries. ACT provides passive and active heat transfer technologies, including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops, thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial markets.

Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.

determination_table_content.details: Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (Rocket) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (ACT), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 March 2026

Blackstone, Inc. (Blackstone) is a US-based investment management firm. Rocket is a special purpose vehicle that is an affiliate of various funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone.

The target, ACT, is a US-based engineering and manufacturing company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling solutions. It supplies passive and active heat transfer technologies, including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops, thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial markets.

The application indicates that no funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone have an interest in any business that competes in relation to any of the products supplied by ACT in Australia, or acquires products from, or supplies products or services to, ACT in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular: a) There is no horizontal overlap in Australia between the products and services supplied by the parties, and there are no vertical relationships. b) ACT has a small presence in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
On 16 February 2026, Austral Resources Australia Ltd (**Austral**) entered into an agreement with Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (**NP2**) and Mount Isa Mines Limited (**MIM**) (both indirect subsidiaries of Glencore plc) to acquire:

* 100% of the shares in Noranda Pacific Pty Ltd (**NPPL**), the owner and operator of the Lady Loretta zinc mine in Queensland, from NP2 and MIM; and
* the exploration permits associated with the Lady Loretta mine from MIM, (together, the **Acquisition**).

The Lady Loretta mine ceased production in 2025.

Austral is an Australian-based copper producer headquartered in Queensland. Austral owns and operates a number of copper mines in Australia, including the Lady Annie copper mine which is located 16km south of the Lady Loretta mine.

NPPL, NP2 and MIM are indirect wholly-owned subsidiaries of Glencore plc, a multi-national commodity trading and mining company headquartered in Switzerland.

Following completion, Austral will assume the progressive rehabilitation obligations of the Lady Loretta mine, and will enter into an offtake agreement with Glencore in respect of future copper products derived from the Lady Loretta tenements.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Austral Resources Australia Limited (Austral) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

• 100% of the shares in Noranda Pacific Pty Ltd (NPPL), the owner and operator of the Lady Loretta zinc mine in Queensland, from Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (NP2) and Mount Isa Mines Limited (MIM); and

• the exploration permits associated with the Lady Loretta mine from MIM, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Austral, is an Australian-based copper producer headquartered in Queensland. Austral owns and operates a number of copper mines in Australia, including the Lady Annie copper mine which is located 16km south of the Lady Loretta mine.

The Lady Loretta mine ceased production in 2025. Prior to its closure, the mine produced zinc concentrate (with lead and silver concentrate by-products), 100% of which was supplied to related entities of NPPL for processing in Mount Isa.

NPPL, NP2 and MIM are indirect wholly-owned subsidiaries of Glencore plc, a multi-national commodity trading and mining company headquartered in Switzerland.

Following completion, Austral will assume the progressive rehabilitation obligations of the Lady Loretta mine, and will enter into an offtake agreement with Glencore in respect of future copper products derived from the Lady Loretta tenements.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Austral’s share of the actual and expected production and supply of copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in Australia and globally is expected to be low

b. there are several alternative producers and suppliers of copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in Australia and globally.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Allied Retail Finance Pty Ltd (**Allied Retail Finance**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Allied Credit Pty Ltd (**Allied Credit**), proposes to acquire the remaining portfolio of automotive loans and leases originated by Macquarie Leasing Pty Limited and other associated assets (including all legal and equitable interests in the data and records relating to the loan assets) held by Macquarie Leasing Pty Ltd, Macquarie Bank Limited and the trustee of the SMART B Trust (together, **Macquarie**).

The proposed transaction is supplementary to previous transactions between the parties as part of Macquarie's staged exit from the automotive finance industry which has been ongoing since 2021. Macquarie will continue to service a small portfolio of car loans originated by Macquarie Bank Limited but ceased new car loan originations in April 2024.

Allied Credit was established in 2010 and is the parent company of the Allied Credit Group, which provides motorcycle, marine, recreation and automotive finance solutions to vehicle and equipment businesses, original equipment manufacturers and distributor dealer networks and their customers in Australia. Allied Credit's product range includes retail consumer, commercial, and wholesale floorplan finance, as well as novated leases.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Allied Retail Finance Pty Ltd (Allied Retail Finance), a wholly owned subsidiary of Allied Credit Pty Ltd (Allied Credit), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of automotive loans and leases originated by Macquarie Leasing Pty Limited and other associated assets (including all legal and equitable interests in the data and records relating to the loan assets) held by Macquarie Leasing Pty Limited, Macquarie Bank Limited and the trustee of the SMART B Trust (together, Macquarie), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Allied Credit was established in 2010 and provides motorcycle, marine, recreation and automotive finance solutions to vehicle and equipment businesses, original equipment manufacturers and distributor dealer networks and their customers in Australia. Allied Credit's product range includes retail consumer, commercial, and wholesale floorplan finance, as well as novated leases.

Macquarie is a global financial services group operating in asset management, retail and business banking, wealth management, as well as advisory, and risk and capital solutions across debt, equity, financial markets and commodities.

Macquarie has ceased originating retail automotive financing loans.

Macquarie's staged exit from the automotive finance industry began in 2021 (Macquarie will continue to service a small portfolio of ongoing car loans but ceased new car loan originations in April 2024). Prior to its exit, Macquarie provided car loans, asset finance, and novated leasing to personal and business clients.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Allied Credit and Macquarie in the supply of retail automative finance in Australia

b. there are alternative suppliers of retail automative finance in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified. The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Wipro Enterprises Private Limited (**Wipro**) proposes to acquire a 91% interest in Indeco Ind S.p.A. and its subsidiaries (**Indeco**). A sale and purchase agreement was executed on 9 April 2026. Pursuant to that agreement, Wipro has designated its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, Wipro Pari Italy S.r.l., as the direct acquiring entity.

Wipro is a privately held company incorporated in India that operates across a variety of sectors including fast-moving consumer goods, lighting, hydraulics, industrial automation, 3D printing, aerospace component manufacturing, and industrial water treatment. Its hydraulics division designs and manufactures hydraulic cylinders (i.e. hydraulic actuators that provide linear motion when hydraulic energy is converted into mechanical movement) with applications in construction and earthmoving, mining, lifting, forestry, agriculture and trucking. Wipro’s presence in Australia is limited to a related entity, Wipro Limited, which is a part of the Wipro Group (i.e. under common control). Wipro Limited and its Australian subsidiaries provide consulting, engineering, IT, and business process services in Australia.

Indeco is a joint stock company incorporated in Italy. Indeco is a manufacturer of hydraulic demolition hammers and other excavator attachments. Indeco also provides maintenance services, primarily related to hydraulic hammers. Indeco’s products and services are primarily used in the mining and quarrying, infrastructure, land clearing, construction, demolition, and recycling sectors. Indeco has multiple subsidiaries globally.

Indeco operates in Australia through its wholly owned subsidiary, Indeco Australia Pty Ltd (**Indeco Australia**). Indeco Australia sells hydraulic hammers and excavator attachments (including compactors, grabs, shears, pulverisers, mulching heads and multi-processors) as well as spare parts, which have applications in construction, mining, demolition, recycling, land clearing, infrastructure and forestry.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Wipro Pari Italy S.r.l. (Wipro Pari), an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Wipro Enterprises Private Limited (Wipro), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 91% interest in Indeco Ind S.p.A (Indeco), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Wipro, is a privately held company incorporated in India that operates across a variety of sectors including fast-moving consumer goods, lighting, hydraulics, industrial automation, 3D printing, aerospace component manufacturing, and industrial water treatment. Its hydraulics division designs and manufactures hydraulic cylinders (i.e. hydraulic actuators that provide linear motion when hydraulic energy is converted into mechanical movement) with applications in construction and earthmoving, mining, lifting, forestry, agriculture and trucking.

Wipro’s related entity, Wipro Limited, together with its subsidiaries, provides consulting, engineering, IT, and business process services in Australia.

The target, Indeco, is incorporated in Italy and is a manufacturer of hydraulic demolition hammers and other excavator attachments.

Indeco operates in Australia through its wholly owned subsidiary, Indeco Australia Pty Ltd, which sells hydraulic hammers and excavator attachments (including compactors, grabs, shears, pulverisers, mulching heads and multi-processors) as well as spare parts. These products have applications in construction, mining, demolition, recycling, land clearing, infrastructure and forestry.

Hydraulic cylinders are an upstream input into some excavator attachments. However, Wipro does not supply hydraulic cylinders to Indeco in Australia, or globally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between Wipro and Indeco (including their connected entities) in the supply of any products or services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Respect Group Limited (**Respect**) proposes to acquire 100% of the business and assets owned by Jallarah Homes Inc (**Jallarah**) (the **Acquisition**)

Respect is a not-for-profit registered charity and unlisted public company based in Tasmania which provides residential aged care, retirement living and support at home services focused on dignity and well-being for older adults. Respect owns and operates 40 residential aged care homes and 30 retirement villages across Tasmania, New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland.

Jallarah is a not-for-profit incorporated association and registered charity operating a single site residential aged care facility and co-located independent living units in Meningie, South Australia. The aged care facility has 30 beds and 5 independent living units.

Each of the parties provides residential aged care services in accordance with the *Aged Care Act 2024* (Cth). Jallarah also provides retirement living services under the *Retirement Villages Act 2016* (SA) whilst Respect provides retirement living services in accordance with the relevant state legislation in Tasmania, New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland. If the Acquisition proceeds, the Jallarah business would constitute Respect’s first entry into the South Australian residential aged care and retirement living market.

The Acquisition is planned to occur through a two step process in which the parties enter into a merger implementation deed, whereby the directors of Respect are appointed to the board of Jallarah, such that Jallarah becomes an associated entity of Respect, and subsequently, the business and assets (including land) of Jallarah are transferred to Respect. The residential aged care home and independent living units will continue to operate following the transaction.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, Respect, is a not-for-profit registered charity and unlisted public company based in Tasmania which provides residential aged care, retirement living and support at home services focused on dignity and well-being for older adults. Respect owns and operates 40 residential aged care homes and 30 retirement villages across Tasmania, New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland.

The target, Jallarah, is a not-for-profit incorporated association and registered charity operating a single site residential aged care facility and co-located independent living units in Meningie, South Australia.

The aged care facility has 30 beds and 5 independent living units.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no local or regional overlap between Respect and Jallarah in the supply of residential aged care and retirement living services

b. the increase in national share of supply of residential aged care and retirement living services arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. there are several alternative suppliers of residential aged care and retirement living services to customers in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Star Hotels is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of QLD as well in South Australia. It currently operates over 50 venues in QLD, including two pubs in the Sunshine Coast area.

The Yandina Hotel is owned by the Trustee for Kingston Family Trust (trading as Yandina Hotel), a single-site operator. The Yandina Hotel offers dining, accommodation, gaming and a bottle shop.

Under the Acquisition, Star Hotels will acquire both the freehold land and the business assets of the Yandina Hotel.

determination_table_content.details: Hotel Maroochydore Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd, subsidiaries of Star Consolidated Holdings Ptd Ltd (Star Hotels) applied for a notification waiver in respect of a proposed acquisition of the business and assets of the Yandina Hotel in Yandina, Qld, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 February 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels, is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating over 50 pubs along the eastern seaboard of QLD and four in SA. It operates multiple licensed venues, accommodation, liquor retail outfits under brands such as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze. It currently operates two pubs in the Sunshine Coast area.

The target, Yandina Hotel, is a hotel in Yandina, QLD that offers dining, accommodation, gaming and a bottle shop. It is currently owned and operated by the Trustee for Kingston Family Trust (trading as Yandina Hotel), a single-site operator.

Star Hotels and the Yandina Hotel overlap in the supply of dining, accommodation, gaming and liquor in the Sunshine Coast area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant, including in the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. while there is horizontal overlap between Star Hotels and the Yandina Hotel, concentration and the combined share of the parties in the supply of accommodation, dining, functions, gaming and liquor services in the broader Sunshine Coast area is low

b. the presence of alternative suppliers of accommodation, dining, functions, gaming and liquor in the broader Sunshine Coast and Noosa area.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between CVC Capital Partners and Smiths Detection Group in the supply of security screening and threat detection technologies in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
