mergers.merger_name: Vets Central – Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic
mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic (the Targets) from Gawler Animal Hospital Pty Ltd and GAH 2.0 Pty Ltd, respectively.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 78 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The Targets are jointly owned, first opinion veterinary practices that provide routine, planned and emergency (excluding after-hours) veterinary services in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South Australia.

Vet Central and the Targets both provide a range of veterinary services for household and exotic pets in their respective local areas.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, Vets Central) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the assets of Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
12 February 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 78 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The targets, Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic, are jointly owned, full-service veterinary practices in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South Australia. Both businesses also provide a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets and exotic pets in their respective local areas. The Gawler Animal Hospital also provides behaviour classes and complicated surgeries.

Vets Central operates 7 veterinary practices in South Australia, the closest being over 30 km away from the targets.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There appears to be limited or no local geographic horizontal overlap between Vets Central and the Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic in the supply of veterinary services in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South Australia.

b. The parties’ combined share of supply of veterinary services in South Australia and Australia is not high and there are alternative suppliers in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South Australia, broader South Australia and Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Permira is a Guernsey registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare and technology sectors.

Warburg Pincus is private equity firm headquartered in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are active in a variety of sectors, including consumer, energy, financial services, healthcare, industrial and business services and technology, media and telecommunications. In Australia, Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active in software and financial sectors.

Clearwater Analytics is a software-as-a-service company, headquartered in Boise, Idaho, USA, that provides automated, cloud-based software solutions for investors to manage investment data. Clearwater Analytics provides accounting and reporting, data, advanced analytics, risk and performance and portfolio and order management capabilities for a range of investor types across both public and private markets.

Clearwater Analytics’ platform services the following clients: asset managers, banks, corporates, energy (e.g., commodity trading), hedge funds, insurance companies, pensions and endowments, and the public sector (i.e., government entities managing public funds).

The Acquisition concerns the proposed acquisition of joint control over Clearwater Analytics by funds managed and advised by subsidiaries or affiliates of Permira and Warburg Pincus by way of purchase of shares.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
GT Silver BidCo, Inc applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of share capital in Clearwater Analytics Holdings, Inc (Clearwater Analytics), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

GT Silver BidCo, Inc is a wholly owned subsidiary of GT Silver Parent LP, which will be jointly controlled by funds managed and advised by subsidiaries or affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (Permira) and Warburg Pincus LLC (along with other affiliates acquiring non-controlling stakes, including Francisco Partners and Temasek).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
31 March 2026

Permira is a Guernsey registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare and technology sectors.

Warburg Pincus LLC is private equity firm headquartered in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus LLC’s portfolio companies are active in a variety of sectors, including consumer, energy, financial services, healthcare, industrial and business services and technology, media and telecommunications. In Australia, Warburg Pincus LLC’s portfolio companies are primarily active in software and financial sectors.

The target, Clearwater Analytics, a software-as-a-service company, headquartered in Boise, Idaho, USA, that provides automated, cloud-based software solutions for investors to manage investment data.

Clearwater Analytics provides accounting and reporting, data, advanced analytics, risk and performance and portfolio and order management capabilities for a range of investor types across both public and private markets.

Clearwater Analytics’ platform services the following clients: asset managers, banks, corporates, energy (e.g., commodity trading), hedge funds, insurance companies, pensions and endowments, and the public sector (i.e., government entities managing public funds).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of investment, accounting and reporting software and a small horizontal overlap in the supply of software in Australia

b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Element Zero's L701 – Option to lease DevelopmentWA industrial land at Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area
mergers.merger_description:
L701 Pty Ltd proposes to enter into an option to lease agreement with the Western Australian Land Authority (trading as **DevelopmentWA**) over part of Lot 701 within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area in Western Australia. The option provides L701 Pty Ltd with the right to lease the land following completion of development milestones.

L701 Pty Ltd is a subsidiary of Element Zero Pty Ltd (**Element Zero**), an Australian company developing technology and projects for the production of low-emissions mineral products, including green iron. Element Zero develops technology and projects for the production of low-carbon mineral products and intends to develop a commercial-scale green iron production facility.

DevelopmentWA is the Western Australian Government’s land and development agency responsible for facilitating industrial land development. DevelopmentWA develops and manages industrial land and infrastructure in Western Australia, including within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area.

If the option is exercised, the parties intend to enter into a long-term industrial ground lease to enable development and operation of a green iron production facility.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: L701 Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposal to enter into an option to lease agreement with the Western Australian Land Authority (trading as DevelopmentWA) over part of Lot 701 within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area in Western Australia, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). The option provides L701 Pty Ltd with the right to lease the land following completion of development milestones.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, L701 Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Element Zero Pty Ltd (Element Zero), an Australian company developing technology and projects for the production of low-emissions mineral products, including green iron. Element Zero develops technology and projects for the production of low-carbon mineral products and intends to develop a commercial-scale green iron production facility.

DevelopmentWA is the Western Australian Government’s land and development agency responsible for facilitating industrial land development. DevelopmentWA develops and manages industrial land and infrastructure in Western Australia, including within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area.

If the option for lease is exercised, the parties intend to enter into a long-term industrial ground lease to enable development and operation of a green iron production facility.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap

b. there appear to be alternative sites available for Element Zero’s competitors

c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or prevent competition from rivals.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Infosys Nova Holdings LLC proposes to acquire Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc including Optimum’s subsidiary, Optimum Healthcare IT Pty Ltd.

Infosys Nova Holdings LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys Limited (**Infosys**). Infosys is a global consulting and IT services company. Infosys provides a range of professional services across consulting, application development and maintenance, testing, business process management, cybersecurity, transformation, digital, cloud services, AI and automation, and data analytics. It is a leading provider of application sustainment, development and testing services. In Australia, Infosys provides services to Government and private sector organisations including in the financial services and telecommunications sectors.

Infosys is a foreign company registered in its original jurisdiction of India. Infosys trades as Infosys Technologies Limited in Australia.

Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc is a digital transformation and consulting firm based in the US that specialises in providing services to the healthcare industry. Its broader suite of services includes digital transformation services, enterprise application services, managed services and application managed services, analytics and AI enablement, and cybersecurity and compliance.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Infosys Nova Holdings LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys Limited (Infosys), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc (Optimum Healthcare IT), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
17 April 2026

Infosys is a global consulting and IT services company registered in India. Infosys provides a range of professional services across consulting, application development and maintenance, testing, business process management, cybersecurity, transformation, digital, cloud services, AI and automation, and data analytics. In Australia, Infosys provides services to Government and private sector organisations including in the financial services and telecommunications sectors.

Optimum Healthcare IT is a digital transformation and consulting firm based in the US that specialises in providing services to the healthcare industry. Its broader suite of services includes digital transformation services, enterprise application services, managed services and application managed services, analytics and AI enablement, and cybersecurity and compliance. In Australia, Optimum Healthcare IT has limited operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Infosys and Optimum Healthcare IT in the supply of IT implementation and support services for healthcare services in Australia

b. Optimum Healthcare IT has a small presence in Australia

c. there are alternative suppliers of IT implementation and support services for healthcare services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Creative Planning Holdco, LLC (**Creative Planning**), through its wholly owned subsidiary, CP Britannia Bidco Limited, will acquire 100% of the interests in MASECO LLP (**Maseco**).

Creative Planning, headquartered in the United States, is a wealth and investment management firm that provides a financial planning led investment management approach, retirement planning, estate planning, trust services, tax planning and family office services for individuals as well as 401(k) and institutional clients. Creative Planning is jointly controlled by Peter Mallouk, TPG Inc. (**TPG**) and General Atlantic Service Company, L.P. (**GA**).

Maseco is a UK based provider of wealth management services primarily to high-net worth and ultra-high net worth UK or US citizens living in the UK or the US. Maseco has a small presence in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Creative Planning Holdco, LLC (Creative Planning), through its wholly owned subsidiary, CP Britannia Bidco Limited, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the interests in MASECO LLP (Maseco), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
22 April 2026

The acquirer, Creative Planning, headquartered in the United States, is a wealth and investment management firm that provides a financial planning led investment management approach, retirement planning, estate planning, trust services, tax planning and family office services for individuals as well as 401(k) and institutional clients.

Creative Planning is jointly controlled by Peter Mallouk, TPG Inc. (TPG) and General Atlantic Service Company, L.P. (GA).

• Peter Mallouk is the President and CEO of Creative Planning.

• TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco in 1992.

• GA is affiliated with a group of investment funds (the GA Group) that provide capital for growth companies globally.

The GA Group, headquartered in the US, provides capital and strategic support for growth companies in six sectors: climate, consumer, financial services, healthcare, life sciences, and technology.

The target, Maseco is a UK based provider of wealth management services primarily to high-net worth and ultra-high net worth UK or US citizens living in the UK or the US. Maseco's presence in Australia is small.

Both parties provide financial advice and wealth management globally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Creative Planning (and its connected entities) and Maseco in the supply of financial advice and wealth management services in Australia

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial advice and wealth management services in Australia is estimated to be low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. there are alternative suppliers of financial advice and wealth management services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
AustralianSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for AustralianSuper (**AustralianSuper**) proposes to acquire additional interests in the IFM Global Infrastructure (Australia) Trust (**IFM GIAT**), a feeder fund to the IFM Global Infrastructure Fund (**GIF Master Fund**) (the **Acquisition**).

AustralianSuper is an Australian industry superannuation and pension fund. It offers its members services relating to superannuation, investment, retirement, and insurance. It has over 3.6 million members and manages over $410 billion in funds (as at 31 December 2025).

The IFM GIAT is a fund established for the purpose of investing in the GIF Master Fund. The GIF Master Fund is a global infrastructure fund that invests in long-duration, illiquid core infrastructure assets across transport, utilities, energy transmission and digital infrastructure.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: AustralianSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for AustralianSuper (AustralianSuper) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of additional interests in the IFM Global Infrastructure (Australia) Trust (IFM GIAT), a feeder fund to the IFM Global Infrastructure Fund (GIF Master Fund), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, AustralianSuper, is an Australian industry superannuation and pension fund. It offers its members services relating to superannuation, investment, retirement, and insurance. It has over 3.6 million members and manages over $410 billion in funds (as at 31 December 2025).

The target, IFM GIAT, is a fund established for the purpose of investing in the GIF Master Fund. The GIF Master Fund is a global infrastructure fund that invests in long-duration, illiquid core infrastructure assets across transport, utilities, energy transmission and digital infrastructure.

Both AustralianSuper and GIF Master Fund have interests in Sydney Airport. AustralianSuper also has an interest in Perth Airport.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the increment to AustralianSuper’s existing interest in IFM GIAT, and the GIF Master Fund, is small

b. while there is common ownership between AustralianSuper and GIF Master Fund’s investments in Sydney Airport, the increment to AustralianSuper’s existing interest is very small

c. while there is cross-ownership between AustralianSuper’s interests in Sydney Airport and Perth Airport, there is limited competition between airports.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of New Lambton Family Practice (**NLFP**) through a business and assets acquisition agreement.

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment Pty Limited, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and New Zealand to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

The target, NLFP is a general practice medical service operating from its premises in New Lambton, New South Wales. NLFP is currently owned and operated by New Lambton Family Practice Pty Ltd and the trustee for Lual Services Trust.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Ochre Health Pty Ltd (Ochre Health) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the assets of New Lambton Family Practice (NLFP) through a business and assets acquisition agreement, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment Pty Limited, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and New Zealand to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital (Genesis) an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s investments include Holdsworth House Medical Group, which operates two general medical practices in Brisbane and Sydney, with a co-located medical research centre, specialist medical clinic and dentistry clinic at its Sydney premises.

The target, NLFP is a general practice medical service operating from its premises in New Lambton, a suburb of Newcastle in New South Wales. NLFP is currently owned and operated by New Lambton Family Practice Pty Ltd and the trustee for Lual Services Trust.

Ochre Health, through Ochre Medical Centre Kotara, and NLFP supply general practice medical services in Newcastle, NSW.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• a. the aggregation arising from the Acquisition appears low

b. there are alternative suppliers of general practice medical services in the New Lambton area, and in the broader Newcastle area in NSW.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: AUB – further interest in KJ Risk

mergers.merger_description:
Austbrokers Corporate Pty Ltd (trading as **AUC**), a directly and wholly-owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (**AUB**), proposes to acquire an additional 43% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk Group Pty Ltd (**KJ Risk**) by way of a purchase of shares from the existing majority shareholder, Niven Industries Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Niven Industries Trust (**Niven Industries**) (the **Acquisition**).

As part of the transaction, AUB Group Limited will transfer its existing 49% interest in KJ Risk to AUC. On completion, AUC will hold 92% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk, with the balance retained by Niven Industries.

AUC is a wholly owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (ASX: AUB), which itself is an ASX200 global insurance broking group with a diversified portfolio spanning General and Specialist Retail Brokers, Authorised Representative and Broking Networks, Managing General Agents and Underwriting Agencies, Wholesale Brokers, InsurTech platforms, and specialist Claims and Loss Adjustment services. Operating from approximately 580 locations, ~6,000 people support around 1.2 million clients and place more than AUD 11 billion in premiums with local and international insurers each year.

KJ Risk is an Australian risk management intermediary business. KJ Risk’s primary activities relate to discretionary mutual fund (**DMF**) services, including DMF broking and management services. KJ Risk also supplies general insurance broking services to a limited extent, including corporate and private client broking and workers compensation consulting services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Austbrokers Corporate Pty Ltd (trading as AUC), a directly and wholly-owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (AUB), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 43% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk Group Pty Ltd (KJ Risk), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

As part of the Acquisition, AUB will transfer its existing 49% interest in KJ Risk to AUC.

On completion, AUC will hold 92% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk, with the balance retained by Niven Industries Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Niven Industries Trust, which is the existing majority shareholder.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, AUC, is a wholly owned subsidiary of AUB. AUB is an ASX200 global insurance broking group with a portfolio that includes General and Specialist Retail Brokers, Authorised Representative and Broking Networks, Managing General Agents and Underwriting Agencies, Wholesale Brokers, InsurTech platforms, and specialist Claims and Loss Adjustment services.

The target, KJ Risk, is an Australian risk management intermediary business. KJ Risk’s primary activities relate to discretionary mutual fund (DMF) services, including DMF broking and management services. KJ Risk also supplies general insurance broking services, including corporate and private client broking and workers compensation consulting services.

KJ Risk operates as an integrated member of AUB, conducting its business exclusively within the AUB network and under the AUB brand, governance and operational framework.

AUB and KJ Risk overlap in the supply of general insurance broking services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

• Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between AUB (and its connected entities) and KJ Risk in the supply of general insurance broking services in Australia

b. there are alternative suppliers of general insurance broking services to customers in Australia

c. except for AUB’s existing interest in KJ Risk, AUB (and its connected entities) does not supply DMF broking or management services in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Paragon Care Limited (**Paragon Care**), proposes to acquire the Presidental business (**Presidental**) through an Asset Purchase Agreement from Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd.

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. Paragon Care established its dedicated dental consumables business in May 2025.

Presidental is an Australian-owned business focusing on the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South Wales, Brisbane and parts of regional Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies a small volume of dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated consumables to medical practices.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Paragon Care Limited (Paragon Care), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the assets of Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd (Presidental), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
16 February 2026

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. The acquirer, Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Paragon Care.

The target, Presidental, is an Australian-owned business focusing on the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South Wales, Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies some dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated consumables to medical practices.

The parties both supply dental consumables to dental surgeries and clinics. The application states that dental consumables cover a broad range of products that are typically used on a single use or limited use basis in the course of delivering dental treatment and maintaining clinical operations, such as face masks, fillings and dental instruments.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. The horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of dental consumables is small.

• b. The parties’ combined share in the supply of dental consumables is low.

c. There are a number of alternative suppliers of dental consumables in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Aluminium Specialties Group – Manufacturing facility site in Orchard Hills, NSW
mergers.merger_description:
Aluminium Specialties Group Pty Ltd (**Aluminium Specialities Group**) has entered into agreements for lease of manufacturing premises owned by Atilol Holdings Pty Ltd (**Atilol**), located on two lots in the same parcel of land in the Alspec Industrial Business Park in 221–227 Luddenham Road, Orchard Hills NSW.

Aluminium Specialities Group manufactures and extrudes aluminium systems supplies such as door and window frames through a related entity known as Alspec.

Atilol owns and leases non-residential property. Atilol is constructing the manufacturing premises in consultation with Aluminium Specialities Group.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Aluminium Specialties Group Pty Ltd (Aluminium Specialities Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed agreements for lease with Atilol Holdings Pty Ltd (Atilol) for Lots 1 and 2 of Alspec Industrial Business Park located at 221–227 Luddenham Road, Orchard Hills, NSW (the Property), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
11 March 2026

The acquirer, Aluminium Specialities Group, manufactures and extrudes aluminium systems supplies such as door and window frames through a related entity known as Alspec.

Atilol owns and leases non-residential property. Atilol is constructing the manufacturing premises on the Property in consultation with Aluminium Specialities Group and will then lease the Property to Aluminium Specialities Group.

The Property will be used by Aluminium Specialities Group and would be an input to their operations in manufacturing and distributing aluminium systems.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap

b. there appear to be alternative sites available for Aluminium Specialities Group’s competitors

c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or prevent competition from rivals.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, through its subsidiary Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (**Henkel**), proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of Stahl Parent B.V. (together with subsidiaries, **Stahl**) from Stahl Group S.A., which is indirectly controlled by affiliates of Wendel S.E. (**Wendel**).

Wendel is headquartered in Paris, France, and is a private assets-focused investment firm listed at the Euronext Paris stock exchange.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and Consumer Brands business.

Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a diverse portfolio of consumer brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat and Bref.

Stahl is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings, and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility (automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle, and home furnishing industries.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (Henkel), which is a subsidiary of Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the share capital in Stahl Parent B.V. (together with its subsidiaries, Stahl), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Henkel, is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and Consumer Brands business. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt.

Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a portfolio of consumer brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat and Bref.

The target, Stahl, is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings, and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility (automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle, and home furnishing.

In Australia, the parties both supply barrier (interior) coatings for paper packaging and laminating adhesives for flexible packaging to industrial customers, which are subsets of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives respectively.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• there is a small horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives

• the parties combined market shares in the supply of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives are estimated to be low

• there are alternative providers of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives in Australia, including in the supply of the subsets of these products in which the parties overlap

• there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

The acquirer, Idemitsu, is a Japanese company oil-refining and marketing company. It has been involved in the development, production, and sale of coal at several mines in Australia. Idemitsu currently operates the Boggabri Coal Mine in NSW. Idemitsu has invested in lithium and vanadium development companies, renewables and acquired a fuel oil retail business.

MidOcean Energy is a global liquid natural gas (LNG) platform formed and managed by EIG Partners in 2023. In Australia, MidOcean holds minority interests in the Queensland Curtis LNG Project, Gorgon LNG Project and Pluto LNG Project. These projects are involved in the extraction and production of natural gas and LNG.

determination_table_content.details:
IDEMITSU EUROPE LTD., (together with its subsidiaries, Idemitsu) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of share capital in MidOcean Holdings II, L.P, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

MidOcean Holdings II, L.P. indirectly owns MidOcean Energy LLC (MidOcean Energy).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
10 April 2026

The acquirer, Idemitsu, is a Japanese company oil-refining and marketing company. It has been involved in the development, production, and sale of coal at several mines in Australia. Idemitsu currently operates the Boggabri Coal Mine in NSW. Idemitsu has invested in lithium and vanadium development companies, renewables and acquired a fuel oil retail business.

MidOcean Energy is a global liquid natural gas (LNG) platform formed and managed by EIG Partners in 2023. In Australia, MidOcean holds minority interests in the Queensland Curtis LNG Project, Gorgon LNG Project and Pluto LNG Project. These projects are involved in the extraction and production of natural gas and LNG.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Idemistu and MidOcean Energy in the production of natural gas or LNG in Australia

b. there are alternative producers of natural gas or LNG in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
On 5 March 2026, Advanced Navigation Pty. Ltd (**AdNav**), several existing shareholders of AdNav, and several new investors, entered into a sale and subscription deed (**SSD**). Under the SSD, Airtree Ventures Opportunity Fund V Partnership and its affiliates, National Reconstruction Fund Corporation, Australian Retirement Trust, Franklin Park Venture Capital Opportunity Fund II, L.P., LGPE SEC S.C.Sp, Crown Growth Opportunities IV SCSp-RAIF, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity V Master SCSp, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity VI Alt S.C.Sp., and Harvard Private Capital Realty Inc (collectively, the **New Investors**) along with a variety of other investors will directly or indirectly (through a separate nominee vehicle or trust) acquire shares of AdNav and enter into a shareholders agreement (the **Acquisition**).

AdNav develops, manufacturers and offers AI powered navigation solutions, primarily for defence primes.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Advanced Navigation Pty. Ltd (AdNav), certain shareholders of AdNav, and certain new investors, entered into a sale and subscription deed (SSD). Under the SSD, Airtree Ventures Opportunity Fund V Partnership and its affiliates (Airtree), National Reconstruction Fund Corporation (NRF), a number of co-investors, along with a variety of other investors propose to directly or indirectly (through a separate nominee vehicle or trust) acquire shares of AdNav and enter into a shareholders agreement (the Acquisition).

Airtree applied for a notification waiver on behalf of the co-investors and in respect of the co-investors’ acquisition of shares of AdNav.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Airtree is a limited partnership that is managed by Airtree Ventures Fund V Manager Pty Ltd, an entity which forms part of Airtree Ventures, a venture capital firm based in Sydney, Australia. Airtree Ventures primarily focuses on early-stage investments in technology startups across Australia and New Zealand.

The NRF is an Australian Government specialist investment vehicle, established in 2023 under the National Reconstruction Fund Corporation Act 2023. The NRF’s investment function is to provide finance in the form of debt, equity and guarantees for projects in 7 priority areas of the Australian economy.

The following parties are acquiring shares in AdNav as part of the Acquisition alongside Airtree and NRF (referred to as co-investors).

• Australian Retirement Trust is one of Australia’s largest superannuation funds, managing in the order of $350 billion of retirement savings for more than 2.4 million members and has a diverse global portfolio.

• Franklin Park Venture Capital Opportunity Fund II, L.P is a venture capital fund managed by Franklin Park, which is a US based investment manager and adviser that specialises in private equity, private debt and venture capital, primarily through fund‑of‑funds, co‑investment vehicles and bespoke advisory mandates.

• LGPE SEC S.C.Sp, Crown Growth Opportunities IV SCSp-RAIF, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity V Master SCSp and Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity VI Alt S.C.Sp are global investment firms and are part of the LGT Capital Partners Group. LGT Capital Partners are involved in managing capital on behalf of institutional investors, sovereign entities, insurers, endowments and foundations.

• Harvard Private Capital Realty, Inc is an investment entity associated with Harvard Management Company, a nonprofit, wholly owned subsidiary of Harvard University. Its activities are limited to managing and investing endowment assets across a diversified portfolio of asset classes.

The target, AdNav, develops, manufacturers and offers AI powered navigation solutions, primarily for defence primes.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Airtree, NRF and the co-investors will have a relatively small interest in AdNav post-Acquisition

b. there is no horizontal overlap, and limited potential vertical relationships, between the co-investors and their connected entities and AdNav in the supply of AI powered navigation software in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Signant Health Holding Corp (**Signant**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in ActiGraph Holdings, LLC (together with its subsidiaries, **ActiGraph**).

Signant is a US-based evidence generation company that provides services used in clinical trials. Signant does not have a physical presence in Australia but provides electronic clinical outcome assessments (**eCOA**), clinical trial randomisation and supply management (**RTSM**) services, and supplementary clinical trial professional services. Signant supplies these services to both contract research organisations (**CROs**) and directly to pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies.

Ametris (formerly ActiGraph) is a US-based provider of digital health technology (**DHT**) for clinical trials, specialising in wearables – in particular, wrist-worn wearables and other sensors – which track biovitals and other digital endpoints for patients participating in clinical trials. Ametris does not have a physical presence in Australia but supplies its DHT products in Australia for non-commercial academic research.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Signant Health Holding Corp (together with its subsidiaries, Signant) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in ActiGraph Holdings, LLC (together with its subsidiaries, Ametris (formerly ActiGraph)), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
16 April 2026

The acquirer, Signant, is a US-based evidence generation company that provides services used in clinical trials. Signant does not have a physical presence in Australia but provides electronic clinical outcome assessments (eCOAs), a data collection service for clinical trials, clinical trial randomisation and supply management (RTSM) services, and supplementary clinical trial professional services.

Signant supplies these services to both contract research organisations (CROs) and directly to pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies (together referred to as Sponsors).

The target, Ametris, is a US-based provider of digital health technology (DHT) for clinical trials. Ametris specialises in wearables – in particular, wrist worn wearables and other sensors – which track biovitals and other digital endpoints for patients participating in clinical trials. Ametris does not have a physical presence in Australia but supplies its DHT products in Australia for non-commercial academic research.

Both Signant and Ametris supply services used to collect digital endpoints in clinical trials.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• there is limited overlap between the parties in the supply of digital endpoint collection services in Australia, as their respective offerings primarily serve different use cases for clinical trials

• the parties each have a small Australian presence

• there are alternative suppliers of the types of digital endpoint collection services offered by the parties in Australia

• there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, or bundling or tying of services, post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition concerns the indirect acquisition of sole control of Nippon Sheet Glass Company, Limited (**Nippon**) by funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital Management, L.P., which is a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (together with its indirect subsidiaries, **Apollo**). Apollo and the investment funds managed by affiliates of Apollo are together referred to as the Apollo Funds.

Apollo is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City and incorporated in the US. Through its fully integrated investment platform spanning the full risk-reward spectrum from investment grade to private equity, Apollo offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors on a global basis. Examples of Apollo’s current investments include, inter alia, companies in education, insurance, financial services, and leisure businesses.

Nippon Sheet Glass is a global manufacturer and supplier of a wide range of glass products, headquartered in Tokyo. It operates across three global business units: architectural glass, automotive glass, and creative technology glass (or technical glass).

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Lumina Japan Acquisition Co., Ltd (Lumina Japan), a special purpose vehicle of funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital Management, L.P., applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed indirect acquisition of sole control of Nippon Sheet Glass Company, Limited (Nippon Sheet Glass), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Lumina Japan, is a special purpose vehicle of funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital Management, L.P., which is a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (together with its indirect subsidiaries, Apollo). For the purposes of this document, Apollo and the investment funds managed by Apollo’s affiliates are referred to as the Apollo Funds.

Apollo is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City and incorporated in the US. It offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors on a global basis. Examples of Apollo’s current investments include, inter alia, companies in education, insurance, financial services, and leisure businesses.

The target, Nippon Sheet Glass, is a global manufacturer and supplier of a wide range of glass products, operating across three global business units: architectural glass, automotive glass, and creative technology glass (or technical glass).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between the Apollo Funds’ controlled portfolio companies and Nippon Sheet Glass in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Australian Venue Co – assets of White Cockatoo Hotel, Petersham
mergers.merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (**AVC**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets of the licensed hotel trading as the White Cockatoo Hotel (including the exclusive right to carry on the business in succession of the vendor and the benefit of the lease) from Petersham Pub Pty Ltd (**Vendor**).

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates over 200 licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The White Cockatoo Hotel is a contemporary licensed hotel located at 30 Terminus Street, Petersham NSW 2049. The venue is a typical Australian pub with a public bar, bistro and outdoor courtyard. The business assets of White Cockatoo Hotel are currently owned and operated by the Vendor as part of Reilly Group.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited (Australian Venue Co), via AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of substantially all of the assets of the licensed hotel trading as the White Cockatoo Hotel (WCH) (including the exclusive right to carry on the business in succession of the vendor and the benefit of the lease) from Petersham Pub Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, AVC, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Australian Venue Co operates over 200 licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. Their venues are individually branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The target, WCH, is a licensed hotel with no gaming facilities, located at 30 Terminus Street, Petersham NSW 2049. WCH facilities include a public bar, bistro and outdoor courtyard.

Australian Venue Co, through its venues (including Public House Petersham), and WCH both supply retail hospitality services in the local area surrounding WCH.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

• while there is horizontal overlap between Australian Venue Co and WCH, the combined share of the parties in the supply of retail hospitality services is low

• there are alternative suppliers of retail hospitality venues in the local area surrounding WCH.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

BPEA Private Equity Fund IX (**BPEA IX**), through the Japanese special purpose vehicle Kamgras 1 K.K., proposes to acquire all issued common shares and share options in Kakaku.com, Inc. (**Kakaku.com**), a Japanese listed company.

BPEA IX is a private investment fund controlled by EQT Fund Management S.à r.l., its alternative investment fund manager.
