mergers.merger_description:
The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. The Michelin Group is the owner of UK-based Fenner PLC, which includes Fenner America, Inc. Fenner is a manufacturer of engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer products.

Globally, the Michelin Group's activities fall into tyres, polymer composite solutions, data and AI based services for professional mobility and transport operators, and the provision of curated travel, dining and hospitality guides in printed and digital format (including the Michelin Guide). As part of the Michelin Group's polymer composite solutions, it manufactures and sells hydraulic seals that are engineered for dynamic sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between moving parts such as pistons and rods). Customers in this area are predominantly hydraulic related, with products being used in the manufacture and repair of hydraulic cylinders across a range of industries.

Flexitallic is a global manufacturer and supplier of high-performance sealing solutions that are engineered for static sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between stationary parts such as flanged pipe joints and similar joints that do not move or rotate), specifically spiral wound gaskets, flat gaskets and related materials. Flexitallic's products are used in stationary pipeline connections and joints unrelated to cylinders. Flexitallic is headquartered in Paris, France, and has a large global network.

Flexitallic established a local presence through the acquisition of Novus Sealing in 2022. In Australia, Flexitallic focuses on serving the oil and gas, mining, petrochemical, and power generation industries.

Michelin is proposing to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Flexitallic through two steps:

* First, Fenner America, Inc. will acquire 100% of the shares in FGI Acquisition Corp. from The Flexitallic Group SAS.
* Second, Compagnie Financiere Michelin SAS will acquire 100% of the shares in the The Flexitallic Group SAS from Flexitallic, S.à r.l., the parent of the The Flexitallic Group SAS.

Together, these steps will result in Michelin acquiring full ownership of the Flexitallic.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Compagnie Financière Michelin SAS (Michelin) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in The Flexitallic Group SAS (Flexitallic), through the following two steps, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

Specifically, the Acquisition comprises the following steps:

• First, Fenner America, Inc. (Fenner) (a wholly owned entity of Michelin) will acquire 100% of the shares in FGI Acquisition Corp. (a wholly owned entity of Flexitallic).

• Second, Michelin will acquire 100% of the shares in Flexitallic from Flexitallic, S.à r.l., the parent of Flexitallic.

The Acquisition will result in the Michelin Group acquiring full ownership of Flexitallic.

Michelin and Fenner applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
27 March 2026

The acquirers, Michelin and Fenner, are part of the Michelin Group.

The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. Fenner is a manufacturer of engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer products.

Globally, the Michelin Group's activities include fall into the following four areas: tyres; polymer composite solutions (development of polymer composite solutions and other high-tech materials for critical applications in industrial applications, mobility, construction, aeronautics, energy, healthcare and mining; connected solutions (provision of data and AI based services for professional mobility and transport operators); and lifestyle (provision of curated travel, dining and hospitality guides in printed and digital format).

As part of the Michelin Group's ‘polymer composite solutions’, it manufactures and sells hydraulic seals that are engineered for dynamic sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between moving parts such as pistons and rods). In Australia, Michelin supplies sealing solutions via Hallite Seals Australia.

The target, Flexitallic, is a global manufacturer and supplier of high-performance sealing solutions that are engineered for static sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between stationary parts such as flanged pipe joints and similar joints that do not move or rotate), specifically spiral wound gaskets, flat gaskets and related materials.

Flexitallic's products are used in stationary pipeline connections and joints unrelated to cylinders. Flexitallic is headquartered in Paris, France and has a large global network.

In Australia, Flexitallic focuses on serving the oil and gas, mining, petrochemical, and power generation industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Michelin and Flexitallic in the manufacture and supply of sealing solutions in Australia

b. the presence of alternative manufacturers and suppliers of sealing solutions in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Coles – land around supermarket site in Balaclava, VIC
mergers.merger_description:
Coles Group Limited (**Coles**) is an Australian public company (ASX: COL) that primarily operates:

* supermarkets nationwide through its subsidiary, Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, and
* retail liquor stores nationwide, trading under the Liquorland, Liquorland Warehouse and Liquorland Cellars brands.

Coles also has a property development arm, Coles Group Property Developments Ltd (**CGPD**), that buys land and constructs shopping centres, standalone supermarkets and/or liquor outlets and then sells these completed sites to third party buyers.

The Port Phillip City Council (**Council**) is the local government authority overseeing the City of Port Phillip, an area in inner Melbourne including the suburbs of Albert Park, Balaclava, Elwood, part of the Melbourne CBD, Middle Park, part of Port Melbourne, Ripponlea, South Melbourne, Southbank, St Kilda, St Kilda East, St Kilda West and Windsor.

Coles currently operates a full line supermarket and a Liquorland store at the intersection of Alfred Street and Camden Street, Balaclava VIC 3183. Coles owns the land on which these stores are situated, along with several adjoining parcels (including land leased to other retailers and sections of the carpark behind the supermarket).

Balaclava is a suburb within the City of Port Phillip local government area in Victoria, situated approximately 8 kilometres from Melbourne’s CBD.

The Council has offered to sell Coles a number of adjacent Council-owned parcels of land located at Nelson Street and Alfred Street to facilitate a significant revitalisation of the Balaclava precinct. This will involve the development of a new mixed-use retail and residential precinct. CGPD intends to enter into an unconditional contract of sale with the Council (the **Acquisition**).

The land proposed for sale includes part of the carpark adjacent to the Coles supermarket area enclosed by Carlisle, Alfred, Nelson, and Camden Streets, and some laneways that will be discontinued, which is approximately 2,240 sqm of land in total.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Coles Group Property Developments Limited (CGPD), a property development arm of Coles Group Limited (Coles), applied for a notification waiver in respect of CGPD’s proposed acquisition of parcels of land located at Nelson Street and Alfred Street, adjacent to the Coles supermarket in Balaclava, Victoria, from the City of Port Phillip, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
23 April 2026

Coles is an Australian public company (ASX: COL) that primarily operates:

• supermarkets nationwide, and

• retail liquor stores nationwide, trading under the Liquorland, Liquorland Warehouse and Liquorland Cellars brands.

Coles has a property development arm, CGPD, that buys land and constructs shopping centres, standalone supermarkets and/or liquor outlets and then sells these completed sites to third party buyers.

The vendor, the Port Phillip City Council, is the local government authority overseeing the City of Port Phillip, an area in inner Melbourne including the suburbs of Albert Park, Balaclava, Elwood, part of the Melbourne CBD, Middle Park, part of Port Melbourne, Ripponlea, South Melbourne, Southbank, St Kilda, St Kilda East, St Kilda West and Windsor.

The target land proposed for sale includes part of the carpark adjacent to the Coles supermarket area enclosed by Carlisle, Alfred, Nelson, and Camden Streets, and some laneways that will be discontinued.

The freehold interest would be an input to Coles’ supermarket retailing operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Coles currently owns the land on which its supermarket in Balaclava is located and the adjacent land that is the subject of the Acquisition is unlikely to be able to accommodate a new supermarket

b. the land that is proposed to be acquired will be used to improve the quality of the offering of Coles’ existing supermarket in Balaclava

c. there are several alternative grocery retailers, including Woolworths, ALDI and IGA, and retail suppliers of packaged liquor for offsite consumption, in and around the local area.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) being met. The ACCC does however observe that the targeted notification requirements for supermarkets determined under section 51ABQ(1) are likely to apply.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The acquirer is ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd. Export Trading Group Australia Pty Ltd (**ETG Australia**) is the sole shareholder of ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd. ETG Australia is a wholesale supplier (including exporter) of certain pulses and grains in Australia.

Queensland Cotton Corporation Pty Ltd (**Queensland Cotton**) operates a business of trading, conditioning, storing and grading certain pulses and grains, and is a wholesale supplier (including exporter) of certain pulses and grains in Australia.

Queensland Cotton owns and operates a receival, grading and container packing facility located in Mount Tyson QLD 4356 (**Mount Tyson Facility**). The Mount Tyson Facility processes, grades, stores and packs chickpeas, mung beans and sorghum for Queensland Cotton, which Queensland Cotton then supplies domestically and overseas.

ETG Australia (through ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd) is proposing to acquire certain business assets (including land) relating to the operation of the Mount Tyson Facility from Queensland Cotton. After the acquisition, ETG Australia proposes to operate the Mount Tyson Facility to process, grade, store and pack pulses and grains for supplying by ETG Australia both domestically and overseas.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Export Trading Group Australia Pty Ltd (ETG Australia), proposes to acquire certain business assets (including land) relating to the operation of a facility located in Mount Tyson QLD 4356 (Mount Tyson Facility) from Queensland Cotton Corporation Pty Ltd (Queensland Cotton), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
28 April 2026

ETG Australia is the sole shareholder of ETG Queensland Processing Pty Ltd and is a wholesale supplier (including exporter) of certain pulses and grains in Australia.

Queensland Cotton operates a business of trading, conditioning, storing and grading certain pulses and grains and is a wholesale supplier (including exporter) of certain pulses and grains in Australia.

Queensland Cotton owns and operates the Mount Tyson Facility, a receival, grading and container packing facility. The Mount Tyson Facility processes, grades, stores and packs pulses and grains owned by Queensland Cotton, which Queensland Cotton then supplies domestically and overseas.

ETG Australia and Queensland Cotton are both wholesale suppliers of pulses and grains, focussing on chickpeas, mung beans and sorghum. Queensland Cotton will continue supply of trading, conditioning, storing and grading of sorghum, as well as other grains such as barley and wheat.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between ETG Australia and the pulses and grains business and facility operated by Queensland Cotton at the Mount Tyson Facility in the supply of pulses and grains, including chickpeas, mung beans and sorghum in Australia

b. there are alternative wholesale suppliers of pulses and grains in Australia.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

determination_table_content.details:
MAHF Custodian Pty Ltd, as custodian for RGM TT Pty Ltd (as trustee for The Entrance Hotel Sub Trust) and Redcape Hotel Group Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the freehold land interest, buildings, licences and business assets of The Entrance Social Club located in The Entrance, NSW, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

30 April 2026

The acquirer, MAHF Custodian Pty Ltd, is the custodian for RGM TT Pty Ltd (as trustee for The Entrance Hotel Sub Trust) and Redcape Hotel Goup Pty Ltd. The acquirers are part of MA Financial’s Redcape Fund, which owns and operates community pubs and hotels. The Redcape Fund portfolio currently has 34 pubs and hotels in NSW, Queensland and Victoria.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Metro Petroleum – Retail sites part of divestiture remedy from Ampol-EG acquisition
mergers.merger_description:
Dib Group Pty Limited (**Dib Group**) proposes to acquire 41 retail fuel sites from Ampol Retail Holding Pty Ltd , a wholly owned subsidiary of Ampol Limited (**Ampol**), as part of Ampol’s proposed remedy offered to the ACCC in relation to its acquisition of all of the issued shares in each of EG Group Australia Pty Ltd and EG AsiaPac Holdings Pty Ltd (**Ampol EG Acquisition**).

The acquirer, Dib Group, is an independent fuel retailer which operates a network of over 300 retail fuel and convenience sites across New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland and Western Australia, under the Metro Petroleum (**Metro**) brand. Metro is also involved in wholesale petroleum supply, fuel logistics and distribution, and related petroleum industry activities.

The vendor, Ampol, is a vertically integrated fuel company with upstream fuel production and importing assets, wholesale supply and distribution facilities and a network of retail fuel and convenience sites across Australia.

Ampol owns the Lytton refinery in Brisbane, Queensland, where it refines imported crude oil, and a number of terminals nationwide through which it imports finished petrol products from overseas refineries and trading houses.

Ampol operates retail sites under both the Ampol brand and the U-GO brand. Ampol branded sites are full-service sites with fuel and convenience offerings. Launched in 2023, the U-GO branded sites are low cost, unstaffed self-service sites selling only fuel and with no convenience offering. Ampol owns and operates 576 sites under the Ampol brand, and 46 under the U-GO brand.

Metro and Ampol are both active in the retail and wholesale supply of fuel, and the retail supply of convenience products in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Dib Group Pty Limited (Dib Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 41 retail fuel sites from Ampol Retail Holding Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Ampol Limited (Ampol), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition arises from Ampol’s remedy offer to the ACCC in respect of Ampol’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in EG Group Australia Pty LTD (Ampol EG Acquisition), which includes the divestiture of 41 retail fuel sites to an ACCC-approved purchaser.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
3 June 2026

The acquirer, Dib Group, is an independent fuel retailer that operates a network of over 300 retail fuel and convenience sites across New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland and Western Australia, under the Metro Petroleum (Metro) brand. Dib Group is also involved in wholesale petroleum supply, fuel logistics and distribution, and related petroleum industry activities.

The vendor, Ampol, is a vertically integrated fuel company with upstream fuel production and importing assets, wholesale supply and distribution facilities, and a network of retail fuel and convenience sites across Australia.

Ampol owns the Lytton refinery in Brisbane, Queensland, where it refines imported crude oil, and a number of terminals nationwide through which it imports finished petrol products from overseas refineries and trading houses.

Ampol operates retail sites under both the Ampol brand and the U-GO brand. Ampol branded sites are full-service sites with fuel and convenience offerings. Launched in 2023, the U-GO branded sites are low cost, unstaffed self-service sites selling only fuel and with no convenience offering. Ampol owns and operates 576 sites under the Ampol brand, and 46 under the U-GO brand.

Metro and Ampol overlap in the retail and wholesale supply of fuel, and the retail supply of convenience products in Australia. Of the 41 sites being acquired in 39 local markets, there are 4 local markets where a Metro owned and operated site overlaps with one of the target sites (Belrose, Raymond Terrace, Jerrabomberra, and Childers).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application (which includes a copy of the Proposed Purchaser Notice provided by Ampol in connection with its 22 April 2026 remedy offer in respect of the Ampol-EG Acquisition) and other relevant information available to it. The ACCC has also had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, and noting the ACCC’s separate analysis of overlaps between Dib Group and the target sites as part of its consideration of that Proposed Purchaser Notice for the ACCC’s assessment of the Ampol EG Acquisition, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition.

In particular, in 35 local markets, the Acquisition would result in new entry. In respect of the remaining 4 local markets where there is overlap, Dib Group would be competitively constrained by a number of alternate fuel retailers and convenience retailers.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (**Henkel**) proposes to acquire all the shares in Aqua Adhesives I GmbH (**Aqua Adhesives**). Through that, Henkel will acquire control of Aqua Adhesives’ subsidiary, ATP adhesive systems Group GmbH (**ATP**).

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active worldwide through: (i) Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit, and (ii) Henkel’s Consumer Brands business unit.  

Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftsmen. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt.

Henkel’s Consumer Brands business unit offers a diverse portfolio of consumer brands with a focus on two main categories: Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Henkel’s consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat, and Bref, among others.

Aqua Adhesives is the parent company of ATP, which is a private company headquartered in Wollerau, Switzerland. ATP manufactures pressure-sensitive adhesive tapes for various end uses. ATP’s business is primarily focused on Europe and North America.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (Henkel), via Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V., applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Aqua Adhesives I GmbH (Aqua Adhesives), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Henkel, is active worldwide through: (i) Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit; and (ii) Henkel’s Consumer Brands business unit. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftsmen.

Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. In Australia, Henkel supplies tapes and labels (T&L) adhesives, and non-water based pressure-sensitive adhesive (PSA) tapes.

The target, Aqua Adhesives, is the parent company of ATP adhesive systems Group GmbH (ATP). ATP manufactures PSA tapes for industrial customers, for various end uses such as: (i) automotive/mobility; (ii) medical; (iii) electronics; (iv) building & construction; and (v) graphic applications, among others. In Australia, ATP supplies water based PSA tapes.

Henkel and ATP potentially overlap in Australia in the supply of PSA tapes. In addition to the potential horizontal overlap, there is a vertical relationship between PSA tapes and T&L adhesives. T&L adhesives are used for the production of PSA tapes.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, including the revenue of the parties in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:

a. The extent of any horizontal overlap between Henkel and ATP in the supply of PSA tapes in Australia.

b. The vertical relationship between the products supplied by Henkel and ATP and the likely impact of the Acquisition on the parties’ ability and/or incentive to foreclose rivals.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Cielo Topco Limited, through its wholly owned subsidiary Kee Safety (Australia) Pty Ltd (**Kee Safety**), proposes to acquire RISSafety Enterprises Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (together, **RIS Safety**).

Kee Safety provides working at height solutions enabling safe access for building, plant and equipment maintenance outside Australia including throughout the United Kingdom and the United States. Safe access solutions include design, installation, inspection and training of permanent and personal fall protection equipment.

Kee Safety does not currently conduct any business in Australia.

RIS Safety provides working at height solution goods and services throughout all states and territories in Australia, operating as RISSafety, Safety Plus, Hallweld Safety and Sydney Safety Training.

determination_table_content.details: Kee Safety (Australia) Pty Ltd (Kee Safety), a wholly owned subsidiary of Cielo Topco Limited, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in RISSafety Enterprises Pty Ltd (RIS Safety), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
13 February 2026

The acquirer, Kee Safety, provides working at height solutions enabling safe access for building, plant and equipment maintenance outside Australia including throughout the United Kingdom and the United States. Safe access solutions include design, installation, inspection and training of permanent and personal fall protection equipment.

The target, RIS Safety, provides working at height solution goods and services throughout all states and territories in Australia, operating as RISSafety, Safety Plus, Hallweld Safety and Sydney Safety Training.

While both parties provide working at height solutions, Kee Safety does not currently conduct any business in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application and on publicly available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, a) There is no horizontal or vertical overlap between Kee Safety and RIS Safety in the supply of working at height solutions in Australia. b) There are several alternative suppliers of working at height solutions operating nationally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Honda Motor Co., Ltd (**Honda**) proposes to acquire an additional 21% of issued shares in Astemo, Ltd (**Astemo**) from Hitachi, Ltd. (**Hitachi)** (the **Acquisition**). On 16 December 2025, Honda and Hitachi entered into a share purchase agreement for this purpose.

Astemo was a company integrated and merged as a jointly owned company by Honda and Hitachi. Now, Honda, Hitachi and JIC Capital, Ltd (**JICC**) hold 40%, 40% and 20% of issued shares in Astemo respectively. The Acquisition will result in:

* changes to shareholding in Astemo to 61% for Honda and 19% for Hitachi, with JICC remaining at 20%; and
* the control of Astemo shifting from joint control by Honda and Hitachi, to sole control by Honda.

Honda is a Japanese automotive company headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and the New York Stock Exchange. Honda is active in the production and supply of automotives, motorcycles, marine engines and a range of power products. It also has operations in robotics and aviation.

In Australia, Honda supplies passenger cars, motorcycles, marine engines and power equipment including generators and lawn equipment and engines.

Astemo is a Japanese automotive parts manufacturer headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Astemo is engaged in the development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts as well as transportation and industrial machinery and systems. Astemo’s products include:

* technologies for software-defined vehicles and autonomous driving and advanced driver assistance systems
* parts for powertrain, chassis and motorcycle systems
* certain components for power products and industrial equipment.

In Australia, Astemo supplies the parts for brake, suspension and steering as replacement parts. These are manufactured outside Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Honda Motor Co., Ltd. (Honda) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of an additional 21% of issued shares in Astemo, Ltd. (Astemo), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

As a result of the Acquisition, Honda will acquire sole control of Astemo.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
31 March 2026

The acquirer, Honda, is a Japanese automotive company headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and the New York Stock Exchange. Honda is active in the production and supply of automotives, motorcycles, marine engines and a range of power products. It also has operations in robotics and aviation. In Australia, Honda supplies automotives (i.e. passenger cars), motorcycles, marine engines and power equipment including generators and lawn equipment and engines.

The target, Astemo, is a Japanese automotive parts manufacturer headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Astemo is engaged in the development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts as well as transportation and industrial machinery and systems.

Astemo’s products include:

• technologies for software-defined vehicles and autonomous driving and advanced driver assistance systems

• parts for powertrain, chassis and motorcycle systems

• certain components for power products and industrial equipment.

Astemo is currently jointly owned by Honda, Hitachi, Ltd. and JIC Capital, Ltd.

In Australia, Astemo supplies parts for brake, suspension and steering as replacement parts. These are manufactured outside Australia.

Globally, Honda acquires certain component parts for passenger cars and other vehicles from Astemo. Honda and Astemo also supply some competing or complementary products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Astemo has a small presence in Australia

b. there are alternative suppliers of parts for brake, suspension and steering as replacement parts in Australia

c. while there is a vertical supply relationship between the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
TWG Hunter Dealerships Pty Ltd (**TWG**) is acquiring 95% of the share capital of Sharton Motors Pty Ltd (**Sharton Motors**), and the associated freehold properties (currently held by N&M Burton Pty Ltd).

Sharton Motors is in the business of selling and servicing Toyota, GWM/Haval vehicles and the associated sales of parts and accessories under the name of Sharton Motors Pty Ltd at the following sites:

* 70 Port Stephens Drive, Taylors Beach
* 104 - 110 Newcastle Street, East Maitland
* 124 Newcastle Street, East Maitland
* Union Street, East Maitland
* 83 - 87 Melbourne Street, East Maitland
* 75 Melbourne Street, East Maitland
* 91 Melbourne Street, East Maitland
* 322 Main Road, Cardiff

The Freehold Properties will be purchased by the TWG directly from N&M Burton Pty Ltd.

Both the TWG and Sharton Motors are in the business of selling new and used vehicles in Australia.

The acquisition will involve:

1. the vendors each selling their entire existing interests in Sharton Motors to TWG, with the effect that TWG will acquire 95% of Sharton Motors, which operates Toyota and GWM/Haval motor vehicle dealerships at locations in Maitland, Port Stephens and  
   
   Cardiff; and
2. N&M Burton Pty Ltd selling their associated freehold properties to TWG.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
TWG Hunter Dealerships Pty Ltd (TWG) and the Trustee for White King Corporate Unit Trust applied for a notification waiver in respect of TWG’s proposed acquisition of:

• 95% of the share capital in Sharton Motors Pty Ltd (Sharton Motors); and

• the associated freehold properties (currently held by N&M Burton Pty Ltd), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
14 April 2026

The acquirer, TWG, is in the business of selling new and used cars in Australia. Within NSW, TWG operates dealerships in Albion Park, Coffs Harbour, Lavington, Albury, Cardiff, Rutherford, North Wollongong, Maryville, Raymond Terrace, Hamilton East, Hamilton, Newcastle West, and Orange.

Specifically in the Newcastle and Hunter Valley region of NSW, TWG operates several used and new car dealerships under the following dealership groups: Klosters Motor Group, Cardiff Motor Group and Highways Group. TWG dealerships include Ford, Honda, Hyundai, Nissan, Suzuki, Volkswagen, BMW, Chery, Jaecoo, Mitsubishi, MINI, Zeekr, and JAC.

The target, Sharton Motors, operates Toyota and GWM/Haval car dealerships in the Hunter Valley region of NSW, including the associated sales of parts and accessories, and servicing. Sharton Motors is owned by N&M Burton Pty Ltd.

Sharton Motors Pty Ltd operates at the following sites:

• 70 Port Stephens Drive, Taylors Beach

• 104 - 110 Newcastle Street, East Maitland

• 124 Newcastle Street, East Maitland

• Union Street, East Maitland

• 83 - 87 Melbourne Street, East Maitland

• 75 Melbourne Street, East Maitland

• 91 Melbourne Street, East Maitland

• 322 Main Road, Cardiff.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, including Sharton Motors’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds are likely to be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties. In particular:

a. there is horizontal overlap between TWG and Sharton Motors in the supply of new and used cars in the Newcastle and Hunter Valley region of NSW, and the extent of competition (including any inter-brand competition) between the parties is unclear

b. TWG’s and Sharton Motors’ combined share of the supply of new cars in areas within the Newcastle and Hunter Valley region, including Maitland and Lake Macquarie, may be significant

c. there is uncertainty regarding the extent to which the parties are likely to face intra-brand and inter-brand competition from alternative suppliers of new cars in the Newcastle and Hunter Valley region.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
East Gate Games Investment Company (**East Gate**) will acquire 100% of the shares of Moonton Holdings Limited (**Moonton**) from Bytedance Ltd. (**Bytedance**), by way of a Sale and Purchase Agreement (the **Acquisition**).

East Gate is a special purpose vehicle wholly owned by Saudi Electronic Games Holding Company (doing business as Savvy Games Group (**Savvy**)). Savvy principally invests in and manages companies that develop, market, and publish games for mobile devices.

Savvy is wholly owned by the Public Investment Fund, the sovereign wealth fund of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Moonton (a wholly owned subsidiary of Bytedance) is active in mobile game development, publishing and related business. Bytedance is a holding company registered in the Cayman Islands.

Savvy and Moonton are involved in the development and publishing of games for mobile devices, and Savvy is also active in organising esports events.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: East Gate Games Investment Company (East Gate), a special purpose vehicle wholly owned by Saudi Electronic Games Holding Company (doing business as Savvy Games Group (Savvy)), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares of Moonton Holdings Limited (Moonton), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
29 April 2026

The acquirer, East Gate, is a special purpose vehicle wholly owned by Savvy. Savvy principally invests in and manages companies that develop, market, and publish games for mobile devices.

Savvy is wholly owned by the Public Investment Fund (PIF), the sovereign wealth fund of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Moonton (a wholly owned subsidiary of Bytedance Ltd. (Bytedance)) is active in mobile game development, publishing and related business. Bytedance is a holding company registered in the Cayman Islands.

Savvy and Moonton are involved in the development and publishing of games for mobile devices, and Savvy is also active in organising esports events.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of mobile video games in Australia is low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

b. there are alternative developers and publishers of mobile video games in Australia

c. while there is a potential vertical relationship between the parties in relation to esports, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The parties to this acquisition are BHP Olympic Dam Corporation Pty Ltd, which is ultimately 100% owned by BHP Group Limited (**BHP**), and the State of South Australia.

BHP is an Australian headquartered natural resources company. BHP is a global leading producer of iron ore, copper and metallurgical coal. Relevantly, BHP operates the Olympic Dam Mine in South Australia, which is one of most significant global mineral deposits.

The State of South Australia, through the Department of Energy and Mining, is responsible for regulating mining rights in South Australia and owns/manages the underlying legal rights to the minerals. It does not compete with BHP in relation to the extraction or supply of any of the minerals extracted from the Olympic Dam precinct.

BHP proposes to acquire mining rights over an additional area of land located in Olympic Dam, South Australia from the State of South Australia. The additional area (SML1 Additional Area) is adjacent to BHP’s existing ‘Special Mining Lease 1’ (**SML1**) at Olympic Dam.

The acquisition, by way of expansion of the area of existing SML1, is for the purpose of a proposal by BHP to increase mining activity and potentially expand the existing mineral processing facilities at Olympic Dam. The acquisition also provides BHP with additional non-proprietary rights in relation to its operations on SML1.

The main mineral mined at Olympic Dam is copper, with gold, silver and uranium as by-products.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: BHP Olympic Dam Corporation Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of BHP Group Limited (BHP), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of mining rights over an additional area of land located in Olympic Dam, South Australia from the State of South Australia, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

BHP is an Australian headquartered natural resources company.

BHP is a global leading producer of iron ore, copper and metallurgical coal. Relevantly, BHP operates the Olympic Dam Mine in South Australia, which is one of most significant global mineral deposits.

The State of South Australia through the Department of Energy and Mining, is responsible for regulating mining rights in South Australia and owns/manages the underlying legal rights to the minerals.

BHP proposes to acquire mining rights over an additional area of land located at Olympic Dam, as well as additional non-proprietary rights in relation to its existing operations on the Olympic Dam mine.

The additional area is adjacent to BHP’s existing special mining lease at Olympic Dam.

The main mineral mined at Olympic Dam is copper, with gold, silver and uranium as by-products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no removal of a competitor as a result of the Acquisition

b. the Acquisition is unlikely to affect BHP’s ability to influence the domestic price of copper, as prices are typically set by reference to global conditions

c. there are alternative producers and suppliers of copper, gold, silver and uranium in Australia and globally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The parties have executed an agreement that provides for funds (**the 26North Funds**) managed by 26North Partners LP (**26North Partners**) to acquire Intermedia Cloud Communications, Inc. and its subsidiaries (**Intermedia**), via Unite Holdco LLC (**Unite Holdco**) – an acquisition vehicle owned and controlled by the 26North Funds.

Under the acquisition, Unite Holdco’s wholly owned subsidiary, Unite Merger Sub Inc., will merge with and into Intermedia, with Intermedia continuing as the surviving entity. As a result, Unite Holdco (and indirectly the 26North Funds) will acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Intermedia.

Intermedia is a corporation incorporated in Delaware, United States. It operates a global business supplying cloud‑based communications and collaboration software services to business customers. These services primarily relate to cloud telephony voice services, as well as video conferencing, messaging, email, file sharing, archiving, security and related cloud‑based tools. Intermedia’s offerings are primarily targeted at small and medium‑sized businesses. Intermedia has several subsidiaries in different jurisdictions which will be indirectly acquired as part of the acquisition, including an Australian subsidiary - Intermedia.net Australia Pty Ltd.

26North Partners is an integrated, multi-asset-class investment platform headquartered in New York that provides investment advice and opportunities to its clients across private equity, credit, insurance and reinsurance strategies.

The 26North Funds were established in 2024 as 26North Partners' flagship fund. The 26North Funds have interests in businesses which supply goods and services in Australia, including kitchen appliances (Composition Brands), digital workplace services (AVI-SPL) and broadcast production services (NEP Group).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Unite Holdco LLC (Unite Holdco), an entity owned and controlled by funds managed by 26North Partners LP (the 26North Funds), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, indirectly via a subsidiary, of 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Intermedia Cloud Communications, Inc. (Intermedia), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, the 26North Funds, was established in 2024 as 26North Partners LP’s flagship fund. 26North Partners LP is an integrated, multi-asset-class investment platform that provides investment advice and opportunities to its clients across private equity, credit, insurance and reinsurance strategies.

The 26North Funds has interests in various businesses which supply goods and services in Australia, including a majority, controlling interest in AVI-SPL Global LLC (AVI-SPL). AVI-SPL supplies digital workplace services including multi-office collaboration and audio video technologies and managed audio video services.

The target, Intermedia, operates a global business supplying cloud-based communications and collaboration software services to business customers. These services primarily relate to cloud telephony voice services, as well as video conferencing, messaging, email, file sharing, archiving, security and related cloud-based tools.

In Australia, Intermedia’s products and services are supplied via resellers.

Intermedia and AVI-SPL both supply products and services related to communications in Australia. Intermediary’s business largely focusses on the supply of unified communications software as a service. AVI-SPL focusses on installation and integration services primarily for enterprise conference room hardware, with some unified communications software solutions ancillary to this.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the 26North Funds (via its interest in AVI-SPL) and Intermedia in the supply of unified communications software solutions

b. Intermedia has a small presence in Australia

c. there are alternative suppliers of unified communications software solutions in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd (**Hillhouse**) is a global alternative investment management firm headquartered in Singapore which focuses on long-term investments in high quality businesses. It has offices in London, Sydney, Tokyo, Hong Kong, Beijing, Mumbai and New York.

The target, Barwon Investment Partners Pty Ltd (**Barwon**), is an Australian alternatives fund manager focusing on healthcare property, real estate credit and private equity. Barwon has one office in Sydney.

The parties supply investment management services, which involves the professional and customised management of various financial assets on behalf of institutional and wholesale investor clients.

Hillhouse proposes to acquire a majority interest in Barwon.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Barwon Bidco Pty Ltd (Bidco), which is ultimately owned by Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd (Hillhouse), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a majority interest in Barwon Investment Partners Pty Ltd (Barwon), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Hillhouse, is a global alternative investment manager that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.

The target, Barwon, is a specialist fund manager investing in alternative assets across healthcare property, real estate credit and global investments, as well as in private equity for institutional and wholesale investors.

Both parties supply investment management services in Australia, although their focus is on different asset classes and in different sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no or limited horizontal overlap between Hillhouse and Barwon’s respective services and investment portfolios (including between their connected entities)

b. there are alternative suppliers of investment management services in Australia

c. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48 venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black Rhino Group operates hospitality venues, including hotels, bottleshops, accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor, entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues and locations in Queensland.

Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd (**Vendor**) owns and operates the freehold land, buildings, licences and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of:

* Granville Tavern and Motel; and
* Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars.

Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming machines. Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both businesses are based in Granville, Queensland.

Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust propose to acquire 100% of the Vendor’s interest in the aforementioned businesses, together with the relevant freehold from which the businesses operate.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust, as part of the Black Rhino Group, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd’s interest in the Granville Tavern and Motel and the Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars businesses, together with the relevant freehold land from which the businesses operate, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48 venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black Rhino Group operate licensed venues, including hotels, bottleshops, accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor, entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues and locations in Queensland.

The Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming machines. The Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both businesses are based in Granville, a suburb of Maryborough in Queensland’s Fraser Coast Region.

The Black Rhino Group’s closest venue to the Granville Tavern and Motel and Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is approximately 390km away by drive.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no local overlap between the Black Rhino Group and the Granville Tavern and Model and the Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars in the supply of on-premises hospitality products and services (including liquor for on-site consumption, dining and gaming), accommodation, or packaged liquor for offsite consumption, in Granville and surrounding areas.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition is a capital structuring transaction which will involve funds and/or vehicles advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone Alternative Credit Advisors LP (**Blackstone Credit**) converting a portion of their existing debt interest in Medallia Parent, LP (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Medallia**) into a majority equity interest (the **Acquisition**) to recapitalise Medallia and support its continued operation. This is in light of Medallia’s pending financial inability to support its ongoing debt service obligations under the existing capital structure.

Blackstone Credit is part of the Credit & Insurance segment within Blackstone Inc. (together with any of its affiliates or affiliated investment funds, **Blackstone**).

Blackstone is a US-based corporation, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States. Blackstone operates as an investment management firm, not as a conglomerate or holding company. Accordingly, each company in its investment portfolio is independently managed and financed and each has different investors (although the investors in different investment funds may overlap). Each Blackstone portfolio company has its own board of directors, which generally also include Blackstone representatives.

Medallia is a US-based software company that specialises in providing customer and employee experience management solutions. Using its experience management platform and AI-driven software, Medallia collects a broad range of feedback about end-customer and employee experiences through surveys, digital behaviour analysis, and social media to provide actionable insights to help its enterprise customers improve their employee and customer experience.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Blackstone Alternative Credit Advisors LP (Blackstone Credit) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed conversion of its existing debt interest in Medallia Parent, LP (Medallia) into a majority 56% equity interest, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Blackstone Credit, is part of the Credit & Insurance segment within Blackstone Inc. (together with any of its affiliates or affiliated investment funds, Blackstone). Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm. It has interests in Prodege, a consumer marketing and insights platform with a limited presence in Australia, and Material+, which provides marketing strategy and insight services including to customers in Australia.

The target, Medallia, is a US-based software company that specialises in providing customer and employee experience management solutions. Using its experience management platform and AI-driven software, Medallia collects feedback about end-customer and employee experiences through surveys, digital behaviour analysis, and social media to provide actionable insights to help its enterprise customers improve their employee and customer experience.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between the products and services supplied by Blackstone Credit, Blackstone (or its controlled portfolio companies) and Medallia in Australia, and no material vertical relationships, and

b. while there may be a complementary relationship between certain products and services supplied by Blackstone’s connected entities and Medallia, there are alternative suppliers of these products and services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
