mergers.merger_name: Ethos (TPG) – EMIS and certain related assets
mergers.merger_description:
Ethos Bidco, Ltd (**Ethos**) proposes to indirectly acquire the following from affiliates of UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (**UHG**) pursuant to a Share and Asset Purchase Agreement (**EMIS SAPA**):

* 100% of the shares of EMIS Group Limited (**EMIS**); and
* Certain healthcare technology assets used in the UK by UHG's wholly owned subsidiary, Optum Health Solutions (UK) Limited (**Transferred Assets**).

Ethos also proposes to acquire 100% of EMIS Health India Private Limited (**EMIS India**) (together with EMIS and the Transferred Assets, **the Target Group**) from UHG through a separate Share Purchase Agreement (**EMIS India** **SPA**, together with EMIS SAPA, **the Acquisition**).

Ethos is a special purpose vehicle established by funds managed by affiliates of TPG Inc. (**TPG**) for the purpose of the Acquisition. TPG is a leading global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco in 1992.

UHG is an NYSE-listed multinational healthcare and well-being company. EMIS is a UK-based healthcare software business ultimately owned by UHG. Its core product is a primary care electronic patient record (**EPR**) system.

In Australia, the Target Group's activities are limited to the provision of ancillary services to a single customer in relation to the Target Group's legacy EPR system.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Chair Cass-Gottlieb pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ethos Bidco, Ltd (Ethos) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the following from affiliates of UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UHG), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application:

a. 100% of the share capital in EMIS Group Limited (EMIS),

b. certain healthcare technology assets used in the UK by UHG’s wholly owned subsidiary, Optum Health Solutions (UK) Limited (Transferred Assets), and

c. 100% of the share capital of EMIS Health India Private Limited (EMIS India), (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
12 January 2026

TPG Inc. (TPG) is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco in 1992. Certain funds managed by affiliates of TPG have established Ethos, a special purpose vehicle with no prior business activities, for the purpose of the Acquisition. In Australia, TPG's portfolio covers transportation, healthcare, real estate, technology, telecommunications, and real estate industries.

The Acquisition target consists of EMIS, the Transferred Assets and EMIS India. EMIS is a healthcare software business that is based in the UK. Its core product is a primary care electronic patient record (EPR) system. EMIS also supplies other software or services, including in relation to pharmacy patient medical records, data analytics, hospital care software, life sciences, medicine optimisation software and population health management. EMIS India and the Transferred Assets have no presence in Australia. UHG is an NYSE-listed multinational healthcare and well-being company.

Both EMIS and two portfolio companies (Nextech and Covetrus) controlled by funds managed by affiliates of TPG supply healthcare software services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act), being: (i) the object of the Act, (ii) the interests of consumers, (iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply, and (iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in any market.

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is very limited horizontal overlap between entities associated with Ethos (i.e. TPG or TPG’s portfolio companies) and EMIS in the supply of healthcare software in Australia.

i. Nextech supplies speciality-specific electronic medical record and practice management software, to customers such as dermatology and plastic surgery clinics in Australia. Covetrus supplies electronic medical record and practice management software to veterinary practices. ii. In Australia, EMIS’ activities are limited and currently only involve the supply of certain maintenance services to a single customer in connection with a legacy EPR system.

b. The parties are likely to continue to be constrained by alternative suppliers of EPR systems and other healthcare software in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-70002/Ethos (TPG)-EMIS (WA-70002) - Notification waiver determination - 12 January 2026.pdf

comments.commentary:
TPG's acquisition of EMIS Group and related assets was the one of the first two acquisitions to be granted a notification waiver by the ACCC. EMIS's core product is a primary care electronic patient record (EPR) system. The ACCC determined the acquisition did not require notification because:

- there is very limited horizontal overlap in Australia between TPG's portfolio companies (Nextech supplies specialty-specific software to dermatology/plastic surgery clinics, while Covetrus supplies software to veterinary practices) and EMIS;
- EMIS' Australian activities are limited to maintenance services for a single customer with a legacy EPR system; and
- alternative suppliers of EPR systems and healthcare software remain in Australia.

While notification thresholds were likely met, the ACCC waived the requirement given its view that there were minimal competition concerns.

targets.name: WILD BUSH LUXURY EXPERIENCE PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Travel Agency and Tour Arrangement Services
Nature Reserves and Conservation Parks Operation

mergers.merger_description:
This application relates to the Acquisition by Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (**Intrepid**) of 100% shares in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (**Wild Bush Luxury**) held by Experience Co Limited (**ExCo**).

Intrepid Group Pty Ltd (**IG**) is a global, purpose-led experiential travel company, specialising in small group tours and travel experiences. IG has 31 offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group adventures in 118 countries. Intrepid is a subsidiary of IG.

Wild Bush Luxury is a boutique Australian tourism operator that manages and operates the 'The Maria Island Walk' (in Tasmania), 'Arkaba Walk (in South Australia), 'Arkaba Homestead' (in South Australia), and 'Bamurru Plains' (in the Northern Territory).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (Intrepid Travel) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (Wild Bush Luxury), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Intrepid Travel, is a subsidiary of Intrepid Group Pty Ltd which is a global, experiential travel company specialising in small group tours and travel experiences. Intrepid Group Pty Ltd has 31 offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group adventures in 118 countries.

The target, Wild Bush Luxury, is a boutique Australian tourism operator that manages and operates the 'The Maria Island Walk' (in Tasmania), 'Arkaba Walk (in South Australia), 'Arkaba Homestead' (in South Australia), and 'Bamurru Plains' (in the Northern Territory).

Wild Bush Luxury is currently held by Experience Co Limited, a tourism company and offers adventure experiences of tandem skydiving, aerial high ropes and ziplining activities and tours to the Great Barrier Reef and Daintree regions.

Intrepid Travel and Wild Bush Luxury both supply tourism services in Australia, and particularly in Top End (Northern Territory), Flinders Ranges (South Australia) and Maria Island (Tasmania).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, including the revenue of the parties in Australia and relevant cumulative acquisitions the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:

a. The extent of any horizontal overlap between Intrepid Travel and Wild Bush Luxury in the supply of tourism services in Australia, and particularly in Maria Island, the Top End and Flinders Ranges,

b. The extent to which the parties are likely to face competition from alternative suppliers at these locations, particularly in relation to Maria Island where alternatives appear limited.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Impression Dental Group - Happy Rock Dental Practice

acquirers.name: GENERAL DENTAL HOLDINGS PTY LTD trading as Impression Dental Group

mergers.merger_description:
General Dental Holdings Pty Ltd, trading as Impression Dental Group (**IDG**), proposes to acquire 100% of the assets of the dental practice known as Happy Rock Dental (**Happy Rock Dental Practice**), through a business sale agreement.

Happy Rock Dental Practice is owned and operated by Dr Wei-Jie Jiang and Wei-Jie Jiang Pty Ltd and supplies general dental services to the public from its premises in Cronulla, NSW.

IDG is an Australian-based dental support organisation that provides centralised support in areas including recruitment, payroll, marketing, IT, finance, and operations, to enable dental practitioners to focus on providing dental services. IDG currently owns and supports 22 independently branded dental practices in Australia which provide dental services to the public. IDG does not have a premises in Cronulla, NSW.

IDG is controlled by Genesis Capital which is an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

Genesis Capital’s investments also include Pacific Smiles Group which sets up new dental centres from which dentists run their practice and provides operational support. Pacific Smiles Group has over 120 dental centres across Australia, including one in the Sutherland Shire which is not in Cronulla, NSW. Pacific Smiles Group’s dental centres are mainly located in shopping centres and operate under the Pacific Smiles Dental and NIB Dental Care brands on the East Coast and under the HBF brand in Western Australia.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: General Dental Holdings Pty Ltd, trading as Impression Dental Group (IDG), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the assets of Happy Rock Dental practice (Happy Rock), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
26 February 2026

The acquirer, IDG, is an Australian-based dental support organisation that provides centralised support in areas including recruitment, payroll, marketing, IT, finance, and operations, to enable dental practitioners to focus on providing dental services. IDG currently owns and supports 22 independently branded dental practices in Australia (including 10 dental practices in NSW).

IDG is controlled by Genesis Capital (Genesis), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’ investments also include Pacific Smiles Group, which sets up new dental centres from which dentists run their practice and provides operational support. Pacific Smiles Group has over 120 dental centres across Australia, including one in the Sutherland Shire. Neither IDG or Pacific Smiles Group have dental practices in Cronulla, NSW.

The target, Happy Rock, is owned and operated by Dr Wei-Jie Jiang and Wei-Jie Jiang Pty Ltd, and supplies general dental services to the public from its premises in Cronulla, NSW.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Genesis and Happy Rock in Cronulla, NSW and a small overlap in the broader Sutherland Shire Council area

b. there are numerous alternative suppliers of dental services in the area The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Scopely, Inc. (**Scopely**) proposes to acquire:

* an initial 51% of the shares of Loom Games Oyun Yazılım ve Pazarlama Anonim Şirketi (**Loom Games**), and
* 4 related 12.25% stakes, up to 100% of the shares of Loom Games.

Scopely is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Saudi Electronic Games Holding Company (doing business as Savvy Games Group (**Savvy**)), which itself is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Public Investment Fund (**PIF**), the sovereign wealth fund of Saudi Arabia.

Scopely is an interactive entertainment company specialising in the development and publishing of mobile-first video games. Scopely develops games in-house, through Scopely’s development studios, as well as through external partner studios.

Scopely’s published mobile games currently available include MONOPOLY GO!, Star Trek Fleet Command, Marvel Strike Force, Pokémon GO, Monster Hunter Now, Campfire, Wayfarer, Pikmin Bloom, Bingo Bash: Live Bingo Games, Tiki Solitaire TriPeaks, Yahtzee with Buddies, WWE Champions, Scrabble GO, GSN Casino: Slot Machine Games, Stumble Guys, Looney Tunes World of Mayhem, Dice With Buddies: Social Game, Wheel of Fortune: Show Puzzles, Temple Run: Puzzle Adventure, and Grand Casino: Slots Games. Games produced by Scopely are built on its Playgami platform, which includes both publishing tools and a learning system to help support game development during all aspects of its development cycle and after launch.

Savvy is the ultimately controlling company within PIF that is active in gaming. The only Savvy-owned companies active in mobile gaming are Scopely and Scopely’s wholly owned subsidiaries, Niantic, and Tag Games. Savvy is otherwise active in organising and commercialising eSports events, developing and investing in experimental gaming venues, and developing video games.

Aside from Savvy, PIF’s portfolio consists of companies active in various industries, including aerospace and defence, construction, financial services, healthcare, mining, real estate and others.

Loom Games is a mobile game developer engaged in the development, distribution and operation of puzzle games, based in Istanbul, Turkey, and established in 2025. Loom Games’ games are (i) Pixel Flow! and (ii) Ropeland (which is currently dormant). It distributes its games mainly through global app stores such as Google Play and Apple App Store, with its core revenue streams derived from in-app advertising and in-app purchasing/subscriptions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Scopely, Inc (Scopely) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

• an initial 51% of the shares of Loom Games Oyun Yazılım ve Pazarlama Anonim Şirketi (Loom Games), and

• 4 related 12.25% stakes, up to 100% of the shares of Loom Games, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Scopely, is an interactive entertainment company specialising in the development and publishing of mobile-first video games. Scopely develops games in-house, through Scopely’s development studios, as well as through external partner studios.

Games produced by Scopely are built on its Playgami platform, which includes both publishing tools and a learning system to help support game development during all aspects of its development cycle and after launch.

Scopely is wholly owned by Savvy Games Group (Savvy). Savvy is active in organising and commercialising eSports events, developing and investing in experimental gaming venues, and developing video games.

Savvy is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Public Investment Fund (PIF), the sovereign wealth fund of Saudi Arabia. PIF’s portfolio consists of companies active in various industries, including aerospace and defence, construction, financial services, healthcare, mining, real estate and others.

The target, Loom Games, is a mobile game developer engaging in the development, distribution and operation of puzzle games. Loom Games’ games are (i) Pixel Flow! and (ii) Ropeland (which is currently dormant). It distributes its games mainly through global app stores such as Google Play and Apple App Store.

Scopely and Loom Games both develop and publish video games for mobile devices in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of mobile video games in Australia is low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

b. the presence of alternative developers and publishers of mobile video games in Australia.

Given the above considerations that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Advance Holdings II, LLC (a vehicle ultimately controlled by Platinum Equity, LLC (**Platinum Equity**)) proposes to acquire BCI Acquisitions, Inc. (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Burke**).

Burke designs, manufactures and supplies outdoor playground and recreational equipment, as well as ancillary products for recreational spaces.

Platinum Equity is a global private equity and investment strategies firm, which is specialised in the merger, acquisition and operation of companies providing services and solutions to customers in a broad range of sectors, including business services, information technology, telecommunications, logistics, metal services, manufacturing and distribution. Among such companies is Play Holdings, Inc. (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **PlayPower**). PlayPower is a designer, manufacturer and supplier of outdoor playground equipment, sport and fitness equipment, and park and urban furniture.

Burke and PlayPower are headquartered in the US with no corporate or physical footprint in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Advance Holdings II, LLC (Advance Holdings) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of BCI Acquisitions, Inc (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, Burke), effected by way of a merger of Activate Play Merger Corporation (a subsidiary of Advance Holdings) and Burke, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). This will involve Burke continuing as the surviving corporation and becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Advance Holdings.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Advance Holdings, is a vehicle established for the purpose of the Acquisition and is ultimately controlled by Platinum Equity, LLC (Platinum Equity). Platinum Equity is a global private equity and investment strategies firm specialising in the merger, acquisition and operation of companies providing services and solutions to customers in a broad range of sectors, including business services, information technology, telecommunications, logistics, metal services, manufacturing and distribution. Among such companies is Play Holdings, Inc. (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, PlayPower). PlayPower is a designer, manufacturer and supplier of outdoor playground equipment, sport and fitness equipment, and park and urban furniture. PlayPower is headquartered in the US.

The target, Burke, designs, manufactures and supplies outdoor playground and recreational equipment, as well as ancillary products for recreational spaces. Burke is headquartered in the US.

PlayPower and Burke both supply commercial playground and outdoor recreation equipment globally and in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. PlayPower and Burke have a small presence in Australia

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of playground and outdoor recreation equipment is likely to be low

c. the presence of alternative global and national suppliers of playground and outdoor recreation equipment, including commercial.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Tetra Tech Australia Group Holdings Pty Ltd (**Tetra Tech**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Providence Consulting Group Pty Ltd (**Providence Consulting**) through a Share Purchase Agreement.

Tetra Tech is a wholly owned subsidiary of Tetra Tech, Inc., a publicly listed corporation (NASDAQ:TTEK), and is the holding company for various subsidiaries.

Tetra Tech's subsidiaries are involved in a variety of industries, in particular water, energy, infrastructure, mining, defence and development, and provide high-end consulting, technical and advisory services in Australia to a variety of customers, both public and private. Tetra Tech also has operations and interests across the Asia-Pacific region.

Providence Consulting is an advisory and project management consultancy based in Australia. Providence Consulting supplies program delivery, systems engineering, and protective security consultancy services for defence, government, and industry clients.

The acquirer, Tetra Tech, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Tetra Tech, Inc., a publicly listed corporation (NASDAQ:TTEK), and is the holding company for various subsidiaries. Tetra Tech's subsidiaries are involved in a variety of industries, in particular water, energy, infrastructure, mining, defence and development, and provide high-end consulting, technical and advisory services in Australia to a variety of customers, both public and private. Tetra Tech also has operations and interests across the Asia-Pacific region.

The target, Providence Consulting, is an advisory and project management consultancy based in Australia. Providence Consulting supplies program delivery, systems engineering, and protective security consultancy services for defence, government, and industry clients.

Providence Consulting appears primarily to be a specialist defence consulting firm whereas Tetra Tech provides consulting services more generally, in a variety of fields.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application , the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Tetra Tech and Providence Consulting in the supply of consulting services in Australia (including defence consulting)

b. there are alternative suppliers of consulting services (including in the defence sector)

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Infotrust Ltd (ASX:ITS) (**Infotrust**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Catalyst Cyber.

Infotrust is an ASX-listed Australian technology services group. Its cybersecurity offering includes the supply of governance, risk and compliance (**GRC**) and assurance consulting, penetration testing to identify and remediate cyber vulnerabilities in networks and systems, and strategic advisory to establish cybersecurity strategies in line with applicable compliance standards, and managed information technology services. Infotrust also resells software and products for advanced security solutions in connection with its consulting engagements, as well as professional assurance and advisory services. Its cybersecurity offerings are primarily directed to commercial and enterprise customers across a range of industries.

Catalyst Cyber, trading as Catalyst Security, is a Canberra-based cybersecurity consultancy business that was established in 2024.

Catalyst Cyber is primarily focussed on the supply of cybersecurity services to Federal Government clients. Its core business comprises GRC and assurance consulting to assist government clients meet regulatory requirements and industry standards and penetration testing to advise on compliance with mandatory information management requirements. Catalyst Cyber resells to a limited degree third-party government-grade security testing software and provides professional services covering governance uplift and accreditation.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Infotrust Ltd (Infotrust) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Catalyst Cyber Pty Limited (Catalyst Cyber), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
26 March 2026

The acquirer, Infotrust, is an ASX-listed Australian technology services group. Its cybersecurity offering includes the supply of governance, risk and compliance (GRC) and assurance consulting, penetration testing to identify and remediate cyber vulnerabilities in networks and systems, and strategic advisory to establish cybersecurity strategies in line with applicable compliance standards, and managed information technology services. Infotrust also resells software and products for advanced security solutions in connection with its consulting engagements, as well as professional assurance and advisory services. Its cybersecurity offerings are primarily directed to commercial and enterprise customers across a range of industries.

The target, Catalyst Cyber, trading as Catalyst Security, is a Canberra-based cybersecurity consultancy business established in 2024.

Catalyst Cyber is primarily focussed on the supply of cybersecurity services to Federal Government clients. Its core business comprises GRC and assurance consulting to assist government clients meet regulatory requirements and industry standards and penetration testing to advise on compliance with mandatory information management requirements. Catalyst Cyber resells to a limited degree third-party government-grade security testing software and provides professional services covering governance uplift and accreditation.

Both Infotrust and Catalyst Cyber provide GRC and assurance consulting, penetration testing, cyber consulting, software and product resale, and other professional cybersecurity services in Australia

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Infotrust and Catalyst Cyber overlap in the supply of cybersecurity services in Australia, including a subset of these services.

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of cybersecurity services to customers in Australia is low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of cybersecurity services in Australia, including in the supply of the subset of services where there is a horizontal overlap.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd, which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group, proposes to acquire the land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla, Queensland. Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) that provide food, drink (including alcohol), gaming machines, accommodation services, and takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops). It currently operates pubs and bottle shops in regional Queensland and the Northern Territory.

Warrego Hotel Motel is a commercial hotel motel business in Cunnamulla, QLD which offers dining, accommodation, gaming machines and liquor retailing, both in-store via a drive-through bottle shop and online.

determination_table_content.details: Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd, which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group (Waymark), applied for a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of the land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla, Queensland, as described in the transaction document provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). As part of the Acquisition:  Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd will acquire land located at 9 Louise Street and Lot 2 Garden Street in Cunnamulla, Queensland, 4490 from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust)  Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd will acquire the commercial hotel business carried on at that location from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 April 2026

Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) and takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops) in regional Queensland and the Northern Territory. The Waymark Hotels Group is part of the Vinta Group, a private real estate group.

The target, Warrego Hotel Motel, is a commercial hotel in Cunnamulla, Queensland (approximately 750km west of Brisbane) which offers dining, accommodation, gaming machines and accommodation services and liquor retailing, both in-store via a drive-through bottle shop and online. Warrego Hotel Motel is operated by G.A.A. Pty Limited (Trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).

Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel both supply dining, accommodation, gaming and liquor in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no local geographic horizontal overlap between Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel in the supply of on-premises or takeaway packaged liquor, dining, gaming or accommodation services in Cunnamulla or the surrounding region in Western Queensland

b. there are alternative suppliers of on-premises and takeaway packaged liquor, dining, gaming and accommodation services in the broader Cunnamulla and Paroo Shire area.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Egis Projects Asia Pacific - Fulton Hogan Egis O&M

mergers.merger_description:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Ltd (**EPAP**) proposes to acquire from Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (**Fulton Hogan Industries**) the 50% of shares in Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (**FHE**) that it does not already own.

FHE was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries. The acquisition involves the effective dissolution of the joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries via FHE and will occur pursuant to a share sale agreement.

FHE supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e., asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the WestConnex project in Sydney. WestConnex is a continuous, 33km traffic-light-free motorway network with connection to Sydney Airport and connections for future projects linking the north shore and the southern suburbs.

In future, FHE will supply facilities management services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney. To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6 tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.

Post acquisition, as a wholly-owned subsidiary of EPAP, FHE will continue to provide operations, maintenance and/or facilities management services to current and future customers, and Fulton Hogan Industries will operate entirely independently of both FHE and EPAP.

EPAP is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a France-based engineering, consulting and operation services company. Neither EPAP nor any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia, other than through FHE. In Australia, other subsidiaries of Egis S.A. provide façade design and engineering services, building sustainability services, multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services (including rail signalling, rail systems, electrical, transmission/distribution, urban development, water, roads, environmental, and civil), architecture services, and enterprise asset management solutions.

Fulton Hogan Industries provides services in pavement, asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Limited (EPAP), which is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A. (Egis), applied for a notification waiver in respect of EPAP’s proposed acquisition of the 50% of shares in Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (FHE) which it does not already own, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
22 April 2026

The target, FHE, was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (Fulton Hogan Industries).

FHE currently supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e., asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the WestConnex project and, in future, will supply facilities management services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney.

To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6 tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.

The acquirer, EPAP, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a France-based engineering, consulting and operation services company. In Australia, Egis S.A. (via its subsidiaries) provides façade design and engineering services, building sustainability services, multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services, architecture services, and enterprise asset management solutions.

EPAP conducts business development activities relating to operations and maintenance services supplied by FHE and other subsidiaries.

The vendor, Fulton Hogan Industries, provides services in pavement, asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.

Other than through the existing FHE joint venture, neither EPAP nor any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and

• had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Outside the existing FHE joint venture arrangement, there are no horizontal overlaps between EPAP (including its connected entities) and FHE in the supply of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.

b. The Acquisition will not result in any reduction in the number of suppliers of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.

c. There are alternative suppliers of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.

mergers.merger_description:
Bendigo and Adelaide Bank Limited (**Bendigo and Adelaide Bank**) and Members Banking Group Limited trading as RACQ Bank (**RACQ Bank**) have entered into an agreement for Bendigo and Adelaide Bank to acquire RACQ Bank’s retail loan and deposit books by way of an asset sale (the **Acquisition**).

Bendigo and Adelaide Bank is an Australian, ASX-listed (ASX:BEN) authorised deposit-taking institution (**ADI**) headquartered in Bendigo, Victoria. Established in 1858, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank provides retail banking services to over 2.9m customers through 416 branches across Australia, and has over $73bn of customer deposits and over $84bn of total lending. Bendigo and Adelaide Bank’s customer-facing banking business is split into a consumer division and a business and agribusiness division.

RACQ Bank is an ADI and wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of The Royal Automobile Club of Queensland Limited (**RACQ**). RACQ Bank provides retail loan and deposit banking services, including personal, home and business loans, transactions accounts, savings accounts and term deposits to approximately 90,000 customers. At 30 June 2025, RACQ Bank’s retail business comprised A$2.7bn of retail loans and A$2.5bn of retail deposits.

Upon completion, RACQ Bank customers will be migrated to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank, and RACQ will refer its members to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank for certain banking products under a separate referral arrangement. The referral arrangement will not commence until post-completion.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Bendigo and Adelaide Bank Limited (Bendigo and Adelaide Bank) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of Members Banking Group Limited’s, trading as RACQ Bank (RACQ Bank), retail loan and deposit books by way of an asset sale, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
28 April 2026

The acquirer, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank, is an Australian, ASX-listed (ASX:BEN) authorised deposit-taking institution (ADI) headquartered in Bendigo, Victoria. Established in 1858, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank provides retail banking services to over 2.9m customers through 416 branches across Australia, and has over A$73bn of customer deposits and over A$84bn of total lending. Bendigo and Adelaide Bank’s customer-facing banking business is split into a consumer division and a business and agribusiness division.

The target, RACQ Bank, is an ADI and wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of The Royal Automobile Club of Queensland Limited (RACQ). RACQ Bank provides retail loan and deposit banking services, including personal, home and business loans, transactions accounts, savings accounts and term deposits to approximately 90,000 customers. At 30 June 2025, RACQ Bank’s retail business comprised A$2.7bn of retail loans and A$2.5bn of retail deposits.

The parties both supply retail lending and retail deposit products in Queensland and across Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. while there is a horizontal overlap between Bendigo and Adelaide Bank and RACQ Bank in the supply of retail lending and retail deposit products in Queensland and nationally, their combined share in the supply of these products in each would be low, and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

b. there are alternative suppliers of retail lending and retail deposit products in Queensland and nationally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hudson Highland (APAC) Pty Ltd (**HHA**) is the holding company of Hudson Global Resources (Aust) Pty Ltd (**Hudson Global Resources (Aust)**), which provides recruitment and labour hire services in Australia.

Hudson Global Resources (Aust) is an Australian-owned recruitment and talent management agency, operating since 1985 with offices across all major Australian states and territories. They specialize in permanent, contract, and high-volume recruitment for sectors including government, tech, engineering, and finance, placing roughly 5,600 on-hire staff annually.

Oceania Capital Partners Ltd (**Oceania Capital Partners)** is a Sydney based unlisted public company that pursues private equity style investments in businesses. This includes interests in home security, aerospace, broadcasting, and health, among others.

K & K Ryan Pty Ltd as trustee for the K & K Ryan Family Trust is a private investor associated with the Hudson Global Resources (Aust)’s Chief Financial Officer, Kendall Ryan.

Oceania Capital Partners and K & K Ryan Pty Ltd propose to each subscribe for 33.15% of the ordinary shares in HHA under a share subscription agreement.

Hudson Global Resources (Aust) is an Australian-owned recruitment and talent management agency, operating since 1985 with offices across all major Australian states and territories. They specialize in permanent, contract, and high-volume recruitment for sectors including government, tech, engineering, and finance, placing roughly 5,600 on-hire staff annually.

Oceania Capital Partners and K & K Ryan Pty Ltd do not have any other investments in recruitment and labour hire businesses, or investments in related businesses at different levels in the supply chain for recruitment and labour hire in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationships between Oceania Capital Partners, K & K Ryan Pty Ltd and Hudson Global Resources (Aust) in the supply of recruitment and labour hire services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

National Dental Care is a dental services organisation that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services. National Dental Care has dental clinics in all mainland Australian states and territories, including 26 clinics in Perth, WA.

mergers.merger_description:
Pursuant to an Agreement and Plan of Merger, Asteroid Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant Domestic Holdings Corporation (**Cognizant DHC**) proposes to merge with and into Astreya Partners, Inc. (**Astreya**). Astreya will continue as the surviving corporation and become an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant Technology Solutions (**Cognizant**), the parent company of Cognizant DHC (the **Acquisition**).

Cognizant operates in Australia through a number of wholly owned indirect subsidiaries (together, **Cognizant Australia**). As part of the Acquisition, Cognizant will also acquire Astreya's Australian subsidiary, Astreya Pty Ltd (**Astreya Australia**).

Cognizant and Astreya supply IT managed services. Cognizant provides IT services including, among other things, consulting, software engineering, cloud services data and AI, cybersecurity, business process outsourcing, and industry-specific solutions.

Astreya is a digital and IT infrastructure managed service provider that is focussed primarily on digital workplace and end-user computing support, as well as delivering cloud and infrastructure managed services. Astreya has limited personnel in Australia that provide IT managed infrastructure services to a small number of multinational clients under global service arrangements.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Cognizant Domestic Holdings Corporation, wholly owned and controlled by Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (Cognizant), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the share capital in Astreya Partners Inc. (Astreya), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

By way of an Agreement and Plan of Merger, Asteroid Merger Sub, Inc., an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Cognizant, will merge with and into Astreya, with Astreya continuing as the surviving corporation as an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Cognizant is a global IT service provider listed on the Nasdaq Stock Exchange and headquartered in Teaneck (New Jersey), United States. Cognizant provides various IT services including, among other things, consulting, software engineering, cloud services data and AI, cybersecurity, business process outsourcing, and industry specific solutions. Cognizant began delivering services to customers in Australia in around 2006 and has registered offices in Sydney and Melbourne, with satellite offices in Brisbane and Perth. Cognizant delivers services to clients across a range of industries in Australia including financial services and insurance, energy and resources, and retail.

Astreya is a digital and IT infrastructure managed service provider that is focused primarily on digital workplace services (though audiovisual technology), as well as delivering cloud, AI and other infrastructure managed services. Astreya is headquartered in California in the United States and has a limited presence in Australia.

Both Cognizant and Astreya supply IT infrastructure managed services, such as cloud infrastructure, AI and workplace collaboration services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Cognizant and Astreya in the supply of IT infrastructure managed services

b. Astreya has a small presence in Australia

c. the presence of alternative suppliers of IT infrastructure managed services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
EGYM SE (together with its subsidiaries, **EGYM**) and Mindbody, Inc. (together with its controlled subsidiaries, **Playlist**) intend to combine in a newly established company, Saturn NewCo, Inc. (**Saturn**), and transfer shares in Saturn to the existing shareholders of EGYM and Playlist.

Playlist is a US-based parent brand of the brands ClassPass, Mindbody and Booker. An aspect of ClassPass’s offering is a fitness and wellbeing aggregation service through which users can obtain access to various fitness, wellbeing and lifestyle experiences such as fitness classes, studios, spas, etc. ClassPass provides subscriptions directly to consumers. Mindbody offers a Software as a Service solution for the fitness, wellbeing and beauty industry, providing back-end infrastructure via a software platform to help fitness studios, wellbeing centres, spas and salons manage their business operations. Booker provides an online booking and business management system specifically for spa facilities, salons and other appointment based service businesses (such as beauty services or wellbeing centres).

EGYM is a German-based provider of smart gym equipment and software to fitness and health facilities, as well as corporate fitness and wellbeing aggregation services (primarily in relation to gyms and fitness clubs) to companies worldwide.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
EGYM SE (together with its subsidiaries, EGYM) and Mindbody, Inc. (together with its controlled subsidiaries, Playlist) intend to combine in a newly established company, Saturn NewCo, Inc. (Saturn), and transfer shares in Saturn to the existing shareholders of EGYM and Playlist, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The application for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition was jointly made by:

• Vista Equity Partners Management, LLC (Vista),

• Playlist,

• ClassPass Aggregator, LLC,

• Saturn and its subsidiary Saturn Merger Sub, Inc.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
19 February 2026

Playlist is a US-based parent brand of the brands ClassPass, Mindbody and Booker. An aspect of ClassPass’s offering is a fitness and wellbeing aggregation service through which users can obtain access to various fitness, wellbeing and lifestyle experiences such as fitness classes, studios, spas, etc. Mindbody offers Software as a Service (SaaS) solution for the fitness, wellbeing and beauty industry, providing back-end infrastructure via a software platform to help fitness studios, wellbeing centres, spas and salons manage their business operations. Booker provides an online booking and business management system specifically for spa facilities, salons and other appointment-based service businesses (such as beauty services or wellbeing centres).

Playlist is controlled by funds managed and/or controlled by Vista.

Vista is a US-based private equity firm that focuses on financing enterprise software, data and technology-enabled businesses.

EGYM is a German-based provider of smart gym equipment and software to fitness and health facilities, as well as corporate fitness and wellbeing aggregation services (primarily in relation to gyms and fitness clubs) to companies worldwide. EGYM’s Gymtech offering provides “smart” gym equipment and technology solutions for operators of gyms, wellbeing centres, and corporate wellbeing programs. EGYM’s Wellpass offering is a fitness and wellbeing aggregation service enabling corporate customers of EGYM to offer individual fitness and wellbeing benefits to their employees. The application states that Wellpass does not generate revenue in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Playlist’s and EGYM’s offering in Australia

b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-Acquisition The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
BetterGrow Holdings Pty Ltd (**Bettergrow**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Hygain Holdco Pty Ltd (**HYG**).

Bettergrow is an Australian-based company in the organic recovery sector. It recovers and re-manufactures organic residuals, waste and by-products, extracting maximum value from materials otherwise destined for landfill, transforming them into quality composts, fertilisers and bespoke soil rehabilitation products for resale. Bettergrow also produces pasture, fodder and beef. It also supplies animal bedding sourced from timber mills.

Bettergrow is part of the Borg Group, a diversified Australian-owned private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging, and engineering and automation services.

HYG is an equine‑nutrition company that formulates and manufactures premium horse feeds and supplements. HYG (through its two brands, Hygain and Mitavite) supplies products to performance, breeding and leisure horse owners through agricultural and equine supplies retailers and wholesalers and distributors in Australia, and globally. HYG operates a manufacturing facility in Victoria and in New South Wales. HYG’s products include manufactured feeds (including pelleted and textured complete feeds and balancers), supplements and additives (including vitamins and minerals, electrolytes, digestive and hoof health products, oil/fat sources and speciality supplements). HYG also offers complimentary nutritional support services for its supplements and commercial feed products.

HYG is controlled by funds under the management of Adamantem Capital, an Australia and New Zealand private equity firm, based in Sydney.

mergers.merger_description:
Zendesk, Inc. (**Zendesk**) proposes to acquire 100% of the business of Forethought Technologies, Inc. (**Forethought**) through a reverse triangular merger whereby Forethought will acquire 100% of the issued shares in a subsidiary of Zendesk which has been newly incorporated for the purpose of the merger. Through this process, Forethought will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Zendesk.

Zendesk is a US-based software company providing software-as-a-service customer relationship management (**CRM**) software designed to improve customer relationships.

Zendesk is owned by affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (**Permira**) and affiliates of Hellman & Friedman LLC (**H&F**).

Permira is a Guernsey-registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare, technology, and climate sectors.

H&F is a US-based private equity investment firm with a focus on the technology sector, among others.

Permira and H&F also own Genesys, which is a contact-centre-as-a-service (**CCaaS**) provider which develops, manufactures and supplies software products and related services for customer interaction management.

Forethought is a US-based start-up company that markets AI-driven software tools which integrate with CRM software.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Zendesk, Inc (Zendesk) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the business of Forethought Technologies, Inc. (Forethought) through a reverse triangular merger whereby Forethought will acquire 100% of the issued shares in a subsidiary of Zendesk which has been newly incorporated for the purposes of the merger, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). Through this process, Forethought will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Zendesk.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Zendesk, is a US-based software company providing software-as-a-service customer relationship management (CRM) software designed to improve customer relationships. Zendesk is owned by affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (Permira) and affiliates of Hellman & Friedman LLC (H&F). Permira is a private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare, technology, and climate sectors.

H&F is a US-based private equity investment firm with a focus on the technology sector, among others. Permira and H&F also own Genesys, which is a contact-centre-as-a-service (CCaaS) provider which develops, manufactures and supplies software products and related services for customer interaction management.

The target, Forethought, is a US-based start-up company that markets AI-driven software tools which integrate with CRM software.

Zendesk, Forethought and Genesys provide artificial intelligence (AI)-based platforms which integrate with CRM software.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain industry information in its possession, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share of supply of CRM software to customers in Australia is low

b. Forethought has a small presence in Australia

c. there are alternative suppliers of CRM software and AI-based platforms for CRM to businesses in Australia

d. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

mergers.merger_description:
Autosports Group Limited (ASX: ASG) (**ASG**) proposes to acquire 100% of the Solitaire Automotive Group business from vendors Toja Investments Pty Ltd as trustee for the Bill Holst Family Trust and David Smoker (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will occur through ASG acquiring 100% interests in the following Solitaire Automotive Group entities:

* Cambridge Motors Pty Ltd, which is the entity owned by David Smoker and holds shares in Russleigh Pty Ltd (**Russleigh**) and units in the Russleigh Unit Trust,
* Russleigh, which is the trustee of the Russleigh Unit Trust, which operates the Solitaire Automotive Group business, and
* the Russleigh Unit Trust.

ASG operates a total of 81 motor vehicle dealerships, 4 used vehicle dealerships, 6 motorcycle dealerships and 8 motor vehicle collision repair facilities located in Sydney, Canberra, Melbourne, Brisbane, Gold Coast and Auckland. The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include ALPINA, Aston Martin, Audi, Bentley, BMW, Geely, Kia, Lamborghini, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Mclaren, Mercedes-Benz, MINI, Polestar, Porsche, Rolls-Royce, Subaru, Volkswagen, Volvo and Zeekr.

Solitaire Automotive Group operates 12 motor vehicle dealerships, 2 used vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle dealership located in Adelaide. The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include Aston Martin, Audi, Cupra, Ducati, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Polestar, Volkswagen, Volkswagen Commercial, Volvo and Zeekr.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Autosports Group Limited (ASG) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% interests in the following Solitaire Automotive Group (Solitaire) entities:

• Cambridge Motors Pty Ltd, which is the entity used by David Smoker to hold the shares in Russleigh Pty Ltd (Russleigh) and units in the Russleigh Unit Trust;

• Russleigh Pty Ltd, which is the trustee of the Russleigh Unit Trust, which operates the Solitaire Automotive Group business; and

• the Russleigh Unit Trust, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition consists of ASG acquiring:

• 2,500 of 10,000 ordinary shares (i.e. 25%) in Russleigh and 64,820 of 259,295 units (i.e. 25%) in the Russleigh Unit Trust; and

• 100% of the shares in Cambridge Motors (which in turn owns 7,500 of 10,000 ordinary shares in Russleigh (i.e. 75%)) and 194,475 of 259,295 units in the Russleigh Unit Trust (i.e. 75%).

The effect of the Acquisition will be that ASG will acquire 100% of the business which operates Solitaire Automotive Group's 12 motor vehicle dealerships, 2 used vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle dealership in Adelaide.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
11 March 2026

The acquirer, ASG, operates a total of 81 motor vehicle dealerships, 4 used vehicle dealerships, 6 motorcycle dealerships and 8 motor vehicle collision repair facilities located in Sydney, Canberra, Melbourne, Brisbane, Gold Coast and Auckland. The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include ALPINA, Aston Martin, Audi, Bentley, BMW, Geely, Kia, Lamborghini, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Mclaren, MercedesBenz, MINI, Polestar, Porsche, Rolls-Royce, Subaru, Volkswagen, Volvo and Zeekr.

The target, Solitaire, operates 12 motor vehicle dealerships, 2 used vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle dealership located in Adelaide.

The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include Aston Martin, Audi, Cupra, Ducati, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Polestar, Volkswagen, Volkswagen Commercial, Volvo and Zeekr.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between ASG and Solitaire in the supply of new and used motor vehicles, car servicing and parts in South Australia

b. the presence of alternative dealerships and dealership groups supplying new and used motor vehicles, car servicing and parts locally and nationally

c. while ASG is one of the largest motor vehicle dealers in Australia, the increment to ASG’s share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
