mergers.merger_description:
The transaction involves the acquisition by Migaloo QLD Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Star Consolidated Holdings Pty Ltd and its controlled entities (**Star Hotels Group**), of:

* the leasehold interest in the land that the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores operate on; and
* business assets including goodwill, fixtures, fittings and all licences involved with the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores.

The Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of Queensland with 4 venues in South Australia. It operates multiple licensed venues and liquor retail outlets under brands such as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as accommodation at selected venues.

Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 are bottle shop businesses which are located at 169 High Street, Berserker, QLD 4701 and 86 Derby Street, Rockhampton, QLD 4700, respectively. These outlets sell a range of alcoholic beverages to retail customers. Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 Liquor are currently owned and operated by The Trustee for The Garden Holdings Trust.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Migaloo QLD Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Star Consolidated Holdings Pty Ltd and its controlled entities (Star Hotels Group), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:

• the leasehold interest in the land that the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores operate on; and

• business assets including goodwill, fixtures, fittings and all licences involved with the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
2 April 2026

The Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of Queensland with 4 venues in South Australia. It operates multiple licensed venues and liquor retail outlets under brands such as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as accommodation at selected venues.

Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 are bottle shop businesses which are located at 169 High Street, Berserker, Queensland 4701 and 86 Derby Street, Rockhampton, Queensland 4700, respectively. Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 Liquor are currently owned and operated by The Trustee for The Garden Holdings Trust.

The Star Hotels Group and Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores overlap in the retail supply of liquor to customers in local areas in Rockhampton.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.

Based on the information provided in the application, including the parties’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be met.

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:

a. there is horizontal overlap between the Star Hotels Group and the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores in the retail supply of packaged liquor for off-site consumption, in the local area surrounding those stores and the broader Rockhampton area

b. Star Hotel Group’s share of the retail supply of packaged liquor in Rockhampton is likely to be significant

c. there is uncertainty regarding the level of competitive constraint likely to be exerted from alternative retail suppliers of packaged liquor for off-site consumption, in the local areas surrounding the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores and in the broader Rockhampton area.

The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant consideration through the notification process, including via consultation with third parties.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 81 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

Vets Central was acquired by Pemba Capital Partners in September 2021 and sits within Pemba Capital Fund II.

Drovers Vet Hospital is a full-service veterinary practice providing a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the Wanneroo area of Western Australia.

mergers.merger_description:
CleanPeak Energy Holdings Pty Ltd, through its wholly-owned subsidiary CleanPeak IFM Projects Pty Ltd as trustee for CleanPeak IFM Projects Trust (**CleanPeak IFM**) (together referred to as **CleanPeak**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Sustainable Energy Infrastructure Holdings Pty Ltd and 100% of the units in the Sustainable Energy Infrastructure Holdings Trust (**SEI**) from CareSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for CareSuper and State Street Australia Ltd as custodian for Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee for Australian Retirement Trust (the **Acquisition**).

CleanPeak is a developer and operator of renewable energy infrastructure, including solar farms and battery energy storage systems, and a supplier of renewable energy and clean energy solutions through solar and battery energy assets to large industrial and commercial customers predominantly in New South Wales. CleanPeak also provides asset management services for compressed natural gas facilities on the premises of industrial customers in Victoria, New South Wales and Queensland. In 2025, KKR & Co. Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, **KKR**) acquired a significant interest in CleanPeak.

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit, and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds.

SEI is a sustainable energy infrastructure investor which owns a diversified portfolio of small scale (up to 5MW) solar farms and battery energy storage systems in Victoria, New South Wales and South Australia as well as a compressed natural gas facility located in Queensland.

(together with its subsidiaries, KKR) acquired a significant interest in CleanPeak.

The parties develop, own and operate renewable energy assets (in particular, solar farms and BESS) and supply electricity generation in the National Electricity Market (NEM) in Victoria, NSW and South Australia. The parties also operate compressed natural gas (CNG) facilities in Queensland.

mergers.merger_description:
Hillhouse, through Hermes Bidco, proposes to acquire a majority interest in KMC Solutions Hong Kong Limited.

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.

One of Hillhouse’s investments is in Ascentium, a Singapore-based platform focusing on trust and corporate services in Asia-Pacific region, including in Australia. It provides administration, compliance, and business solution, which includes employer of record (**EOR**) or Professional Employer Organisation (**PEO**) services.

The target, KMC Solutions is a business that primarily operates in the Philippines, supplying workspace and EOR and PEO services, including to Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Hermes Bidco Limited, a subsidiary of Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd (Hillhouse), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a majority interest in KMC Solutions Hong Kong Limited (KMC Solutions), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.

One of Hillhouse’s investments is in Ascentium, a Singapore-based platform focusing on trust and corporate services in Asia-Pacific region, including in Australia. It provides administration, compliance, and business solution, which includes employer of record (EOR) or Professional Employer Organisation (PEO) services.

KMC Solutions is a business that primarily operates in the Philippines, supplying workspace and outsourced HR services including to Australia. KMC Solutions supplies physical workspaces in the Philippines, with strategic locations in financial hubs or business districts. KMC Solutions manages these workspaces through providing 24/7 access and facilitating reliable internet network infrastructure. KMC Solutions also offers end-to-end outsourced HR services centred around workforces in the Philippines. KMC Solutions recruits individuals for companies around the world (including Australian companies) interested in establishing an offshore workforce primarily from the Philippines, as well as Vietnam, Mexico and Colombia. It also handles related HR services in the Philippines such as initial talent recruitment and negotiation of employment contracts, onboarding, training and continuous payroll services. As part of its end-to-end outsourced solution, it offers EOR and PEO services.

EOR or PEO services allows organisations to establish and manage an offshore workforce. An EOR service provider will be the legal employer of the person being hired. In contrast, a PEO service provider will enter into a co-employment relationship with the organisation and the person being hired.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Hillhouse and KMC Solutions in the supply of offshore recruitment and HR services to Australian customers

b. there are alternative suppliers of offshore recruitment and HR services to Australian customers

c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of offshore corporate services and offshore recruitment and HR services to Australian customers post-acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Alcoa Gallium Holdings Pty Ltd (**AGH**, a wholly owned subsidiary of Alcoa of Australia Limited (**AoA**)), the United States Department of War (**DOW**), and the Australian Government (through Export Finance Australia (**EFA**)) propose to subscribe to shares in a recently incorporated Australian special purpose vehicle, United States-Australia Gallium Pty Ltd (**United States-Australia Gallium SPV**).

United States-Australia Gallium SPV will subsequently acquire an interest in the assets of an unincorporated joint venture (**Gallium UJV**) to be formed between United States-Australia Gallium SPV and Japan Australia Gallium Associates Pty Ltd (**JAGA**). The Gallium UJV will develop, construct and operate a gallium production plant next to AoA's Wagerup alumina refinery in Western Australia (the **Gallium Project**). As part of the acquisition, United States-Australia Gallium SPV will acquire an entitlement under the Gallium UJV to take in kind its proportionate share of the Gallium UJV’s gallium production. The acquisition comprises the proposed acquisition of shares in United States-Australia Gallium SPV and the entitlement to gallium offtake from the Gallium Project.

The Gallium UJV will start a new business in Australia to develop, manufacture and supply critical goods, specifically gallium, intended for Japanese, US and Australian industrial use, as well as other commercial purposes.

AoA is the primary vehicle for Alcoa Corporation's Australian operations. AoA is a major alumina and aluminium producer and its main operations are: mining bauxite at two locations in the Darling Range, Western Australia (Huntly and Willowdale); refining bauxite into alumina at two refineries in Western Australia; operating shipping terminals at Kwinana and Bunbury in Western Australia and Portland in Victoria; and holding a 55% interest in the Portland aluminium smelter in western Victoria, the day-to-day operations of which AoA manages.

EFA is the Australian Government's export credit agency, established as a corporation under the *Export Finance and Insurance Corporation Act 1991* (Cth). Its purpose is to support Australian export trade, overseas investment and infrastructure development that benefits Australia.  EFA's role in the Gallium Project is as an investor supporting the development of the Gallium Project.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Alcoa Gallium Holdings Pty Ltd (a wholly owned subsidiary of Alcoa of Australia Limited (AoA)), the United States Department of War (DOW), and the Australian Government (through Export Finance Australia (EFA)) applied for a notification waiver in respect of their proposed acquisition of:

• shares in a recently incorporated Australian special purpose vehicle, United States-Australia Gallium Pty Ltd (United States-Australia Gallium SPV), and

• an entitlement to a proportionate share of gallium produced by a proposed gallium production plant next to AoA’s Wagerup alumina refinery in Western Australia to be developed, constructed and operated by an unincorporated joint venture between United States-Australia Gallium SPV and Japan Australia Gallium Associates Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

AoA is a major alumina and aluminium producer, and its main operations are mining bauxite at two locations in the Darling Range, Western Australia, refining bauxite into alumina at two refineries in Western Australia, operating shipping terminals at Kwinana and Bunbury in Western Australia and Portland in Victoria, and holding a 55% interest in the Portland aluminium smelter in western Victoria.

The DOW is a United States government agency.

EFA is the Australian Government’s export credit agency, whose purpose is to support Australian export trade, overseas investment, and infrastructure development that benefits Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, the Acquisition seeks to introduce a new producer and supplier of primary gallium in Australia and does not involve the horizontal aggregation of any existing gallium production capacity.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Arena HoldCo Pty Ltd, through its subsidiary, Arena BidCo Pty Ltd (Arena) proposes to acquire:

(a) 100% of the issued share capital in GameOn Holdings Pty Ltd (**GameOn**) and its subsidiaries PlayHQ Sports Pty Ltd (**PlayHQ**), PlayHQ Sports UK Limited and PlayHQ Sports Ltd from its current shareholders; and

(b) 100% of the issued share capital in RFGI Group Pty Ltd, a shareholder of GameOn,

(together, the **Acquisition**).

Following completion of the Acquisition, it is anticipated that RFGI’s shares in GameOn will be transferred to Arena, followed by the deregistration for RFGI.

Arena is an indirect subsidiary of ASG IV, LLC and part of the broader Alpine Software Group (**ASG**) investment structure focusing on acquisitions and investments in enterprise software companies. ASG is an acquirer of software as a service (**SaaS**) businesses.

GameOn is a software platform provider that simplifies the administration and management of sports, through its two SaaS platforms, PlayHQ and TeamPay.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Arena BidCo Pty Ltd (Arena), a subsidiary of Arena HoldCo Pty Ltd and part of the Alpine Software Group (ASG), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the following, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application:

a. 100% of the issued share capital in GameOn Holdings Pty Ltd (GameOn) and its subsidiaries PlayHQ Sports Pty Ltd, PlayHQ Sports UK Limited and PlayHQ Sports Ltd from its current shareholders; and

b. 100% of the issued share capital in RFGI Group Pty Ltd (RFGI), a shareholder of GameOn, (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
29 January 2026

Alpine Investors is a private equity firm headquartered in San Francisco, California. ASG is a portfolio company of Alpine Investors which buys and invests in software as a service (SaaS) businesses in a range of industries. ASG provides software services to a range of industries including health & human services, hospitality, real estate, non-profits, compliance, manufacturing and legal. The acquirer, Arena, is part of the ASG group and is ultimately wholly owned by Alpine Fund IX which is Alpine Investors’ latest flagship fund.

The Acquisition target consists of GameOn, its subsidiaries and RFGI. GameOn is a sports software platform provider, which offers two SaaS platforms in Australia, known as PlayHQ and TeamPay.

PlayHQ simplifies the administration and management of sports for participants, clubs, leagues and national/state governing bodies, with features such as registrations, payments, fixtures, live scoring, statistics and competition management. TeamPay is a payment solution that allows for teams and players to pay game fees to one streamlined and secure platform. TeamPay is available as a standalone platform or through the PlayHQ platform. RFGI is Basketball Victoria Inc.’s holding company for its shares in GameOn.

It does not otherwise have any commercial operations or activities.

Both ASG and GameOn are active in the provision of software services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap between Alpine Investors’ portfolio companies and PlayHQ in the supply of sports management software in Australia.

b. There are a number of alternative suppliers of sports management software in Australia.

c. While there is a potential conglomerate relationship between Alpine Investors’ or ASG’s portfolio companies and GameOn, the risk of the merged entity foreclosing rival suppliers of sports management software or other software in Australia appears to be low, given the availability of other suppliers.

Given the above considerations that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Russell Investment Group Pty Ltd (**Russell**) proposes to acquire 100 per cent of the issued and outstanding shares of Zurich Investment Management Limited (**Zurich**) through a share sale agreement (the **Acquisition**).

Russell provides investment and asset management services in Australia.

Zurich is an Australian incorporated member of the global Zurich Insurance Group. The ultimate holding company is Zurich Insurance Group Ltd (incorporated in Switzerland). Zurich provides managed fund solutions to Australian investors. The Acquisition only relates to Zurich, rather than the broader Zurich Insurance Group.

Both Russell and Zurich supply investment and asset management services in Australia, including issuing interests in Australian and global equities funds, property securities funds, fixed interest funds, diversified or multi-asset funds and cash funds to Australian investors.

determination_table_content.details: Russell Investment Group Pty Ltd (Russell) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Zurich Investment Management Limited (Zurich), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
12 March 2026

The acquirer, Russell, provides investment and asset management services in Australia.

The target, Zurich, is an Australian incorporated member of the global Zurich Insurance Group. The ultimate holding company is Zurich Insurance Group Ltd (incorporated in Switzerland). Zurich provides managed fund solutions to Australian investors. The Acquisition only relates to Zurich, rather than the broader Zurich Insurance Group.

Both Russell and Zurich’s supply investment and asset management services in Australia, including issuing interests in Australian and global equities funds, property securities funds, fixed interest funds, diversified or multi-asset funds and cash funds to Australian investors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Russell and Zurich in the supply of investment and asset management services

b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of investment and asset management services is low

c. there are alternative suppliers of investment and asset management services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Admiral AU Bidco Pty Ltd (**Admiral Bidco**) intends to acquire 100% of the Eptec Defence Solutions (**EDS**) business by acquiring 100% of the issued shares in Eptec Holdco Pty Ltd and, indirectly, its subsidiaries Eptec Marine Pty Ltd and Eptec Bidco Pty Ltd (together the **Target Group)**, from the current shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

Admiral Bidco is a newly established fund that is ultimately controlled by Arlington Capital Partners (**ACP**), a US-based private equity investment firm that acquires, manages, and grows portfolio companies in sectors such as defence & aerospace, government & technology, healthcare, and business services software.

Eptec Group carries on two businesses in Australia: EDS and Eptec Infrastructure Solutions. EDS specialises in the maintenance, preservation, and remediation of naval vessels and defence infrastructure. Prior to the Acquisition, the Eptec Group will be restructured so that the Eptec Infrastructure Solutions business will be transferred out of the Target Group and retained by the seller.

Prior to the Acquisition, the Eptec Group will be restructured so that the Eptec Infrastructure Solutions business will be transferred out of the Target Group and retained by the seller.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

mergers.merger_description:
Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Pty Ltd (**Princeton Digital Group Australia**), as trustee of Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Trust, proposes to acquire land located in Campbelltown, NSW from MH Property No. 1 Pty Ltd (**MH Property**)(the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is for the purpose of building, owning and operating a data centre in Australia. Once developed, Princeton Digital Group Australia intends to supply data centre facilities and associated infrastructure to tenants in Australia.

The land is currently vacant and contains no existing fixtures, structures or buildings. The land will be acquired by way of a land title transfer from MH Property to Princeton Digital Group Australia .

Princeton Digital Group Australia is an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Princeton Digital Group Limited (**Princeton Digital Group**), a Singapore headquartered group which operates a data centre business across more than 20 data centres in seven countries – Singapore, Japan, China, Indonesia, India, Malaysia and South Korea. Princeton Digital Group does not currently own or operate any data centre assets in Australia.

MH Property is an Australian property developer which owns land in New South Wales.

mergers.merger_description:
Mizuho Securities Co. Ltd (**Mizuho**) proposes to acquire an interest of between 61.60% and 78.30% in Avendus Capital Private Limited (**Avendus**) (the **Acquisition**).

Mizuho is a subsidiary of Mizuho Financial Group, a full service Japanese financial services company. Mizuho through its connected entities, supplies financial services within Australia, including business banking, financial advisory and auto finance and car leasing.

Avendus is an Indian investment bank and financial services firm that provides bespoke solutions in the areas of investment banking, institutional equities, wealth management, asset management and credit solutions. Founded in 1999, Avendus operates in India, the United States, Singapore and United Kingdom through its subsidiaries. It is a Securities and Exchange Board of India registered merchant banker.

Avendus has limited operations in Australia, supplying some research analyst and stock broking services via its wholly owned subsidiary, Spark Institutional Equities Private Limited.

mergers.merger_description:
CRH Americas Products, Inc., (**CRH Americas**) and Oldcastle Infrastructure, Inc. (**Oldcastle**) propose to acquire 100% of the partnership interests and voting rights in Axius Water Parent LP (**Axius Water**) (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition will result in CRH Americas and Oldcastle having sole control of Axius Water.

CRH Americas is a Delaware-incorporated intermediate holding company. It does not directly manufacture or supply goods or services.

Oldcastle is a US-based supplier of engineered concrete products related to water, communications, energy and transportation infrastructure with a focus on sustainability. Oldcastle has no presence in Australia.

CRH Americas and Oldcastle are owned by CRH Plc (**CRH**), a global supplier of building materials and infrastructure products headquartered in Ireland and listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

CRH has various interests in Australia, including CTC Precast Pty Ltd (**Civilmart**). Civilmart supplies precast concrete infrastructure products, including stormwater and drainage components, stormwater quality improvement devices, sewer chambers and pump stations, and domestic aerated wastewater treatment systems.

Hydro International is a CRH subsidiary that supplies water filtration products globally. Hydro International supplies a very small volume of sludge screens, and stormwater separators and filters, which are used in primary treatment of wastewater, into Australia via direct supply channels.

Axius Water is a US based supplier of nutrient removal solutions for municipal and industrial wastewater and water treatment processes via a portfolio of brands (Aero-Mod, atac, EDI, Eosi, MITAwt, Napier-Reid, Nexom and triplepoint). Axius Water is currently majority-owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by various subsidiaries of KKR & Co. Inc.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: CRH Plc (CRH), via CRH Americas Products, Inc. (CRH Americas) and Oldcastle Infrastructure, Inc. (Oldcastle), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the partnership interests and voting rights in Axius Water Parent LP (Axius Water), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ultimate acquirer, CRH, is a global supplier of building materials and infrastructure products headquartered in Ireland and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. CRH Americas and Oldcastle would have sole control of Axius Water.

CRH Americas is an intermediate holding company which does not directly manufacture or supply goods or services. Oldcastle is a US-based supplier of engineered concrete products related to water, communications, energy and transportation infrastructure with a focus on sustainability. It has no presence in Australia.

CTC Precast Pty Ltd (Civilmart), which is indirectly owned by CRH, is a supplier of precast concrete infrastructure products in Australia, including stormwater and drainage components, stormwater quality improvement devices, sewer chambers and pump stations, and domestic aerated wastewater treatment systems.

Hydro International is a CRH subsidiary that supplies water filtration products, including sludge screens and stormwater separators and filters, into Australia The target, Axius Water, is a US based supplier of nutrient removal solutions and process-intensive treatment for municipal and industrial waste-water and water treatment processes via brands including Aero-Mod, atac, EDI, Eosi, MITAwt, Napier-Reid, Nexom and triplepoint. Axius Water has minimal export sales into Australia and no operations, assets, employees or subsidiaries in Australia.

CRH and its controlled portfolio companies, and Axius Water, both supply wastewater treatment solutions in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. Axius Water has a small presence in Australia

b. while there may be a small horizontal or complementary relationship between the products and services supplied by CRH (including its controlled portfolio companies) and Axius Water, there are alternative suppliers of these products and services in Australia.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: FC Group Holdings Corporation & ors - Fluent Retail
mergers.merger_description:
Fluent Retail Pty Ltd (**Fluent Retail**) operates in the retail technology and enterprise software industry, supplying cloud-based distributed order management system software as a SaaS platform. Its solutions serve omnichannel retailers, e-commerce businesses, and grocery and fashion retailers, enabling real-time inventory visibility and order orchestration across multiple fulfillment locations.

Fluent Retail, FC Group Holdings Corporation (**FC Group Holdings**), Fluent Commerce MidCo (**FC MidCo**) and Fluent Commerce Holdings, Inc (**FC HoldCo**) propose to undertake an internal reorganisation resulting in a US redomicile that will occur in 3 stages (together, the **Acquisition**).

Firstly, all Fluent Retail shares will be transferred to FC Group Holdings in exchange for equivalent classes of FC Group Holdings shares. Following the transfer, Fluent Retail shareholders will be the sole shareholders of FC Group Holdings, holding the same number and proportion of equivalent shares as they held in Fluent Retail immediately prior (i.e. the overall ownership structure of the group will remain unchanged).

Second, immediately after the transfer to FC Group Holdings, all the shares of Fluent Retail are immediately transferred to FC Group Holdings's wholly owned subsidiary, FC MidCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares.

Third, on completion of the transfer to FC MidCo, all the shares of Fluent Retail are immediately transferred by FC MidCo to its wholly owned subsidiary, FC HoldCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares.

FC Group Holdings, FC MidCo and FC HoldCo are vehicles for the purpose of the Acquisition and do not engage in business or otherwise supply any goods or services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
FC Group Holdings Corporation (FC Group Holdings), Fluent Commerce MidCo Inc. (FC MidCo) and Fluent Commerce Holdings Inc. (FC HoldCo) applied for a notification waiver in respect of a US redomicile occurring in three stages:

• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail Pty Ltd (Fluent Retail) shares to FC Group Holdings in exchange for equivalent classes of FC Group Holdings shares, following which the Fluent Retail shareholders will be the sole shareholders of FC Group Holdings, holding the same number and proportion of equivalent shares as they held in Fluent Retail immediately prior

• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail shares from FC Group Holdings to its wholly-owned subsidiary, FC MidCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares

• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail shares from FC MidCo to its wholly-owned subsidiary, FC HoldCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Fluent Retail operates in the retail technology and enterprise software industry, supplying cloud-based distributed order management system (OMS) software as a SaaS platform. Its solutions serve omnichannel retailers, e-commerce businesses, and grocery and fashion retailers, enabling real-time inventory visibility and order orchestration across multiple fulfillment locations.

FC Group Holdings, FC MidCo and FC HoldCo are vehicles for the purpose of the Acquisition and do not engage in business or otherwise supply any goods or services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal consolidation, removal of a competitor, or change to Fluent Retail’s competitive position as a result of the Acquisition.

In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

The ACCC also considered the potential application of section 51ABD of the Act. The application states that on the facts of this matter, including the particular entities and structures involved, it was not clear that the Acquisition fell within that provision. Having regard to the particular circumstances, including the consequences of non-notification in reliance on the exemption if it was found not to apply, the ACCC considered it appropriate to make a determination on the basis that the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the Acquisition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Sydney Water - Windsor Sewerage Scheme Assets
mergers.merger_description:
Sydney Water Corporation (**SWC**) proposes to acquire from the Hawkesbury City Council (**HCC**) the following assets of the Windsor Sewerage Scheme in New South Wales:

* the South Windsor wastewater treatment plant; and
* the pumping stations and reticulation system (pipes and mains) required to supply wastewater services to customers in the Hawkesbury City local government area (the **Acquisition**).

SWC is a State Owned Corporation established under the *Sydney Water Act 1994* and *State Owned Corporations Act 1989*. SWC provides water and wastewater services across the greater Sydney metropolitan region, but it currently does not provide wastewater services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area. However, Sydney Water does provide drinking water services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area.

The HCC is a body politic of the State of NSW constituted under the *Local Government Act 1993*. HCC provides wastewater services to properties in part of the Hawkesbury City local government area through the Windsor Sewerage Scheme. The Windsor Sewerage Scheme comprises two wastewater treatment plants at McGraths Hill and South Windsor, 21 pumping stations and the associated reticulation systems.

The McGraths Hill wastewater treatment plant will continue to be owned and operated by the HCC pending delivery of a sewage pumping station at McGraths Hill at which point it is expected that the McGraths Hill wastewater treatment plant will be bypassed.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Sydney Water Corporation (SWC) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of certain assets of Hawkesbury City Council (HCC), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The assets to be acquired are part of the Windsor Sewerage Scheme and include the South Windsor wastewater treatment plant and the pumping stations and reticulation systems required to supply wastewater services to customers in the Hawkesbury City local government area.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, SWC, is a state owned corporation established under the Sydney Water Act 1994 (NSW) and State Owned Corporations Act 1989 (NSW). SWC provides water and wastewater services across the greater Sydney metropolitan region, but it currently does not provide wastewater services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area. SWC does provide drinking water services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area.

The HCC is a body politic of the State of NSW constituted under the Local Government Act 1993 (NSW). HCC provides wastewater services to properties in part of the Hawkesbury City local government area through the Windsor Sewerage Scheme. The Windsor Sewerage Scheme comprises two wastewater treatment plants at McGraths Hill and South Windsor, 21 pumping stations and the associated reticulation systems. The McGraths Hill wastewater treatment plant is not subject to the Acquisition SWC and the HCC do not compete for customers. Customers within the Windsor Sewerage Scheme area of operations cannot currently contract with SWC to provide wastewater services, and customers outside the Windsor Sewerage Scheme area of operations cannot contract with the HCC to provide wastewater services using the Windsor Sewerage Scheme.

SWC and the HCC also acquire chemicals to be used in their respective wastewater systems, in particular, aluminium chlorohydrate, biosolids polymer ferric caustic soda, sodium hypochlorite, and methanol.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between SWC and HCC in the supply of wastewater services to customers within the Windsor Sewerage Scheme’s areas of operation in the Hawkesbury City local government area

b. the pricing of wastewater services is regulated and set by the Independent Regulatory and Pricing Tribunal of NSW

c. while the parties have a small overlap in the acquisition of chemicals used in wastewater systems in Australia, any increment arising from the Acquisition is very limited.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
MH South Brisbane Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, **Star Hotels Group**) propose to acquire 100% of the business assets and freehold of Shafston Hotel, a hospitality venue in East Brisbane.

Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs and hotels on the eastern seaboard of Queensland and in South Australia. Star Hotels Group’s existing presence in the local area surrounding Shafston Hotel includes various retail liquor stores, as well as:

* Hotel West End, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store
* Hotel Kingsley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, and accommodation, and
* The Garage Fortitude Valley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store.

Shafston Hotel offers on-premises liquor, dining, and gaming, as well as a drive-through retail liquor store.

Following completion of the Acquisition, Shafston Hotel will continue to operate under its existing name and format and will be integrated into Star Hotel Group's portfolio.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: MH South Brisbane Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, Star Hotels Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the business assets and freehold of Shafston Hotel, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels Group, is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs and hotels on the eastern seaboard of Queensland and in South Australia. Star Hotels Group operates various liquor retailers in the local area surrounding the target, as well as 3 hospitality venues:

• Hotel West End, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store

• Hotel Kingsley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, and accommodation, and

• The Garage Fortitude Valley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store.

The target, Shafston Hotel, is a hospitality venue in East Brisbane offering food, liquor, and gaming, as well as a drive-through retail liquor store.

Following the Acquisition, Shafston Hotel will be integrated into Star Hotels Group’s portfolio.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined shares of the supply of on-premises liquor, dining, and gaming, as well as the retail supply of packaged liquor, within the local area surrounding Shafston Hotel are estimated to be low

b. there are alternative suppliers of retail liquor and on-premises liquor, dining, and gaming in the local area and the broader Brisbane area.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Direct Couriers (Aust) Pty Ltd (**DC**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Direct Couriers Group Pty Ltd (together, **Direct Couriers**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Phoenix Transport Holdings Pty Ltd, along with its 100% wholly owned subsidiary, Phoenix Distribution (NSW) Pty Ltd (together, **Phoenix Transport**).

Direct Couriers is an Australian provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery and warehousing services. It services logistics, freight forwarder, commercial and retail customers across Australia and New Zealand, and operates a number of sub-brands including ‘Direct Cold’ and ‘Direct Road’. It focuses on same day point-to-point services.

Anacacia V Fund A and Anacacia V Fund B (together, the **Fund**), which are both unregistered Australian unlisted unit trusts, together own 49.7% of the shares in DC, with the remaining 50.3% held by existing and past management, as well as a passive investor.

Phoenix Transport is also an Australian provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery and warehousing services, operating in New South Wales and Queensland. Although Phoenix Transport has national customers, its coverage is primarily limited to Brisbane and Sydney. Phoenix Transport services a mix of logistics, retail and commercial customers, with a stronger focus on third-party warehousing and distribution, larger-scale heavy-freight dedicated hire work, and on- transport for warehouse relocations and other similar project work.

determination_table_content.details: Direct Couriers (Aust) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Direct Couriers Group Pty Ltd (Direct Couriers), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its acquisition of 100% of the shares in Phoenix Transport Holdings Pty Ltd and its wholly owned subsidiary, Phoenix Distribution (NSW) Pty Ltd (together, Phoenix Transport), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, Direct Couriers, is an Australian provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery, and warehousing services. It services logistics, freight forwarder, commercial, and retail customers across Australia and New Zealand, and operates a number of sub-brands including ‘Direct Cold’ and ‘Direct Road’. Direct Couriers focuses on same day point-to-point services.

Phoenix Transport is also an Australian-headquartered provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery, and warehousing services. It operates in New South Wales and Queensland, with coverage primarily limited to Brisbane and Sydney. Phoenix Transport services a mix of logistics, retail, and commercial customers, with a stronger focus on third-party warehousing and distribution, large-scale heavy-freight dedicated hire work, and on-transport for warehouse relocations and other similar project work.

Both parties supply courier and interstate delivery services in Queensland and New South Wales.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. the parties’ combined share of supply of courier and interstate delivery services in Queensland, New South Wales, and nationally is estimated to be low and the increase in share arising from the Acquisition would be small

• b. there are alternative suppliers of courier and interstate delivery services in Queensland, New South Wales, and nationally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
SOILCO Pty Ltd (**SOILCO**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Garden Mediums Pty Ltd (trading as **Go Grow**) from Lumime Pty Ltd and trustee of the Honey & Nut Unit Trust (the **Acquisition**).

SOILCO operates organic waste recycling facilities in New South Wales, ACT and Victoria, including a facility at Tweed Heads in northern NSW. SOILCO’s facilities process food and garden organic (**FOGO**) waste from local councils, blending it with forestry and agricultural feedstocks (and other suppliers’ inputs) to manufacture compost and soil improvement products. SOILCO supplies these products for a range of applications including, including urban landscaping and projects and agriculture and horticulture production systems (orchards, viticulture, turf farms, nurseries, and greenlife).

SOILCOs majority shareholder, Australian Waste Management Investments is part of the I Squared Capital group (**I Squared**). I Squared is a global infrastructure investment firm headquartered in the United States,

Go Grow manufactures and supplies potting mixes, fertilisers and garden products from a single site in Ballina NSW. Part of Go Grow’s business involves processing locally sourced garden waste to produce compost and mulch which is supplied directly to retail customers, as well as farms and nurseries. Go Grow does not acquire or process FOGO waste.

The acquirer, SOILCO, operates organic waste recycling facilities in New South Wales, ACT and Victoria, including a facility at Tweed Heads in northern NSW.

SOILCO supplies these products for a range of applications including urban landscaping and projects and agriculture and horticulture production systems (orchards, viticulture, turf farms, nurseries, and greenlife).

SOILCOs majority shareholder, Australian Waste Management Investments is part of the I Squared Capital group (I Squared). I Squared is a global infrastructure investment firm headquartered in the United States.

The target, Go Grow, manufactures and supplies potting mixes, fertilisers and garden products from a single site in Ballina NSW.

Part of Go Grow’s business involves processing locally sourced garden waste to produce compost and mulch which is supplied directly to retail customers, as well as farms and nurseries. Go Grow does not acquire or process FOGO waste.

mergers.merger_description:
Treysta Wealth Management Pty Ltd (**Treysta**) proposes to acquire the businesses and related assets of Locumsgroup Asset Management Services Pty Ltd, Locumsgroup Private Accounting Services Pty Limited and Locums Nominees Pty Limited (together, **Locumsgroup**).

Treysta supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney, Brisbane and the Gold Coast. Treysta is partly owned by a wholly-owned subsidiary of AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Limited, which invests in small-to-medium sized financial advisory and accounting businesses. AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Limited is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty Ltd, whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management LP, a global investment management firm specialising in alternative investments, and Azimut Holding S.p.a., a global asset and wealth management business listed on the Milan Stock Exchange.

Locumsgroup supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (AZ Next Generation Advisory) applied for a notification waiver in respect of a proposed acquisition by Treysta Wealth Management Pty Ltd (Treysta) of the businesses and related assets of Locumsgroup Asset Management Pty Ltd, Locumsgroup Private Accounting Services Pty Limited and Locums Nominees Pty Limited (together, Locumsgroup), as described in the transaction document provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
27 January 2026

AZ Next Generation Advisory’s subsidiary, Associated Planners Financial Services Pty Ltd, owns a minority interest in Treysta.

Treysta, supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney, Brisbane and the Gold Coast. AZ Next Generation Advisory invests in small to medium sized financial advisory and accounting businesses and, according to its website, has made several other acquisitions of financial services businesses in recent years.

AZ Next Generation Advisory is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty Ltd whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management LP (Oaktree) and Azimut Holding S.p.a. (Azimut). Oaktree is a global investment management firm and Azimut is a global asset and wealth management business.

Locumsgroup, supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney.

Treysta and Locumsgroup both supply financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney. Several of the AZ Next Generation Advisory’s connected entities also carry on financial services businesses in Sydney.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided by the applicant, including in the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided by the applicant and publicly available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. While there is horizontal overlap between the services supplied by Treysta, its connected entities and Locumsgroup, the parties’ combined market shares in the supply of financial planning, tax and accounting services in Sydney and nationally are low and the Acquisition will result in a small increment to Treysta and its connected entities’ market shares in the supply of those services.

b. The market concentration in the supply of financial planning and tax and accounting services is low and there are several thousand competing suppliers of such services nationally, including many competing suppliers in Sydney, that are expected to continue to constrain the parties.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given the above consideration that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Altor Fund Manager AB (**Altor**), through CAVE BidCo Oy (**Cave**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Evac Holding Oy (**Evac**).

Altor is a Swedish-based private equity group. Altor is a well-established investment firm with a portfolio of companies across various sectors. Cave is a Finnish limited liability investment vehicle controlled by Altor that does not supply any goods or services.

The target, Evac, is a Finnish limited liability company. Evac is currently majority owned by Bridgepoint Europe V Investments S.à r.l. along with several management minority shareholders. Evac’s key brands are “Evac”, “Cathelco” and “HEM”. Evac products include integrated water and waste management solutions, primarily for the marine industry and, to some extent, for land-based applications. Evac has no Australian subsidiaries or Australian presence, but generates some revenue in Australia from sales to distributors based in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Altor Fund Manager AB (Altor), via CAVE BidCo Oy (Cave), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in Evac Holding Oy (Evac), as described in the transaction document provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
30 January 2026

Altor is a Swedish-based private equity group, with a portfolio of companies across various sectors. Cave is a Finnish limited liability investment vehicle ultimately controlled by Altor that does not supply any goods or services.

The target, Evac, is a Finnish limited liability company that operates under key brands “Evac”, “Cathelco” and “HEM”. Evac supplies integrated water and waste management systems and specialist marine equipment, including vacuum sewage collection, wet and dry waste handling, wastewater treatment, freshwater generation (reverse osmosis), marine growth prevention, corrosion protection, ultrasonic antifouling and water makers. Evac also supplies some water and waste solutions for land-based applications.

Evac products are sold in Australia via Australian channel partners, but Evac does not otherwise have any Australian subsidiaries or an Australian presence.

The application indicates that none of the connected entities controlled by Altor, including Cave, supply any products in Australia that overlap with Evac’s products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap in Australia between the products and services supplied by the parties, and there are no vertical relationships.

b. Evac has a small presence in Australia.

Given that the ACCC considers that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, it is not necessary to reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc. (**SPE**) and Sony Music Entertainment (Japan) Inc. (**SMEJ**) (together with Sony Group Corporation, Sony) propose to acquire 100% of the voting securities of DHX Entertainment (USA) Inc. (**DHX**).

The acquirer, Sony, is a global entertainment and technology business headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and New York Stock Exchange. It operates in sectors such as game and network services, music, pictures, entertainment services, imaging solutions, and financial services.

The target, DHX, is a holding company with a controlling interest in Peanuts Worldwide. Peanuts Worldwide owns, manages, and licenses copyrights and other intellectual property rights of the Peanuts brand. This includes characters such as Snoopy and Charlie Brown. The family of Charles M Shulz, creator of Peanuts, will retain its 20% stake in the brand.

Sony and Peanuts Worldwide are both active in character intellectual property licensing for various customer use cases and the licensing of film and TV content related to those characters, globally and in Australia.

mergers.merger_description:
TDR Capital LLP (**TDR**) and Estoril Coinvest SCSp (**Estoril**) propose to enter a Shareholders Agreement, pursuant to which TDR will hold a majority interest while Estoril will hold a minority interest in Escode.

TDR is a private equity firm based in the United Kingdom. Estoril is a co-investment vehicle managed by a private equity firm.

The target, Escode, is a UK headquartered business active in providing software escrow and related services. Escode primarily supplies software escrow services, whereby software code and data or other business critical information is deposited by a licensor in Escode's physical or virtual vaults, and released to the licensee where the release criteria are met. In Australia, Escode also supplies verification services.

Escode is a division of NCC Group plc (**NCC**), which is also based in the UK and is a supplier of cybersecurity services. NCC launched its Escode brand in Australia in 2024.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
TDR Capital LLP (TDR) and Estoril Coinvest SCSp (Estoril) propose to acquire 100% of the two NCC Group plc (NCC) entities that comprise the Escode business:

a. 100% of the entire issued share capital of Escode Jersey Holdco Limited (a UK incorporated company), and

b. the entire membership interest of NCC Group Software Resilience (Americas) LLC (a US-incorporated company), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition will result in TDR acquiring a majority interest and Estoril a minority interest in Escode.

TDR and Estoril applied for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
17 March 2026

One of the acquirers, TDR, is a private equity firm based in the United Kingdom. The other acquirer, Estoril, is a co-investment vehicle managed by a private equity firm.

The target, Escode, is a UK-headquartered business active in providing software escrow and related services. Escode primarily supplies software escrow services, whereby software code and data or other business critical information is deposited by a licensor in Escode's physical or virtual vaults, and released to the licensee where the release criteria are met. In Australia, Escode also supplies verification services.

Escode is a division of NCC, which is also based in the UK and is a supplier of cybersecurity services. NCC launched its Escode brand in Australia in 2024.

The acquirers and their connected entities do not provide software escrow and verification services in Australia or globally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal or non-horizontal overlap between the acquirers (or their connected entities) and Escode in the supply of software escrow and verification services in Australia

b. the presence of alternative suppliers of software escrow and verification services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of Hills Veterinary Centre.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The Hills Veterinary Centre is a full-service veterinary practice in the Blackwood area of South Australia. The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details: VC Operations (WA) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, Vets Central), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the business assets of Hills Veterinary Centre, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.
14 April 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The Hills Veterinary Centre is first opinion veterinary practice that provides routine, planned and emergency (excluding after-hours) veterinary services. Hills Veterinary Centre is located in the Blackwood area of South Australia.

Vets Central owns one clinic in the Blackwood area, Familypet Vet Coromandel Valley, which is located approximately 3.5km from Hills Veterinary Centre.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, including the revenue of the parties in Australia and relevant cumulative acquisitions, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notifications thresholds would be met.

The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the information available to us. It considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process, including via further information gathering and consultation with third parties.

This may include a focus on:

a. the horizontal overlap between Vets Central and Hills Veterinary Centre in the supply of veterinary services in Blackwood, South Australia

b. the extent to which the parties are likely to face competition from alternative suppliers of veterinary services in the areas surrounding Blackwood, South Australia.

Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the notification waiver determination applied for.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
