determination_table_content.details: Regent Motors Pty Ltd (Regent Motors Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the portfolio of franchised automotive dealerships and associated real property located in Mandurah, Western Australia, currently operated by WA Automotive Pty Ltd, controlled by the Lane Group, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
9 April 2026

The acquirer, Regent Motors Group is an Australian-owned automotive retail group headquartered in Perth, Western Australia.

Regent Motors Group owns and operates franchised motor vehicle dealerships across Western Australia and the Northern Territory, supplying new and used motor vehicles across a range of OEM brands including Ford, Toyota, Hyundai, Suzuki, BMW, Mini, MG, Geely, GMSV, Isuzu Ute, Subaru, and Volkswagen. Regent Motors Group also provides vehicle servicing and repairs, genuine parts and accessories, fleet sales and service, and facilitates finance, insurance, and aftercare products in connection with vehicle transactions.

The target, the Lane Group dealerships, comprises of six franchised retail dealerships and associated storage facilities in Mandurah, WA, supplying new and used motor vehicles for the following brands: Ford, Mitsubishi, Hyundai, Kia, Nissan, and Suzuki. Lane Group also provides vehicle servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet services, and finance and insurance facilitation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no overlap between the Regent Motors Group and the Lane Group in the supply of new and used motor vehicles and associated services in Mandurah and the surrounding areas

b. while there is a small horizontal overlap between the Regent Motors Group and the Lane Group in the broader area surrounding Mandurah (including Perth and Bunbury), there are independent alternatives suppliers of new and used cars in Mandurah, Perth, Bunbury and Rockingham The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Discovery Holiday Parks Pty Limited (**Discover Holiday Parks**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Discovery Parks Holdings Pty Limited (**Discovery Parks Holdings**), proposes to acquire the business of operating and managing "Habitat Noosa Everglades Eco Camp" (**Habitat Noosa**).

Habitat Noosa is an integrated hospitality business comprising accommodation options for backpackers, couples and families; a 200 seat bar and bistro with an on site micro brewery; and Noosa Everglades eco safari boat tours. Associated brands include Everglades Eco Safaris, Kangarooms Noosa Everglades, Eco Brewing Co and Coothara Bar.

Discovery Holiday Parks, under its trading names Discovery Parks and Discovery Resorts, owns and operates over 90 accommodation offerings throughout Australia. Discovery Holiday Parks provides short-stay holiday accommodation, worker accommodation, and long-stay accommodation, as well as tours and food and beverage services within the tourism and hospitality industry. Discovery Holiday Parks does not have a presence in Noosa, Queensland or on the Sunshine Coast – its nearest property is in Hervey Bay.

Associated brands include Everglades Eco Safaris, Kangarooms Noosa Everglades, Eco Brewing Co and Coothara Bar.

mergers.merger_description:
Francisco Partners Management, LP (**Francisco Partners**) is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London. Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate across multiple sectors, including in Australia.

CSHC Buyerco, LLC (together with its connected entities, **Capsa Healthcare**) manufactures and supplies healthcare equipment and technology-enabled point-of-care hardware, including mobile medication carts, pharmacy automation hardware, clinical workstations, and medical supply chain software. Capsa Healthcare does not have a physical presence in Australia but does generate a proportion of its global revenue from supply to Australian customers.

Pursuant to a share sale agreement and as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application, the funds advised by Francisco Partners which are currently controlled by the acquiring vehicles will acquire 100% control and ownership of Capsa Healthcare.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC, Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC, Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC, and Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub II LLC, which are each a vehicle controlled by funds advised by Francisco Partners Management, LP (Francisco Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of 100% of the business of Capsa Healthcare (defined below), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition is a share sale which involves the following:

• Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with and into CSHC Buyerco, LLC, the direct parent company of Capsa Solutions, LLC.

• Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with and into CSHC Holdings, LLC, the indirect parent company of Capsa Solutions, LLC.

• Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC and Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub II, LLC are proposing to merge with and into CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker Corp., LLC, respectively, which hold equity interests in CSHC Holdings, LLC.

CSHC Buyerco, LLC, CSHC Holdings, LLC, Capsa Solutions, LLC, CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC, and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker Corp., LLC together form and are connected entities of Capsa Healthcare.

As a result of the Acquisition, funds advised by Francisco Partners will have 100% control and ownership of the business of Capsa Healthcare.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
2 April 2026

Francisco Partners, which is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.

Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate across multiple sectors, including in Australia. The acquiring vehicles are controlled by funds advised by Francisco Partners.

The target, Capsa Healthcare, manufactures and supplies healthcare equipment and technology-enabled point-of-care hardware, including mobile medication carts, pharmacy automation hardware, clinical workstations, and medical supply chain software. Capsa Healthcare does not have a physical presence in Australia but does generate a proportion of its global revenue from supply to Australian customers.

Francisco Partners has two portfolio companies which operate in the healthcare sector in Australia – Merative, which supplies data, analytics, and software products for healthcare and government social services, and Sermo, which supplies social networking and platform services to healthcare professionals. However, Francisco Partners states that neither of these portfolio companies offer substitutable products to those which are supplied by Capsa Healthcare in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between Francisco Partners (including its portfolio companies with operations in Australia) and Capsa Healthcare in the supply of technology-enabled healthcare equipment in Australia, and there are no vertical relationships in this respect

b. while there may be a small complementary relationship between the healthcare-related products and services provided by Capsa Healthcare, Merative, and Sermo, there are alternative suppliers of these types of products and services in Australia

c. Capsa Healthcare has a small presence in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Yancoal Australia Limited (**Yancoal**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Kestrel Coal Group Pty Ltd (**Kestrel Coal Group**) from investment funds and/or their investors which are managed by EMR Capital Management Limited or EMR Capital Advisors Pty Ltd and Adaro Capital Limited (the **Acquisition**).

Kestrel Coal Group holds an 80% interest in the Kestrel Joint Venture, an unincorporated joint venture which owns and operates the Kestrel Coal Mine in Queensland. The remaining 20% of the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture is held by Mitsui Kestrel Coal Investment Pty Ltd.

Kestrel Coal Group was formed to hold the 80% interest in the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture and does not itself supply goods or services. The Acquisition will result in Yancoal holding an 80% interest in the Kestrel Coal Mine.

Yancoal produces thermal coal from mines located in New South Wales, Queensland, and Western Australia. It also produces a smaller volume of metallurgical coal.

The Kestrel Coal Mine is located in Queensland’s Bowen Basin and produces metallurgical coal, in addition to a smaller volume of thermal coal.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Yancoal Australia Limited (Yancoal) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in Kestrel Coal Group Pty Ltd (Kestrel Coal Group) from investment funds and/or their investors which are managed by EMR Capital Management Limited, EMR Capital Advisors Pty Ltd, and/or Adaro Capital Limited, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
13 April 2026

The acquirer, Yancoal, produces thermal coal and, to a lesser extent, metallurgical coal, from mines located in New South Wales, Queensland, and Western Australia.

The target, Kestrel Coal Group, holds an 80% interest in the Kestrel Joint Venture, an unincorporated joint venture which owns and operates the Kestrel Coal Mine in Queensland. The remaining 20% of the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture is held by Mitsui Kestrel Coal Investment Pty Ltd.

The Kestrel Coal Mine is located in Queensland’s Bowen Basin and produces metallurgical coal, and to a lesser extent, thermal coal. The Acquisition would result in Yancoal holding an 80% interest in the Kestrel Coal Mine.

Both Yancoal and the Kestrel Coal Mine supply metallurgical and thermal coal from mines in Australia, with the majority of supply being exported to the global seaborne coal market.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small overlap in coal production by the parties in Australia as Yancoal primarily produces thermal coal and the Kestrel Coal Mine primarily produces metallurgical coal, and both parties are primarily exporters

b. the parties’ combined share of supply of thermal and metallurgical coal in Australia is low and the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small

c. there are alternative suppliers of both thermal and metallurgical coal in Australia and globally.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
PH Medical Centres Pty Ltd (**PHMC**) proposes to acquire the business and assets of the Riverstone Family Medical Practice (**RFMP**) from Riverstone GP Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

PHMC is part of the Partnered Health Group and an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Partnered Health Pty Ltd (**PHPL**).

PHMC together with other PHPL subsidiaries (the **PH Primary Care Group**) provide primary care services with more than 60 general practice clinics and medical centres across Australia, engaging more than 600 general practitioners. The PH Primary Care Group also operates skin cancer clinics, allied health providers (physiotherapy and dietetics) and TeleWell, a telehealth platform which provides 24/7 continuous access to general practitioners.

The Partnered Health Group also operates a healthcare platform which provides corporate and occupational healthcare solutions alongside its primary care network.

PHPL is part of a larger group of companies which are ultimately controlled by Quadrant Private Equity (**QPE**) through Quadrant Private Equity No. 6 (**QPE Fund 6**).

RFMP is a medical centre located at 71 Garfield Road East, Riverstone, NSW 2765, which provides primary care services through 12 general practitioners (plus four Registrars) and three allied health professionals.

16 April 2026

The acquirer, PHMC, is part of the Partnered Health Group and an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Partnered Health Pty Ltd (PHPL). PHPL is part of a larger group of companies which are ultimately controlled by Quadrant Private Equity through Quadrant Private Equity No. 6.

The PH Primary Care Group and the RFMP clinic both supply general practice medical services.

b. there is no competitive overlap with Quadrant Private Equity No. 6 Fund or other Quadrant Private Equity managed entities.

c. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical services near Riverstone, NSW.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

mergers.merger_description:
Apollo Purchaser, Inc (**Apollo Purchaser**), a special purpose vehicle which is ultimately controlled by vehicles comprising funds advised by Francisco Partners Management L.P. (together with its affiliated funds, **Francisco Partners**), proposes to acquire all of the issued and outstanding shares in Blackline Safety Corp. (together with its subsidiaries, **Blackline Safety**) from its current shareholders by way of a statutory plan of arrangement under the provisions of the *Business Corporations Act* (Alberta) (the **Acquisition**). The shareholders of Blackline Safety, other than certain Rollover Shareholders (as defined and described below), will receive cash as consideration (as an upfront payment on closing and by way of a potential future contingent payment) for the Acquisition.

Francisco Partners is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.

Blackline Safety is a public company registered in Canada, headquartered in Calgary and listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSE: BLN). Blackline Safety specialises in designing and manufacturing hardware enabled Software-as-a-Service products for worker safety monitoring. Its products include wearable devices, personal and area gas monitors and cloud-based data analytics software. Through deploying Blackline Safety’s products, employers can monitor and communicate with lone workers and detect possible leakage of gas or other hazardous materials that may pose a threat to workers. The accompanying software streams data from devices in the field and enables employers to manage emergencies.

Blackline Safety has a worldwide presence with customers in more than 75 countries, including Australia. Blackline Safety has an Australian subsidiary, Blackline Safety Australia Pty Ltd.

Apollo Purchaser will obtain sole control of Blackline Safety by way of the Acquisition and Blackline Safety will transition to a private company. A number of Blackline Safety’s existing shareholders, namely DAK Capital Inc. (**DAK**), the Lowy Family Group, Cody Slater (the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Blackline Safety) and Brad Gilewich (President of DAK and a nominee director of Blackline Safety), and certain affiliates (together, the **Rollover Shareholders**) will each retain minority interests in Blackline Safety by way of a rollover arrangement.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Apollo Purchaser, is a special purpose vehicle which is ultimately controlled by vehicles comprising funds advised by Francisco Partners Management L.P. (together with its affiliated funds, Francisco Partners). Francisco Partners is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.

The target, Blackline Safety, is a public company registered in Canada, headquartered in Calgary and listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSE: BLN). Blackline Safety specialises in designing and manufacturing hardware enabled Software-as-a-Service products for worker safety monitoring. Its products include wearable devices, personal and area gas monitors and cloud-based data analytics software. Blackline Safety’s products allow employers to monitor and communicate with lone workers and detect possible leakage of gas or other hazardous materials that may pose a threat to workers.

The accompanying software streams data from devices in the field and enables employers to manage emergencies.

Blackline Safety has a worldwide presence with customers in more than 75 countries, including Australia. Blackline Safety has an Australian subsidiary, Blackline Safety Australia Pty Ltd.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no competitive overlap between Francisco Partners (including its portfolio companies with operations in Australia) and Blackline Safety in the manufacturing and supply of worker safety monitoring equipment (such as gas detection devices and lone worker SOS devices) in Australia

b. there are alternative manufacturers and suppliers of worker safety monitoring equipment in Australia, including global and domestic suppliers.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

Certain affiliates of American Industrial Partners propose to acquire 100% of the outstanding shares of Avanos Medical, Inc. through a share sale.

American Industrial Partners is an operationally oriented middle-market private equity firm based in New York, U.S, with a focus on acquisition and improvement of industrial businesses.

Avanos Medical is a US-headquartered medical device business focused on clinically differentiated solutions in two segments: Specialty Nutrition Systems (including enteral feeding products) and Pain Management & Recovery.

Avanos Medical is a US-headquartered medical device business focused on clinically differentiated solutions in two segments: Specialty Nutrition Systems and Pain Management & Recovery.

Speciality nutrition systems includes a range of enteral feeding products and services designed to deliver nutrition into the patient’s gastrointestinal tract and support care throughout the entire patient journey.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between American Industrial Partners (including its connected entities) and Avanos Medical in the supply of specialty nutrition systems or pain management and recovery products

b. American Industrial Partners’ portfolio company ADDMAN, a provider of medical manufacturing solutions, has a small presence in Australia

c. there are alternative suppliers of speciality nutrition systems and pain management and recovery products in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

mergers.merger_description:
UP Education Australia Pty Limited, the Australian subsidiary of New Zealand Parent company UP Education HoldCo Limited (**UP Education**), proposes to acquire Australian Academy of Beauty Dermal & Laser Pty Ltd (**AABDL**) by entering into a share sale deed (the **Acquisition**).

AABDL is an Australian tertiary education provider providing beauty, laser and dermal training courses to approximately 487 students. It has four campuses in NSW (Bella Vista, North Strathfield, Rockdale and Sydney CBD).

UP Education is a tertiary education provider in Australia and New Zealand which provides higher education, vocational education and training courses and university partnerships. UP Education offers courses in various topic areas such as health, hospitality, community services, construction and creative arts.

UP Education operates a group of higher education and vocational education and training providers across Australia and New Zealand, including 10 registered providers in Australia. This includes 4 providers that have campuses in New South Wales (including online courses), with 3 providers located in Sydney. UP Education also delivers online qualifications nationally through several providers, including to students in NSW.

UP Education does not currently supply any beauty, laser or dermal training courses in Australia.

UP Education is owned and operated by funds managed and/or advised by Pacific Equity Partners (the **PEP Funds**). UP Education is the only tertiary education business owned by the PEP Funds.

The target, AABDL, is an Australian tertiary education provider providing beauty, laser and dermal training courses to approximately 487 students. It has four campuses in NSW (Bella Vista, North Strathfield, Rockdale and Sydney CBD).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

b. whilst there is a horizontal overlap between UP Education and AABDL in the supply of tertiary education services in NSW and nationally, the increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be very small

mergers.merger_description:
NEC Corporation (**NEC**) proposes to acquire the entire issued and outstanding share capital of CSG Systems International, Inc. (**CSG**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will be implemented by way of a reverse triangular merger pursuant to an Agreement and Plan of Merger between NEC, CSG and Canvas Transaction Company, Inc. This will involve CSG merging with NEC’s wholly-owned Delaware subsidiary (Canvas Transaction Company, Inc.) and then surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC.

NEC is a Japanese conglomerate listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. NEC is mainly active in IT services, systems integration, and telecommunications infrastructure. Netcracker Technology Corporation (**Netcracker**) is a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC, headquartered in the United States, which provides cloud-based business support systems which support customer-facing operations and operations support systems which support the technical and operational aspects of telecommunications networks.

CSG is headquartered in the United States and listed on the Nasdaq Stock Exchange. CSG is a Software-as-a-Service company that provides customer engagement and billing software to companies in various industries, including telecommunications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: NEC Corporation (NEC) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued and outstanding share capital in CSG Systems International, Inc. (CSG), by way of a reverse triangular merger involving NEC, its wholly owned subsidiary Canvas Transaction Company, Inc. (Canvas) and CSG, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). This will involve CSG merging with Canvas and then surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
24 February 2026

The acquirer, NEC, is a Japanese conglomerate listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. NEC is active in IT services, systems integration, and telecommunications infrastructure. Netcracker Technology Corporation (Netcracker) is a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC, headquartered in the United States, which provides cloud-based business support systems (BSS) and operations support systems (OSS) globally.

The target, CSG, is headquartered in the United States and listed on the Nasdaq Stock Exchange. CSG is a Software-as-a-Service company that provides customer engagement and billing software to companies in various industries, including telecommunications.

The application states that in Australia, NEC (through Netcracker) supplies OSS and not BSS, whereas CSG supplies BSS in Australia and not OSS. BSS and OSS are software applications that are used to support telecommunications networks. BSS supports customer-facing operations, such as billing, customer engagement and support, customer relationship management, order fulfillment and product management. OSS supports the technical and operational aspects, such as network management, performance monitoring, service provisioning and service assurance.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There does not appear to be horizontal overlap between Netcracker’s and CSG’s software offerings in Australia.

b. While there is a potential vertical or conglomerate relationship between NEC and CSG, there are alternative suppliers of software for billing, customer engagement, managing and monitoring telecommunications network operations and/or associated applications for customers in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Crossmuller Pty. Limited (**Crossmuller**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of GEMCORP Holdings Pty Ltd (**GEM**).

Crossmuller designs, engineers and delivers manufacturing, electrical and industrial automation solutions for industrial clients. Crossmuller provides project delivery services, from concept and detailed engineering through to electrical installation, software and systems integration, fitting and machining commissioning, and ongoing support across a range of manufacturing sectors.

Crossmuller owns the registered businesses Main Control Systems and Main Control Switchboards (together, **MCS**), which manufactures low-medium amperage electrical switchboards (up to 1,200 amps) for use in residential and commercial projects.

Crossmuller is part of the Borg Group, an Australian-owned private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging, engineering and automation services.

GEM designs, engineers and manufactures custom low voltage and high amperage electronical switchboards (up to 4,000 amps), as well as motor control centres and control panels, which are built for industrial, commercial and infrastructure applications. GEM offers services encompassing design, assembly, factory testing, site installation, commissioning and maintenance.

determination_table_content.details: Crossmuller Pty Ltd (Crossmuller) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in GEMCORP Holdings Pty Ltd (GEM), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
5 March 2026

The acquirer, Crossmuller, designs, engineers and delivers manufacturing, electrical and industrial automation solutions for industrial customers. Crossmuller provides project delivery services, from concept and engineering through to electrical installation, software and systems integration, fitting and machining commissioning, and ongoing support across a range of manufacturing sectors.

Crossmuller owns the registered businesses Main Control Systems and Main Control Switchboards (together, MCS), which manufactures electrical switchboards for use in residential and commercial projects.

Crossmuller is part of the Borg Group, an Australian-owned private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging, engineering and automation services.

The target, GEM, designs, engineers and manufactures custom low voltage and high amperage electronical switchboards, as well as motor control centres and control panels, which are built for industrial, commercial and infrastructure applications. GEM offers services including design, assembly, factory testing, site installation, commissioning and maintenance.

Crossmuller and GEM overlap in the supply of electrical switchboards. The application states that MCS’ switchboards are certified up to 1,200 amps (low-medium amperage), GEM’s switchboards are certified up to 4,000 amps (high amperage) and GEM does not currently offer any switchboards in the low-medium amperage range.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Crossmuller and GEM in the supply of electrical switchboards resulting in a low combined share of supply

b. there are several alternative suppliers of electrical switchboards in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

The Acquisition involves a purchase of stapled units in Southern Way Holding Trust and shares in Southern Way Holdings Pty Limited.

Prime Super is a multi-industry fully independent profit-to-members superannuation fund with approximately $8.4 billion in funds under management and 140,000 members.

The target, Southern Way Holdings, is responsible for the delivery and maintenance of the Peninsula Link Project. The Peninsula Link Project is a Public Private Partnership to design, construct, maintain and operate the 27km Peninsula Link Freeway between Frankston and Mount Martha in Victoria, Australia. Southern Way Holdings is responsible for the maintenance and availability of Peninsula Link.

determination_table_content.details:
Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit Trust, which is a wholly owned entity of Prime Super Pty Ltd as trustee for Prime Super (together, Prime Super), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of an additional 8.57% of stapled units and shares in Southern Way Holdings Pty Ltd and Southern Way Holdings Trust (together, Southern Way Holdings), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

As a result of the Acquisition, Prime Super will hold a 27.36% interest in Southern Way Holdings.
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
20 April 2026

The acquirer, Prime Super, is a multi-industry fully independent profit-to-members superannuation fund with approximately $8.4 billion in funds under management and 140,000 members. Prime Super’s investment portfolio comprises a number of different assets including infrastructure investments which represent approximately 16% of its total investment holdings. Those infrastructure investments include investments in international education infrastructure funds, energy infrastructure projects, an Australian based parking group, and an existing 18.79% interest in Southern Way Holdings. Prime Super does not hold interests in any infrastructure assets or projects similar to Peninsula Link, nor does it hold investments in any other entities which provide large scale road infrastructure services to an Australian state government.

The target, Southern Way Holdings, is a special purpose vehicle which is responsible for the Peninsula Link Project. The Peninsula Link Project is a Public Private Partnership to design, construct, maintain and operate the 27km Peninsula Link Freeway between Frankston and Mount Martha in Victoria, Australia. The Peninsula Link Project has a 25-year operation period after which ownership of the road will be handed back to the State of Victoria (expected in 2038).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

• Based on the information currently before it, including publicly available information regarding the Peninsula Link Project, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between Prime Super’s other investment holdings and Southern Way Holdings in the supply of large-scale road infrastructure services

b. the increment to Prime Super’s existing interest in Southern Way Holdings is small

c. Southern Way Holding’s obligations with respect to the Peninsula Link Project are governed, and will continue to be governed post-acquisition, by long-term contracts with the Victorian state government.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Buroserv Telco Holdings Pty Ltd (**BTH**) is a special purpose vehicle owned and incorporated by Salter Brothers Private Equity Pty Ltd which is in turn owned by Salter Brothers Funds Management Pty Ltd (**Salter Brothers**).

Salter Brothers is a professional fund management firm with a focus on specialist property, equities and credit investments. Salter Brothers is the fund manager of several property focused funds which own over 40 hotels and resorts around Australia, the US and Asia.

LMGPS Pty Ltd (**LMGPS**) provides telecommunication services to wholesale and retail customers in Australia, focussed on small-to-medium enterprises. LMGPS owns 100% of Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd, one of several operating entities owned by LMGPS (including Totalcom Corp Pty Ltd, Waterfront Networks Pty Ltd, Buroserv Networks Pty Ltd, V4 Communications Pty Ltd, Loaded Net Australia Pty Ltd, and Hurricane Compute Pty Ltd).

Salter Brothers, through BTH, is proposing to acquire select assets and liabilities of LMGPS Pty Ltd, including the above-named subsidiaries.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Buroserv Telco Holdings Pty Ltd, a special purpose vehicle owned by Salter Brothers Funds Management Pty Ltd (Salter Brothers), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of select assets and liabilities of LMGPS Pty Ltd (including 100% of LMGPS Pty Ltd’s shareholding in Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd and other subsidiaries listed below), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
29 April 2026

Salter Brothers is a professional fund management firm with a focus on specialist property, equities and credit investments. Salter Brothers is the fund manager of several property focused funds which own over 40 hotels and resorts around Australia, the US and Asia. Salter Brothers has an interest in Skymesh. Skymesh provides services to approximately 49,000 active fixed line services nationally.

LMGPS Pty Ltd provides telecommunication services to wholesale and retail customers in Australia, focussed on small-to-medium enterprises. LMGPS and its subsidiaries (Totalcom Corp Pty Ltd, Waterfront Networks Pty Ltd, Buroserv Networks Pty Ltd, V4 Communications Pty Ltd, Loaded Net Australia Pty Ltd, Hurricane Compute Pty Ltd and Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd) operate under the brand name Buroserv. Buroserv provides services to approximately 37,000 NBN active fixed line services and 8,200 mobile services nationally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Salter Brothers and Buroserv in the supply of NBN and telecommunications services in Australia

b. there are alternative providers of NBN and telecommunication services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
DGH Design and Fabrication Services Pty Ltd (**DGH**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Cement Australia Holdings Pty Limited (**Cement Australia**), proposes to acquire 100% of the Gladstone business assets of Aylward Dowling And Wall Pty Ltd, trading as Global Manufacturing Group (**GMG**).

DGH is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing metal engineering, drafting, fabrication, fitting, machining, blast and painting services, with branches in Mackay, Emerald, and Townsville.

GMG is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing steel fabrication and coating services for all types of metal across the industrial manufacturing industry, with workshops in Maryborough and Gladstone.

The Acquisition only concerns GMG’s workshop in Gladstone. DGH does not have a branch in the Gladstone region but does supply some of Cement Australia’s Gladstone requirements from its Mackay branch.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: DGH Design and Fabrication Services Pty Ltd (DGH), a wholly owned subsidiary of Cement Australia Holdings Pty Limited (Cement Australia), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the Gladstone business assets of Aylward Dowling And Wall Pty Ltd, trading as Global Manufacturing Group (GMG), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, DGH, is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing metal engineering, drafting, fabrication, fitting, machining, blast, and painting services, with branches in Mackay, Emerald, and Townsville. It does not have a branch in the Gladstone region but supplies Cement Australia in Gladstone from its Mackay branch.

The target, GMG, is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing steel fabrication and coating services for all types of metal across the industrial manufacturing industry, with workshops in Maryborough and Gladstone. The Acquisition relates only to GMG’s workshop in Gladstone.

The parties both supply metal fabrication and engineering services, including coating services, in the Gladstone region.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is small horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of metal fabrication and engineering services in the Gladstone region, and their combined share of supply is small

b. while there is a vertical relationship between the parties and DGH’s parent company, Cement Australia, there appears to be low risk of vertical foreclosure arising from the Acquisition due to the presence of alternative suppliers and customers of metal fabrication and engineering services, including in Gladstone.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Sail Parent Holdco Ltd, an acquisition vehicle ultimately wholly owned by Constellation Software Inc. (**CSI**), a publicly listed company on the Toronto Stock Exchange, proposes to acquire a majority shareholding of around 90% of the issued equity interests in DerbySoft Holdings Limited (**DerbySoft**), with DerbySoft’s management retaining the remaining shareholding.

Sail Parent Holdco Ltd is part of the Vela Operating Group, a group of operating companies owned by Vela Software International Inc., which in turn is a wholly owned subsidiary of CSI. The CSI group of companies (**CSI Group**), including Vela Operating Group, acquire, manage and build industry-specific software businesses globally in a variety of public and private sector industries. CSI Group includes two operating groups which, among other things, have teams focused on providing software to the global travel and hospitality industries: the Juniper Group, which is part of the Vela Operating Group, and the Jonas Group, another operating group of CSI.

DerbySoft and its subsidiaries are a business-to-business travel technology group that provides software and connectivity platform services facilitating digital commerce across the global travel ecosystem. It acts as a travel commerce accelerator, combining connectivity, marketing technology and end-to-end content distribution into a single ecosystem.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Sail Parent Holdco Ltd (Parent), an acquisition vehicle ultimately wholly owned by Constellation Software Inc. (CSI), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a majority shareholding of around 90% of the issued equity interests in DerbySoft Holdings Limited (DerbySoft), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Parent, is part of the Vela Operating Group, a group of operating companies owned by Vela Software International Inc., which in turn is a wholly owned subsidiary of CSI. The CSI group of companies (CSI Group), including Vela Operating Group, acquire, manage, and build industry-specific software businesses globally in a variety of public and private sector industries.

CSI Group includes two operating groups which, among other things, have teams focused on providing software to the global travel and hospitality industries: the Juniper Group and the Jonas Group. The services provided by these two groups include flight distribution services, booking engine services, and digital marketing and hospitality technology services.

The target, DerbySoft, is a B2B commerce platform operating in the global travel industry. This industry involves platforms like DerbySoft providing connectivity, digital marketing, and B2B marketplace services to hotels, airlines, and other travel suppliers (e.g. tour operators), connecting them with distributors and other channels, such as online travel agencies, travel management companies, metasearch platforms, and wholesalers across the globe.

The parties both supply digital software solutions and services for the global travel and hospitality industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between any of the operations of CSI Group’s portfolio companies and DerbySoft in Australia

b. DerbySoft has a small Australian presence.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Türk Hava Yolları Anonim Ortaklığı (together with all its controlled entities, **Turkish Airlines**) is proposing to acquire a 26–27% controlling stake in Air Europa Holding, S.L. and indirectly its wholly owned subsidiaries Air Europa Líneas Aéreas, S.A.U.; Air Europa Suma Miles, S.L.U.; Aeronova, S.L.U.; León Activos Aeronáuticos, S.L.U.; and Globalia Activos Inmobiliarios, S.A.U. (together, **Air Europa**).

Turkish Airlines operates in the aviation sector, globally. It provides passenger and cargo air transportation, including international flights into and out of over 130 countries. Turkish Airlines also conducts other related activities including maintenance and repair operations, courier services, manufacturing of aircraft interiors, and ground services. These services are provided in Türkiye. In Australia, Turkish Airlines provides international passenger air transport into, and out of, Melbourne and Sydney, either directly or by way of cooperation agreements; and cargo air transportation using the belly-hold capacity of certain passenger flights.

Air Europa is a Spanish aviation group that primarily operates international and domestic scheduled air services for the carriage of passengers and cargo. Air Europa does not operate any flights to or from Australia. Its activities in Australia are largely confined to the sale of passenger tickets for routes that are wholly operated outside of Australia, with some sales for flights that include a segment operated by a third-party airline pursuant to a codesharing/interlining agreement for passenger transportation services, and that may have Australia as either the origin or the destination.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Türk Hava Yolları Anonim Ortaklığı (together with its controlled entities, Turkish Airlines) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 26–27% controlling stake in Air Europa Holding, S.L. (together with its subsidiaries, Air Europa), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The acquirer, Turkish Airlines, operates in the aviation sector globally. It provides passenger and cargo air transportation, including international flights into and out of over 130 countries.

Turkish Airlines also conducts other related activities including maintenance and repair operations, courier services, manufacturing of aircraft interiors, and ground services. These services are provided in Türkiye.

In Australia, Turkish Airlines provides international passenger air transport services into, and out of, Melbourne and Sydney (either directly or by way of cooperation agreements) and cargo air transportation using the belly-hold capacity of certain passenger flights.

The target, Air Europa, is a Spanish aviation group that primarily operates international and domestic scheduled air services for the carriage of passengers and cargo. Air Europa is a wholly-owned and controlled subsidiary of Globalia Corporación Empresarial, S.A. (Globalia). Globalia is a Spanish tourism group which does not have any corporate presence or assets in Australia.

Air Europa does not operate any flights to or from Australia. Its activities in Australia are largely confined to the sale of passenger tickets for routes that are wholly operated outside of Australia (e.g.

Madrid–Paris), with some sales for international flights that include a segment to/from Australia where that segment is operated by a third-party airline pursuant to a codesharing/interlining agreement for air passenger transport services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no direct horizontal overlap between Turkish Airlines and Air Europa in the supply of international air passenger transport services to and from Australia

b. Air Europa has a small, indirect presence in the supply of international air passenger transport services to and from Australia via codesharing/interlining agreements with a third-party airline, however it does not operate the Australian segment of any international flights

c. there are several alternative suppliers of international air passenger transport services to and from Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
HCL Technologies Limited (**HCLTech**) proposes to acquire Jaspersoft, a business unit of Cloud Software Group that supplies a reporting software platform (including JasperReports®).

The acquirer, HCLTech, is a global technology company listed on the National Stock Exchange of India. HCLTech provides a broad range of services and software products with capabilities centred around AI, digital, engineering, cloud and software to a range of industries. These solutions include supporting organisations with AI infrastructure, product engineering services, digital consultancy services, and specialized industry software. Relevantly, HCLTech provides software that includes data management, integration, governance and analytics readiness.

The target, Jaspersoft, provides reporting solutions software (including JasperReports) used by organisations to develop pixel-perfect reports, dashboards and visualisations. Jaspersoft licenses its reporting software to organisations to embed within their applications and to independent software vendors for inclusion within their applications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published

determination_table_content.details: HCL Technologies Limited (HCLTech) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of Jaspersoft, a business unit of Cloud Software Group, Inc., as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
18 February 2026

The acquirer, HCLTech is a global technology company listed on the National Stock Exchange of India. HCLTech provides a broad range of services and software products with capabilities centred around AI, digital, engineering, cloud and software to a range of industries.

These solutions include supporting organisations with AI infrastructure, product engineering services, digital consultancy services, and specialized industry software. Relevantly, HCLTech provides software that includes data management, integration, governance and analytics readiness.

The target, Jaspersoft, provides reporting solutions software (including JasperReports) used by organisations to develop pixel-perfect reports, dashboards and visualisations. Jaspersoft licenses its reporting software to organisations to embed within their applications and to independent software vendors for inclusion within their applications.

While the parties both supply enterprise software in Australia, HCLTech does not supply reporting software solutions.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. There is no horizontal overlap in the supply of reporting software in Australia and a small horizontal overlap between HCLTech and Jaspersoft in the supply of enterprise software in Australia.

b. Jaspersoft has a small presence in Australia.

c. The presence of several competitors in the supply of enterprise and reporting software.

d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from bundling or tying of products post-Acquisition.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Spike Media Pty Ltd, a newly-formed entity under the control of Arthur Laundy, proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Nine Radio Pty Limited (**Nine Radio**) from Fairfax Media Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Nine Entertainment Co. Holdings Limited.

Arthur Laundy currently controls the Laundy Group, which operates over 40 hospitality venues throughout New South Wales through Laundy Hotels Pty Ltd and invests in Australian assets, including in commercial property, sports, tourism, hospitality, events and accommodation.

Nine Radio is an Australian broadcaster engaged in commercial radio broadcasting through its radio stations in Sydney (2GB and 2UE), Melbourne (3AW and Magic 1278), Brisbane (4BC and 4BH), and Perth (6PR). Nine Radio also offers these radio stations through linear radio broadcast live streaming distributed via Nine Radio websites and apps, in addition to on-demand catch-up podcasts and other original podcasts.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: Spike Media Pty Ltd (Spike Media), an entity under the control of Arthur Laundy, applied for a notification waiver in respect of Spike Media’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Nine Radio Pty Ltd (Nine Radio) (ACN 063 906 927), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
20 February 2026

Arthur Laundy currently controls the Laundy Group, which operates over 40 hospitality venues throughout New South Wales through Laundy Hotels Pty Ltd (ACN 001 217 554) and invests in Australian assets, including in commercial property, sports, tourism, hospitality, events and accommodation. Spike Media is a newly-formed entity for the purpose of this acquisition.

The target, Nine Radio, is an Australian broadcaster engaged in commercial radio broadcasting through its radio stations in Sydney (2GB and 2UE), Melbourne (3AW and Magic 1278), Brisbane (4BC and 4BH), and Perth (6PR). Nine Radio also offers these radio stations through linear radio broadcast live streaming distributed via Nine Radio websites and apps, in addition to on-demand catch-up podcasts and other original podcasts. Nine Radio is currently owned by Fairfax Media Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Nine Entertainment Co. Holdings Limited.

Laundy Group and its connected entities do not currently have any assets or investments in radio or interests in any other broadcasting, media or communications business.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In particular:

a. there is no horizontal overlap between the Laundy Group (including its connected entities) and Nine Radio in the supply of news and other media content to consumers or listeners.

b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition, due to the presence of alternative suppliers of advertising opportunities, including other radio stations and streaming platforms.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery from Cicuration Pty Ltd as trustee for Burke Family Trust.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery is a single full-service veterinary practice in the Toowoomba area of Queensland. The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details: VC Operations (WA) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, Vets Central), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the business assets of West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
10 March 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The target, West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery, is a single full-service veterinary practice in the Toowoomba area of Queensland.

The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

Vets Central and West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery supply first opinion veterinary services to household pets, livestock, and exotic species in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered the information provided with the notification waiver application and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC considers that:

a. there is no geographic horizontal overlap between Vets Central and West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery in the supply of veterinary services in Toowoomba.

b. the presence of alternative suppliers of veterinary services in Toowoomba and more broadly at the state and national level.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.

The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting competition.

For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
